Changing the width and length of this opening creates different interference structures in the results. This model was tested using helium atoms and matches what scientists have seen in real experiments.

original_text: Pamela may refer to: Pamela; or, Virtue Rewarded, a novel written by Samuel Richardson in 1740 Pamela (name), a given name and, rarely, a surname Pamela Spence, a Turkish pop-rock singer. Known as her stage name "Pamela" MSC Pamela, a container ship launched in 2005 Pamela (butterfly), a butterfly genus Perrhybris pamela, a butterfly with the common name Pamela Pamela hat, a straw hat named after Richardson's heroine, worn 1790s–1870s Pamela (film), a 1945 French film Super Typhoon Pamela, a typhoon in 1976 Una donna da guardare, a 1990 Italian erotic movie P.A.M.E.L.A., a first-person survival video game Songs "Pamela Pamela", a song recorded by Wayne Fontana that reached number 11 in the UK Singles Chart in 1967 "Pamela" (song), a 1988 hit song for the band Toto "Pamella", a song by Remmy Ongala from the album Songs For the Poor Man "Pamela Wan", a song composed by Vhong Navarro in 2004, inspired by the movie Otso-Otso Pamela-Mela-Wan Acronyms PAMELA Project, Process for Advanced Management of End of-Life-Aircraft Payload for Antimatter Matter Exploration and Light-nuclei Astrophysics, astrophysical satellite module Pedestrian Accessibility and Movement Environment Laboratory, artificial pavement at a research center at University College London See also Pam (disambiguation)
simplified_kindergarten: Pamela is a girl's name. It is a name for many things.
simplified_primary_school: Pamela is a name for many people and things. It is a book, a song, and a butterfly. It is even the name of a big ship.
simplified_secondary_school: Pamela is a common name used for people, famous books, and popular songs. It can refer to a novel from 1740 or a Turkish singer. The name is also used for a type of straw hat and a large container ship. Scientists even use the name for a satellite and a weather typhoon.

original_text: are a style of baggy sock worn by Japanese high school girls, as part of culture. This style of socks has also become popular among American teens and college students who are fans of Japanese anime and manga. These socks come in a variety of styles, defined by the knitting pattern of the upper portion of the sock. The two most popular styles are the traditional 2×2 rib knit (pictured) and tube-style loose socks, which are thigh-high length tube socks worn pushed down around the ankles. A skin-safe body adhesive popularly known as "sock glue" can be used to affix the uppermost part of the sock to the calf so that the entire sock will not bunch downward and spoil the look. Loose socks were adopted as a fashion which flattered plump calves and also expressed rebellious deviation from Japan's strict dress code for school uniforms. They have been used as an inspiration for photography by Akira Gomi. They are also used in Japanese fashions like and Fairy Kei. See also References Socks Japanese fashion Japanese clothing Japanese culture 1990s fashion
simplified_kindergarten: Big socks look like soft clouds. Girls wear them for fun.
simplified_primary_school: Loose socks are big and baggy. Japanese school girls like to wear them. Some girls use special glue to keep them up. They wear them to look stylish and different.
simplified_secondary_school: Loose socks are a baggy style popular among Japanese students and fans of anime. They come in different knit patterns and are often worn pushed down. Some people use skin-safe glue to keep the socks from falling too far. This fashion started as a way to rebel against strict school dress codes. Today, these socks are still used in many different Japanese street fashion styles.

original_text: the role of electronic interactions in the level structure of semiconductor quantum dots is analyzed in terms of the correspondence to the integrability of a classical system that models these structures . we find that an otherwise simple system is made strongly non - integrable in the classical regime by the introduction of particle interactions . in particular we present a two - particle classical system contained in a @xmath0-dimensional billiard with hard walls . similarly , a corresponding two - dimensional quantum dot problem with three particles is shown to have interesting spectral properties as function of the interaction strength and applied magnetic fields . + keywords : quantum dots , chaos , level statistics + sergio e. ulloa + e - mail : sulloa1@ohiou.edu + fax : ( 614 ) 5930433
simplified_kindergarten: Tiny bits push each other. They move like bouncing balls.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists study how tiny parts inside computers push one another. These parts act like balls bouncing in a box. When they touch, their movement changes and becomes messy. This helps us understand how small electronics work.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers are studying how particles interact inside tiny structures called quantum dots. By using a model of balls bouncing in a box, they found that these interactions make the system more chaotic. They also looked at how magnetic fields change the way these particles behave. This study helps explain the complex energy levels found in modern semiconductors.

original_text: In Greek mythology, Electryone (Ancient Greek: Ἠλεκτρυώνην) or Alectrona (Doric form) was a daughter of Helios and Rhodos, and sister to the Heliadae. She died a virgin and was worshipped as a heroine on the island of Rhodes. She was possibly a goddess of the sunrise, or of man's waking sense. The Doric form of her name is akin to the Greek word for "rooster" (Alectrona, the feminine genitive of Αλεκτορ, Alektor, the ancient Greek word for "rooster"), while the Attic form Electryone is akin to the word for "amber" (Ἠλέκτρα, Elektra), as in the amber color of sunrise. A marble tablet from the 3rd century BC found in Ialyssus contains an inscription about the regulations for visitors to the temple of Alectrona. Genealogy Notes References Graves, Robert; The Greek Myths, Penguin Books Ltd. (1960 edition). 42. c, 4. Smith, William; Dictionary of Greek and Roman Biography and Mythology, London (1873). "Electryo'ne" Numismatic Chronicle, Volume 18 Greek goddesses Greek Virgin goddesses Greek mythological heroes Children of Helios Demigods in classical mythology Women in Greek mythology Rhodian characters in Greek mythology Characters in Greek mythology
simplified_kindergarten: She is a sun girl. She wakes up the world.
simplified_primary_school: Electryone was a girl in old Greek stories. Her father was the sun god Helios. People on the island of Rhodes loved her. She might be the goddess of the sunrise.
simplified_secondary_school: In Greek mythology, Electryone was the daughter of the sun god Helios and Rhodos. She lived on the island of Rhodes and was honored as a heroine. Some people think she was a goddess of the sunrise or waking up. Her name is related to the words for rooster and amber. An old stone tablet explains the rules for visiting her temple.

original_text: Phaser may refer to: Phaser (microwave weapon) Phaser (fictional weapon), in the Star Trek fictional universe Phaser (effect), in electronics, an audio signal processor used to distort the signal Light Phaser, a lightgun for the Sega Master System video game system Xerox Phaser, Xerox's trade name for makes of thermal transfer printers, solid ink printers and color laser printers Personnel Halting and Stimulation Response rifle (PHASR), a dazzler weapon Phaser, an insecticide containing the active ingredient endosulfan Phaser Digital Power Transducer from Second Wind Phaser (band), a Washington, D.C. rock band Phaser (game framework), a free software 2D game framework for making HTML5 games See also Phase (disambiguation) Phasor (disambiguation) FASOR (disambiguation)
simplified_kindergarten: A phaser is a space toy. It is also a loud tool.
simplified_primary_school: The word phaser has many different meanings. It is a famous space gun in movies. It is also a tool for music and printing.
simplified_secondary_school: A phaser can refer to several different things in technology and media. It is most famous as a fictional weapon in the Star Trek universe. In music, a phaser is an electronic tool used to change sounds. It is also the name of a video game lightgun and a brand of office printers. Additionally, a phaser can be a type of weapon or a software tool for making games.

original_text: (CNN) -- A day after North Korea test-fired two missiles, South Korea captured a fishing boat from the North that had crossed into South Korean waters, officials say. The boat crossed the sea demarcation line that separates the two Koreas and was captured by the South Korean navy Thursday, the South Korean Ministry of Defense said. The action comes as tensions between the two Koreas are rising once again. On Wednesday, North Korea tested two medium-range ballistic missiles, firing them into the ocean. South Korea says it believes the missile tests are a reaction to ongoing U.S.-South Korean military drills. North Korea had harsh words for the South, accusing it of provocations. "The puppet military gangsters perpetrated such provocations as firing bullets and shells from the areas around the five islands including Paekryong and Big Yonphyong islands and scattering leaflets hurting the dignity of the supreme leadership of the DPRK on March 24," the North Korean news agency KCNA reported. North Korea ups stakes with latest missile launch . Report: North Korea fires two missiles off eastern coast .
simplified_kindergarten: Two ships are in the water. The men are not friends.
simplified_primary_school: North Korea fired two missiles into the ocean on Wednesday. One day later, South Korea took a North Korean fishing boat. The boat was in the wrong part of the sea. Both countries are very angry with each other right now.
simplified_secondary_school: South Korea captured a North Korean fishing boat after it crossed the sea border on Thursday. This event happened just one day after North Korea tested two medium-range missiles. Tensions are rising because South Korea and the United States are currently holding military drills. North Korea is angry and has accused the South of acting like gangsters.

original_text: nighttime sky transparency statistics on mauna kea are reported based on data from the canada - france - hawaii telescope skyprobe monitor . we focus on the period beginning with the start of megacam wide - field optical imager operations in 2003 , and continuing for almost three years . skies were clear enough to observe on 76% of those nights ; attenuations were less than 0.2 magnitudes up to 60% of the time . an empirical model of cloud attenuation and duration is presented allowing us to further characterize the photometric conditions . this is a good fit to the skyprobe data , and indicates that mauna kea skies are truly photometric ( without cloud ) an average of 56% of the time , with moderate seasonal variation . continuous monitoring of transparency during the night is necessary to overcome fluctuations in attenuation due to thin cloud .
simplified_kindergarten: Look at the stars. Many nights are very clear.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists studied the night sky from a big mountain in Hawaii. They found that the sky is clear most of the time. This helps them see stars better. They use a special camera to watch for clouds.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers analyzed three years of data from a telescope on Mauna Kea to study sky clarity. The results showed that the skies were clear enough for observations on 76% of nights. For about 56% of the time, the sky was completely free of clouds. Scientists created a model to better understand how clouds block starlight. They suggest constant monitoring is necessary because thin clouds can change quickly during the night.

original_text: ASPS may refer to: Advanced sleep phase syndrome, a sleep disorder in which patients feel very sleepy early in the evening American Society of Plastic Surgeons, the largest plastic surgery specialty organization in the world Alveolar soft part sarcoma, a rare type of soft tissue sarcoma tumor Association of Scottish Police Superintendents Albino Squirrel Preservation Society, an organization dedicated to the preservation of albino squirrels See also Asp (disambiguation), for the singular of Asps ASP (disambiguation), for the singular of ASPs
simplified_kindergarten: ASPS can mean many things. It can even help white squirrels.
simplified_primary_school: ASPS is a name used by many different groups. It can mean a sleep problem or a doctor group. It also helps protect rare white squirrels.
simplified_secondary_school: ASPS is an abbreviation that refers to several different things. It can describe a sleep disorder or a group for plastic surgeons. It also names a rare cancer and a society that protects white squirrels.

original_text: computer simulations have been employed in recent years to evaluate the configurational entropy changes in model glass - forming liquids . we consider two methods , both of which involve the calculation of the ` intra - basin ' entropy as a means for obtaining the configurational entropy . the first method involves the evaluation of the intra - basin entropy from the vibrational frequencies of inherent structures , by making a harmonic approximation of the local potential energy topography . the second method employs simulations that confine the liquid within a localized region of configuration space by the imposition of constraints ; apart from the choice of the constraints , no further assumptions are made . we compare the configurational entropies estimated for a model liquid ( binary mixture of particles interacting _ via _ the lennard - jones potential ) for a range of temperatures , at fixed density .
simplified_kindergarten: Computers help us study tiny things. We watch how they move.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists use computers to study how liquids turn into glass. They use two ways to measure how particles move inside the liquid. They tested these ways on a mix of tiny particles. This helps them see how things change when they get cold.
simplified_secondary_school: Scientists recently used computer simulations to study how particles are arranged in liquids that form glass. They compared two different methods to calculate the entropy, which measures the disorder within the liquid. One method uses vibrations to estimate energy, while the second method limits the particles to a small space. They tested these methods on a mixture of particles across different temperatures. This research helps us understand how the structure of a liquid changes as it cools down.

original_text: Baco noir (pronounced BA-koh NWAHR) is a hybrid red wine grape variety produced by Francois Baco from a cross of Vitis vinifera var. Folle blanche, a French wine grape, and an unknown variety of Vitis riparia indigenous to North America. Regions In 1951 the variety was brought to the cooler viticulture regions of North America, such as British Columbia, Ontario, Nova Scotia, New York, Michigan, Mississippi, Wisconsin, Pennsylvania and Oregon. In 1955 the variety was brought back to Canada, where the "George" clonal variety is commonly used. Baco noir was the target of a vine-pull program in Canada in the early 1980s, which means that there are few older plots of this variety left in Canada. Oregon's first Baco Noir vines were imported by Philippe Girardet in 1971 for his winery located in the Umpqua Valley. This variety is also grown in certain parts of Colorado as vineyard area expands beyond the traditional AVA's of Colorado and across the Front Range. See also Baco 22A (Baco blanc) References 2. BC Baco Noir wineries Red wine grape varieties Hybrid grape varieties
simplified_kindergarten: This is a red grape. It makes a yummy drink.
simplified_primary_school: Baco noir is a special red grape for making wine. It grows well in cold places like Canada and New York. Many old plants were removed long ago. Now, farmers grow it in many new places.
simplified_secondary_school: Baco noir is a hybrid red wine grape created by crossing a French variety with a North American variety. It was introduced to cool regions like Ontario and New York in the 1950s. Although many old vines were removed in Canada during the 1980s, the grape remains popular in several states. Today, it is grown in places like Oregon, Michigan, and Colorado as vineyards continue to expand.

original_text: The Battle of Burgos, also known as Battle of Gamonal, was fought on November 10, 1808, during the Peninsular War in the village of Gamonal, near Burgos, Spain. A powerful French army under Marshal Bessières overwhelmed and destroyed the outnumbered Spanish troops under General Belveder, opening central Spain to invasion. Battle The French were attacking the Spanish positions in Burgos with a ratio 2:1. Only two divisions of the Army of Extremadura were in Burgos and the so the result of the battle was clear. Arrayed in an open plain at Gamonal, the Spaniards were broken by a massive cavalry charge and fled in panic, leaving the invaders to sack Burgos. Notes References Battle of Burgos Battles of the Peninsular War Battles in Castile and León Battles involving Spain Battle of Burgos November 1808 events History of the province of Burgos Burgos Battles inscribed on the Arc de Triomphe
simplified_kindergarten: The big army won the fight. Many men ran away.
simplified_primary_school: French soldiers fought Spanish soldiers in a village long ago. The French army was much bigger and stronger. They won the battle because they had many horses. The Spanish soldiers had to run away quickly.
simplified_secondary_school: The Battle of Burgos took place in Spain on November 10, 1808. A powerful French army led by Marshal Bessières attacked a smaller Spanish force. Because the French had twice as many soldiers, they easily defeated the Spanish troops. A large cavalry charge broke the Spanish lines and forced them to flee. This victory allowed the French army to invade the center of Spain. After the battle, the invaders entered the city of Burgos to take supplies.

original_text: an interaction between dark matter and dark energy , proportional to the product of their energy densities , results in a scaling behavior of the ratio of these densities with respect to the scale factor of the robertson - walker metric . this gives rise to a class of cosmological models which deviate from the standard model in an analytically tractable way . in particular , it becomes possible to quantify the role of potential dark - energy perturbations . we investigate the impact of this interaction on the structure formation process . using the ( modified ) camb code we obtain the cmb spectrum as well as the linear matter power spectrum . it is shown that the strong degeneracy in the parameter space present in the background analysis is considerably reduced by considering _ planck _ data . our analysis is compatible with the @xmath0cdm model at the @xmath1 confidence level with a slightly preferred direction of the energy flow from dark matter to dark energy .
simplified_kindergarten: Space has two invisible things. They pull and push each other.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists study how invisible space energy and dark matter work together. This changes how stars and galaxies grow over time. They used a computer to test these big ideas. Their new map shows how space moves and changes.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers studied the interaction between dark matter and dark energy to see how it affects the universe. This interaction changes how we understand the growth of cosmic structures. By using special computer code, they created maps of light and matter from the early universe. Their results were compared with data from the Planck satellite to improve accuracy. The study suggests that energy might flow from dark matter to dark energy. This new model is similar to standard theories but provides more detail about space.

original_text: voltage - driven 180@xmath0 magnetization switching without electric current provides the possibility for revolutionizing the spintronics . we demonstrated the voltage - driven charge - mediated 180@xmath0 magnetization switching at room temperature by combining first - principles calculations and temperature - dependent magnetization dynamics simulation . the electric field ( @xmath1 ) induced interface charge is found to allow a giant modulation of the magnetic anisotropy ( @xmath2 ) of the nanomagnet . particularly @xmath2 is revealed to vary linearly with respect to @xmath1 and the epitaxial strain . magnetization dynamics simulations using the so - obtained @xmath2 show that both in - plane and perpendicular 180@xmath0 switching can be achieved by @xmath1 pulses . the temperature effect renders the 180@xmath0 switching as probability events . statistical analysis indicates a fast ( around 2 ns ) and deterministic 180@xmath0 switching achievable at room temperature by controlling the magnitude of e and the pulse width . the study inspires the rational design of miniaturized nanoscale spintronic devices where finite temperature effect has a great impact .
simplified_kindergarten: We use power to move magnets. It makes tiny tools work well.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists found a way to flip magnets using electricity. This works at room temperature and is very fast. It helps us make better and smaller computers. Using electricity instead of heat saves a lot of energy.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers have demonstrated a way to flip a magnet's direction by using an electric field instead of a current. This process happens at room temperature and is controlled by changing the magnetic anisotropy. Computer simulations show that this switching is very fast, taking only two nanoseconds. Even at normal temperatures, the switching is reliable if the electric pulse is timed correctly. This discovery will help engineers design much smaller and more efficient electronic devices.

original_text: (CNN) -- Former Detroit mayor Kwame Kilpatrick is headed back behind bars, but only for a few days. The Michigan Department of Corrections charged him with 14 parole violations late Thursday and he'll spend the weekend in jail. Kilpatrick agreed to report to the Detroit Reentry Center late Friday and will be released Monday, according to Corrections spokesman Russell Marlan. Kilpatrick is currently on trial in a public corruption case in U.S. District Court. His release would allow him to be back in court next week. The 14 violations include submitting a false and inaccurate report to a field agent by failing to report money transfers and cash gifts, as well as failing to report several months of his income, according to an e-mail from Russell. Kilpatrick is on parole after a 2008 state conviction for obstruction of justice. By the numbers: Politicians in trouble .
simplified_kindergarten: A man must go to jail. He will stay for two days.
simplified_primary_school: Former mayor Kwame Kilpatrick is going to jail for the weekend. He broke the rules of his parole fourteen times. He will be out on Monday for his court trial. He did not tell the truth about his money.
simplified_secondary_school: Former Detroit mayor Kwame Kilpatrick will spend the weekend in jail after being charged with fourteen parole violations. He failed to report cash gifts and his monthly income to his parole officer. He must report to a detention center on Friday but will be released on Monday. This release allows him to attend his current public corruption trial in federal court. Kilpatrick has been on parole since 2008 following a conviction for obstruction of justice.

original_text: In telecommunication, the term conditioning equipment has the following meanings: At junctions of circuits, equipment used to obtain desired circuit characteristics, such as matched transmission levels, matched impedances, and equalization between facilities. Corrective networks used to improve data transmission, such as equalization of the insertion loss-vs.-frequency characteristic and the envelope delay distortion over a desired frequency range. References Communication circuits
simplified_kindergarten: Tools help machines talk. They make the sound clear.
simplified_primary_school: Conditioning equipment helps messages travel through wires. It fixes the sound so it is clear and loud. This makes sure the information arrives correctly.
simplified_secondary_school: Conditioning equipment is used in communication to improve how data travels through circuits. It adjusts signals at junctions to ensure different parts of the system work together smoothly. These tools also fix distortions by balancing sound levels and timing across various frequencies.

original_text: (CNN) -- A U.S. soldier who escaped Army custody while facing a rape charge in California has been returned to Los Angeles County, authorities said Monday. Pvt. Daniel Brazelton, 20, who is accused of raping a 15-year-old girl in Los Angeles, was captured at a motel in Daytona Beach, Florida, late Friday. He was returned to California the next day. Brazelton is being held on $325,000 bail and is scheduled to appear in Lancaster Court on March 23, the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department said. He escaped from military custody on February 11 while he was being returned to a jail in Hinesville, Georgia. He had been taken to nearby Fort Stewart for medical reasons, authorities said. It was not clear whether Brazelton had retained defense counsel.
simplified_kindergarten: A bad man ran away. Now the police caught him.
simplified_primary_school: A soldier ran away from the police in Georgia. He was caught at a motel in Florida. Now he is back in a California jail. He must speak to a judge soon.
simplified_secondary_school: A 20-year-old soldier named Daniel Brazelton is back in California after escaping from military custody. He was originally arrested for a serious crime but ran away while receiving medical care. Police eventually found him at a motel in Florida and flew him back to Los Angeles. He is now being held on a high bail amount. Brazelton is scheduled to appear in court later this March.

original_text: The Great Rebellion or Great Revolt is a term that is generally used in English for the following conflicts: First Jewish–Roman War in 66–73 CE, also known as the Great Revolt of Judaea Peasants' Revolt in England in 1381, also called Wat Tyler's Rebellion English Civil War in 1642–1651, also called English Revolution Rebellion of Túpac Amaru II in 1780–83, against Bourbon reforms in the Spanish Viceroyalty of Peru Wars of the Three Kingdoms, an intertwined series of conflicts that took place in England, Ireland and Scotland between 1639 and 1651, including the English Civil War Indian Rebellion of 1857, against the British East India Company In the northern states of the US, an alternate term used in naming the American Civil War (1861–65) East Timorese rebellion of 1911–12 against Portuguese colonial authorities Arab Revolt or Great Arab Revolt of 1917 (; ), against the Ottoman Empire 1936–1939 Arab revolt in Palestine, that would come to be known as "the Great Revolt". Fiction The war against the Evil Horde in She-Ra: Princess of Power The Butlerian Jihad, an event in Frank Herbert's fictional Dune universe See also List of revolutions and rebellions
simplified_kindergarten: Many people fought for their homes. They wanted to be free.
simplified_primary_school: The term Great Rebellion describes many different wars in history. People fought against leaders in places like England, India, and America. These groups wanted to change their rules. Some stories in books also use this name for big fights.
simplified_secondary_school: The term Great Rebellion refers to several historical conflicts where people rose up against their governments. Notable examples include the First Jewish-Roman War and the Indian Rebellion of 1857. In England, both the Peasants' Revolt and the English Civil War are sometimes called by this name. The American Civil War was also known as the Great Rebellion in the northern states. This title is even used in fictional stories like the Dune series. Most of these events involved citizens fighting for independence or social change.

original_text: Loic Remy has played his last game for Newcastle United after not being included in the squad for Sunday’s trip to Liverpool. It was suggested earlier in the day that the French forward had pulled out of the game and the club later said he had picked up a knock in training. Top scorer: Loic Remy has bagged 14 goals for Newcastle this season but will not be back at the club next season . Out: Reny missed the final game of the season at Liverpool because of injury . Ambition: Remy wants to move to one of Europe's top clubs this summer . Remy’s season-long loan deal with the Magpies expires following the final-day clash against the Reds. The Queens Park Rangers striker - who looks set to be included in the France squad for this summer’s World Cup - has already declared his intention to join a ‘top European club’ ahead of next season. Remy scored 14 goals for United during his season on Tyneside and has attracted interest from North London rivals Arsenal and Spurs. Meanwhile, compatriot Hatem Ben Arfa was also absent from the Newcastle squad which travelled to Anfield as his first-team exile continued.
simplified_kindergarten: The soccer player is leaving. He is hurt today.
simplified_primary_school: Loic Remy played his last game for Newcastle. He is hurt and cannot play today. He scored many goals this season. Now he wants to join a new team.
simplified_secondary_school: Loic Remy has played his final game for Newcastle United. He missed the last match against Liverpool due to an injury from training. Remy scored fourteen goals this season while on loan from his main club. He now hopes to join a top European team this summer. Both Arsenal and Spurs are interested in signing the talented French striker. Meanwhile, his teammate Hatem Ben Arfa was also left out of the squad.

original_text: The class Heterotardigrada includes tardigrades (water bears) that have cephalic appendages and legs with four separate but similar digits or claws on each. 444 species have been described. Anatomy The anatomy of the reproductive system is an important defining feature in distinguishing the different groups of tardigrades. Heterotardigrades have gonoducts that open to the outside through a preanal gonopore, rather than opening into the rectum as in the only other confirmed class of tardigrades, the Eutardigrada. The third class, Mesotardigrada, is represented by a single species known from a single specimen that is now lost, and the location from which that specimen was collected has since been destroyed by an earthquake, so its reproductive anatomy has not been studied recently. Ecology Some orders of heterotardigrades are marine, others are terrestrial, but as for all tardigrades, all are aquatic in the sense that they must be surrounded by at least a film of moisture in order to be active – though they can survive in a dormant state if the habitat dries out. References Protostome classes Taxa named by Ernst Marcus (zoologist)
simplified_kindergarten: Water bears are tiny animals. They have cute little feet.
simplified_primary_school: Water bears are small creatures with four claws on each leg. There are many different types of these animals. Some live in the ocean and some live on land. They need water to stay active and move around.
simplified_secondary_school: Heterotardigrada is a class of tardigrades known as water bears. These creatures have special appendages on their heads and four claws on every leg. Scientists identify different groups by looking at how their bodies are built. This class has over 400 species that live in many environments. They can live in the sea or on land. However, they all need a thin layer of water to remain active. If their home dries out, they can survive by sleeping for a long time.

original_text: Abyssinia is a historical name for the Ethiopian Empire Abyssinia may also refer to: Languages Ethiopian Semitic languages, mainly found in the Amhara Region and Tigray Region of Ethiopia People Abyssinian people, the Amhara and the Tigrayans Places Abyssinia Lines, a neighbourhood of Jamshed Town in Karachi, Sindh, Pakistan Apostolic Vicariate of Abyssinia, the former Eastern Catholic missionary Arts and media Abyssinia, a theatrical show by Bert Williams Abyssinia (musical), a show first staged in 1987 "Abyssinia", a song by the Patti Smith Group on Radio Ethiopia "Abyssinia, Henry", an episode of the television series M*A*S*H Vessels SS Abyssinia, an 1870 Canadian Pacific steamship HMS Abyssinia (1870), a British armoured ship Other uses Abyssinia (battle honour) Abyssinia Creek, The Pilbara, Western Australia See also Abyssinian (disambiguation) Habash (disambiguation) Habishi (disambiguation) History of Ethiopia
simplified_kindergarten: Abyssinia is an old name. It is for a place.
simplified_primary_school: Abyssinia is the old name for Ethiopia. It is also used for languages and people from that area. You can find this name in songs, plays, and old ships too.
simplified_secondary_school: Abyssinia is a historical name for the Ethiopian Empire and its people. This term also refers to Semitic languages spoken in regions like Amhara and Tigray. Beyond history, the name appears in various arts, including musicals and songs by the Patti Smith Group. It has even been used for famous steamships and locations as far away as Pakistan and Australia.

original_text: Crosstalk refers to any signal or circuit unintentionally affecting another signal or circuit. Crosstalk may also refer to: Science and computing Crosstalk (biology) Crosstalk Mk.4, a communications application for PCs. Arts and entertainment Cross talk, a style of comedy used in performance by double acts Xiangsheng, also translated as "crosstalk", a traditional Chinese comedic monologue or dialogue Crosstalk (novel), a 2016 novel by American science fiction author Connie Willis Crosstalk (film), a 1982 science fiction thriller film Cross Talk, a 1980 album by the Pretty Things Crosstalk: American Speech Music, a compilation album by the produced by Mendi + Keith Obadike CrossTalk (TV series), a television program on the Russian-based international broadcaster RT Cross Talk, a defunct radio show hosted by Indian RJ Balaji
simplified_kindergarten: One sound can mix with another. It can be a funny show.
simplified_primary_school: Crosstalk is when two signals mix together by mistake. It is also the name of a funny Chinese show. You can find it in books, movies, and music too.
simplified_secondary_school: Crosstalk occurs when a signal or circuit accidentally interferes with another one. This term also describes a traditional style of Chinese comedy called Xiangsheng. Additionally, it is the title of various books, films, and music albums. You might even recognize it as a television program or a computer application.

original_text: three , interrelated biologically - relevant examples of biased random walks are presented : ( 1 ) a model for dna melting , modelled as dna unzipping , which provides a way to illustrate the role of the boltzmann factor in a venue well - known to biology and pre - medical students ; ( 2 ) the activity of helicase motor proteins in unzipping double - stranded dna , for example , at the replication fork , which is an example of a brownian ratchet ; ( 3 ) force generation by actin polymerization , which is another brownian ratchet , and for which the force and actin - concentration dependence of the velocity of actin polymerization is determined . a
simplified_kindergarten: Small parts move like a dance. They help our bodies grow.
simplified_primary_school: Tiny parts in our body move in special ways. They help unzip our DNA. These parts work like little motors to help us grow.
simplified_secondary_school: This text explores three biological examples of biased random walks. It explains how DNA unzips using the Boltzmann factor, which is common in biology. It also describes how helicase proteins and actin polymerization act as tiny motors called Brownian ratchets. These processes generate force and help cells function properly.

original_text: Peking or Beijing is the capital city of the People's Republic of China. Peking may also refer to: Peking (ship), a 1911 German square-rigged sailing ship launched 2045 Peking, an asteroid named for the city SS City of Peking, an 1874 American iron steamship built Peking Plan, a 1939 operation in which three Polish Navy destroyers evacuated to the United Kingdom Peking Man, formally Homo erectus pekinensis, an ancient hominin Local nickname of the Swedish town Norrköping See also Pekingese Beijing (disambiguation) Beijingese (disambiguation) Pekin (disambiguation) Pekin duck (disambiguation)
simplified_kindergarten: Beijing is a big city. Many things have this name.
simplified_primary_school: Beijing is the capital city of China. It is also the name of a famous ship and a space rock. Even a very old human was named Peking Man.
simplified_secondary_school: Beijing is the capital of China, but the name Peking refers to many other things too. It was the name of a German sailing ship and a large American steamship. Scientists even used the name for an asteroid and an ancient human relative. During a famous 1939 operation, the Polish Navy used a plan named after the city.

original_text: (CNN) -- Actress Keira Knightley is engaged to James Righton, the keyboardist and singer with British rock band Klaxons, her publicist said Monday. Knightley's publicist, Sara Keene, said there would be no further details. People Magazine reports the couple has been together for about a year, following Knightley's split from actor Rupert Friend. Knightley appeared in the "Pirates of the Caribbean" movies and other films including "Atonement," "Pride & Prejudice," and "Love Actually." She also appears as Anna Karenina in the upcoming movie of the same name. Klaxons won the 2007 Mercury Music Prize in Britain for their debut album, "Myths of the Near Future," which featured the hit "Golden Skans." Their latest album, "Surfing the Void," was released in 2010.
simplified_kindergarten: The movie star is getting married. She is very happy.
simplified_primary_school: Actress Keira Knightley is getting married to a musician named James Righton. They have been together for one year. She is famous for the movie Pirates of the Caribbean. James plays in a rock band called Klaxons.
simplified_secondary_school: Actress Keira Knightley is engaged to James Righton, who is a singer and keyboardist for the British band Klaxons. The couple has been dating for about a year after Knightley ended a previous relationship. Knightley is famous for her roles in movies like Pirates of the Caribbean and Pride & Prejudice. Her husband-to-be belongs to a successful band that won a major music prize in 2007. Her publicist confirmed the news but did not share any extra details about the wedding.

original_text: A sojourner is a person who resides temporarily in a place. Sojourner may also refer to: Sojourner Truth (1797–1883), abolitionist and women's rights activist Albert Sojourner (1872-1951), member of the Mississippi House of Representatives Melanie Sojourner (born 1968) member of the Mississippi State Senate Mike Sojourner (born 1953), American retired National Basketball League player Willie Sojourner (1943–2005), basketball player and brother of Mike Sojourner Sojourner (rover), a robotic rover that was part of the Mars Pathfinder mission Sojourner (album) a box set by the alternative country band Magnolia Electric Co. Sojourners, a Christian monthly magazine Sojourner, a member of the DC Comics superhero team Hellenders See also Sojourner-Douglass College, a private learning institution named after the abolitionist Sojourner Bolt, the ring name of wrestler Josette Bynum Sojourn (disambiguation)
simplified_kindergarten: A sojourner stays for a short time. It is also a person's name.
simplified_primary_school: A sojourner is someone who stays in a place for a short time. It is also the name of a famous woman named Sojourner Truth. Many other people and even a robot on Mars use this name. You can find this name in books, music, and sports too.
simplified_secondary_school: A sojourner is someone who lives in a place temporarily rather than staying forever. The name is most famous because of Sojourner Truth, who fought for women's rights and to end slavery. Several politicians and professional basketball players also share this last name. In science, a robotic rover named Sojourner explored the planet Mars. The word also appears in popular culture as a magazine title, a music album, and even a comic book character.

original_text: (CNN) -- Firefighters in Clinton, North Carolina, discovered six bodies inside what remained of a mobile home that burned early Saturday morning. There was no immediate sign of foul play, the Sampson County Sheriff's Office said, but investigators are processing the location as a potential crime scene until the cause of the blaze is determined and autopsies are completed. The single-wide mobile home caught fire around 1 a.m.. The victims have not yet been identified, the sheriff's office said. The sheriff's office, local fire marshal's office, the North Carolina State Bureau of Investigation and the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) are investigating. Clinton is about 25 miles east of Fayetteville.
simplified_kindergarten: A home had a big fire. Six people died in the house.
simplified_primary_school: A mobile home in North Carolina caught fire on Saturday morning. Firefighters found six people dead inside the house. Many groups are working together to find out why. No one knows the names of the victims yet.
simplified_secondary_school: Firefighters discovered six bodies inside a mobile home that burned early Saturday in North Carolina. Investigators are treating the area as a crime scene until they find the cause. Several state and federal agencies are working together on this case. The victims have not been identified yet, and autopsies will be performed soon.

simplified_primary_school: Prescott is a name used for many people and places. There are cities called Prescott in America and Canada. It is also the name of some schools and a fast computer chip.
simplified_secondary_school: Prescott is a common name that refers to various famous people, locations, and organizations. There are many cities named Prescott across the United States and Canada, including a town in Ontario. Famous individuals with this name include bankers, politicians, and the physicist James Prescott Joule. Additionally, the name is used for colleges, a motor racing event, and even a specific type of computer processor.

original_text: in social networks of human individuals , social relationships do not necessarily last forever as they can either fade gradually with time , resulting in `` link aging '' , or terminate abruptly , causing `` link deletion '' , as even old friendships may cease . in this paper , we study a social network formation model where we introduce several ways by which a link termination takes place . if we adopt the link aging , we get a more modular structure with more homogeneously distributed link weights within communities than when link deletion is used . by investigating distributions and relations of various network characteristics , we find that the empirical findings are better reproduced with the link deletion model . this indicates that link deletion plays a more prominent role in organizing social networks than link aging .
simplified_kindergarten: Friends can go away. New friends come later.
simplified_primary_school: Friends can drift apart or stop talking suddenly. We studied how people make and lose friends. Losing friends quickly happens more often in real life. This helps shape how groups of people stay together.
simplified_secondary_school: In social networks, relationships can slowly fade away or end suddenly. This study looks at different ways that these social links disappear over time. When links fade slowly, the groups look more organized and similar. However, the model where friendships end abruptly matches real-world data much better. This suggests that sudden breakups are more important for shaping our social world.

original_text: the spectral excess theorem for distance - regular graphs states that a regular ( connected ) graph is distance - regular if and only if its spectral - excess equals its average excess . a bipartite graph @xmath0 is distance - biregular when it is distance - regular around each vertex and the intersection array only depends on the stable set such a vertex belongs to . in this note we derive a new version of the spectral excess theorem for bipartite distance - biregular graphs .
simplified_kindergarten: Math helps us see shapes. We look for patterns.
simplified_primary_school: Math helps us find patterns in shapes and dots. Some shapes have special rules about their lines. We found a new way to check these rules.
simplified_secondary_school: The spectral excess theorem helps identify specific types of connected graphs. Distance-regular graphs have special patterns where certain math values are equal. This note provides a new version of the theorem for bipartite distance-biregular graphs. These graphs follow different rules depending on which group of points you study.

original_text: (CNN) -- In the world of diving, this is as good as it gets. Standing on a platform some 27 meters high with Bilbao's Nervion River flowing underneath, the view from La Salve Bridge is something to behold. The spectacular Guggenheim Museum provides a breathtaking backdrop for those brave enough to leap into the water from a platform three times higher than those used at the Olympic Games. The Red Bull Cliff Diving World Series brings together some of the best in the business and the 52,000 which flocked to see the likes of Russia's Artem Silchenko and overall champion, Gary Hunt, were not to be disappointed. The 14 men from across the world produced a number of breathtaking dives, hitting the water at 85 kilometers an hour during the event which has been held every year since 2009. While Silchenko won the Bilbao event, Hunt finished second as the Briton secured his fourth world title in five years.
simplified_kindergarten: A man jumps from a high bridge. He lands in the water.
simplified_primary_school: Men jump from a very high bridge into a river. Many people watch them dive near a big museum. Gary Hunt won the world title this year. These brave divers move very fast in the air.
simplified_secondary_school: The Red Bull Cliff Diving World Series recently took place on a high bridge in Bilbao. Fourteen brave men jumped into the river from twenty-seven meters above the water. This platform is three times higher than the ones used in the Olympics. Over 50,000 fans watched as the divers reached speeds of 85 kilometers per hour. Artem Silchenko won the event, but Gary Hunt earned the overall world title. This exciting competition has been held every year since 2009.

original_text: Arensnuphis (in Egyptian: Iryhemesnefer, ỉrỉ-ḥms-nfr, "the good companion") is a deity from the Kingdom of Kush in ancient Nubia, first attested at Musawwarat el-Sufra in the 3rd century BC. His worship spread to the Egyptian-controlled portion of Nubia in the Ptolemaic Period (305–30 BC). His mythological role is unknown; he was depicted as a lion and as a human with a crown of feathers and sometimes a spear. Arensnuphis was worshipped at Philae, where he was called the "companion" of the Egyptian goddess Isis, as well as at Dendur. In the ancient Egyptian religion, the Egyptians syncretized him with their gods Anhur and Shu. References Nubian gods Egyptian gods
simplified_kindergarten: He is a good friend. He looks like a lion.
simplified_primary_school: Arensnuphis was a god from a place called Nubia. He looked like a man or a brave lion. People thought he was a good friend to a goddess. He wore a tall crown with feathers on his head.
simplified_secondary_school: Arensnuphis was an ancient deity who originally came from the Kingdom of Kush in Nubia. His worship later spread into Egypt during the Ptolemaic Period. He was often depicted as a lion or a man wearing a feathered crown and holding a spear. In Egyptian mythology, he was known as the companion of the goddess Isis. The Egyptians also linked him to their own gods, such as Anhur and Shu. He had important temples at places like Philae and Dendur.

original_text: A former Guantanamo Bay detainee from Britain appeared in a London court Saturday, charged with Syria-related terrorism offenses, UK police said. Moazzam Begg, 45, from Birmingham, is accused of providing instruction and training for terrorism and funding terrorism overseas, West Midlands Police said. A 44-year-old woman, Gerrie Tahari, also from Birmingham, is also accused of funding terrorism overseas. Both suspects, who were arrested Tuesday and charged late Friday, denied the charges at Westminster Magistrates' Court. They were remanded in custody to appear at the Old Bailey criminal court in London on March 14. Two other men, aged 20 and 36, who were also arrested on Tuesday remain in custody, the police said. Begg was arrested as a suspected al Qaeda member in Pakistan in February 2002 and was held at the U.S. airbase at Bagram, Afghanistan, before being sent to the U.S. prison camp at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba. Begg spent nearly three years in custody. He was sent back to Britain by the Bush administration in early 2005 and was never prosecuted. He co-authored a book about his experiences at Guantanamo Bay in 2006 and has appeared as a public speaker and commentator in Britain and overseas since his release.
simplified_kindergarten: The police caught a man. He must go to a big court.
simplified_primary_school: Police caught a man and a woman in England. They are in trouble for helping bad people in Syria. They went to court on Saturday to talk to a judge. They will stay in jail until their next court date.
simplified_secondary_school: A man named Moazzam Begg and a woman named Gerrie Tahari appeared in a London court on Saturday. Police charged them with supporting terrorism in Syria through training and funding. Both suspects denied the charges during the hearing at Westminster Magistrates' Court. Begg was previously held at Guantanamo Bay for three years but was released without charges in 2005. Since his release, he has become a well-known author and public speaker. The court decided to keep both suspects in custody until their next appearance on March 14. Two other men who were arrested earlier this week also remain in police custody.

In music, a Canadian rock band and a Pearl Jam song both used this name. You might also find it as a surname for people like musician Felton Pilate.

simplified_kindergarten: Tiny bits of light change colors. Some colors are very small.

simplified_secondary_school: The word client usually describes a person who pays for services or a computer that connects to a server. It is also a popular title for various novels, films, and television shows. In history, it referred to people protected by powerful leaders in ancient Rome.

original_text: Year 109 (CIX) was a common year starting on Monday (link will display the full calendar) of the Julian calendar. At the time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Palma and Tullus (or, less frequently, year 862 Ab urbe condita). The denomination 109 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Roman Empire June 24 – The Aqua Traiana is inaugurated by Emperor Trajan; the aqueduct channels water from Lake Bracciano, north-west of Rome. The Via Traiana is constructed at the Emperor Trajan's personal expense; the road connects Benevento with Brundisium (Brindisi). The Baths of Trajan, built by the architect Apollodorus of Damascus, are dedicated during the Calends. The thermae are constructed on the platform of the Palace of Nero (Domus Aurea) in Rome. Osroes I of Parthia succeeds his brother Pacorus II, and rules over the western Parthian Empire. Pliny the Younger is legate to Bithynia. By topic Religion The Christian Church proclaims itself to be universal (catholic). References
simplified_kindergarten: Long ago, a king built roads. He also made clean water flow.
simplified_primary_school: In the year 109, a Roman leader named Trajan built many things. He opened a new path for water and a long road. He also built big public baths for the people. During this time, the Christian Church began to call itself universal.
simplified_secondary_school: The year 109 was known in Rome as the Year of the Consulship of Palma and Tullus. During this period, Emperor Trajan inaugurated the Aqua Traiana, an aqueduct that brought water from Lake Bracciano. He also personally funded the construction of the Via Traiana road. In Rome, the famous Baths of Trajan were dedicated on the site of Nero's former palace. Meanwhile, Osroes I became the new ruler of the western Parthian Empire. In religious history, this year is significant because the Christian Church began describing itself as catholic, or universal.

original_text: (CNN) -- The 9-year-old boy who sneaked onto a plane for a trip to Las Vegas is back in his hometown of Minneapolis, a family spokesman said Saturday night. Spokesman V. J. Smith would not provide details. He said he would speak with the boy's father Sunday. Crew members aboard a Delta Air Lines flight October 3 became suspicious of their child passenger and then found that he had no ticket or boarding pass. He was placed in a foster home as authorities began an investigation. The Transportation Security Administration has said the boy went through security at Minneapolis-St. Paul International Airport, although it has not explained how he did so without a boarding pass. An airport spokesman said video shows the boy chatted with a Delta agent when he reached the gate and moved toward the plane when the agent became distracted. Father: 'No one stepped up to help' 'He's got a behavioral problem,' dad says . CNN's Janet DiGiacomo contributed to this report.
simplified_kindergarten: A young boy flew away. Now he is home.
simplified_primary_school: A nine-year-old boy snuck onto a plane to Las Vegas. He did not have a ticket. Workers found him and sent him home to his family. His dad says the boy needs help with his behavior.
simplified_secondary_school: A nine-year-old boy who secretly flew to Las Vegas has returned to his home in Minneapolis. He managed to pass through airport security and board a Delta flight without a ticket. Crew members became suspicious during the flight and contacted the authorities. The boy's father says his son has behavioral problems that led to this incident.

original_text: Year 289 (CCLXXXIX) was a common year starting on Tuesday (link will display the full calendar) of the Julian calendar. At the time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Bassus and Quintianus (or, less frequently, year 1042 Ab urbe condita). The denomination 289 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Roman Empire In this or the following year, Emperor Diocletian campaigns with success against the Sarmatians. The future emperor Galerius may have distinguished himself during this campaign. In this or the following year, Maximian attempts to reconquer Britain from the usurper Carausius but is defeated at sea. Births Flavia Maxima Fausta, Roman empress (d. 326) Yu Liang (or Yuangui), Chinese politician (d. 340) Deaths Alexander of Rome, Christian martyr Kyriaki the Great, Christian martyr Xun Xu (or Gongzeng), Chinese official References
simplified_kindergarten: Long ago, people lived in Rome. Two kings fought on the water.
simplified_primary_school: The year 289 was a long time ago. Emperor Diocletian won a big fight. Another leader lost a battle at sea. A future empress named Fausta was born this year.
simplified_secondary_school: In the year 289, the Roman Emperor Diocletian led a successful military campaign against the Sarmatians. Meanwhile, Maximian failed to retake Britain after losing a battle at sea against Carausius. This year also marked the birth of Fausta, who later became a Roman empress. During this time, the Romans used different names for years based on their leaders.

original_text: The year 670 BC was a year of the pre-Julian Roman calendar. In the Roman Empire, it was known as year 84 Ab urbe condita . The denomination 670 BC for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events King Gyges of Lydia mints the first Greek coins. Miletus begins establishing colonies in the Black Sea and Mediterranean Sea. Metallurgy was invented Births Deaths References 670s BC
simplified_kindergarten: Long ago, a king made coins. People built new homes by the sea.
simplified_primary_school: In the year 670 BC, King Gyges made the first Greek coins. People moved to new places by the sea to live. This happened a very long time ago in history.
simplified_secondary_school: The year 670 BC was an important time in history when King Gyges of Lydia created the first Greek coins. During this period, people from Miletus traveled to build new colonies near the Black Sea. Although we call it 670 BC today, the Romans had a different name for this year. These events helped trade and travel grow across the Mediterranean region.

simplified_primary_school: Scientists studied how small parts form in different places. They found a new way to measure time for these parts. This helps us see how things grow. It works the same way every time.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers analyzed data from the HERMES experiment to study how particles are produced in different nuclei. original_text: Zijpe () is a former municipality in the Netherlands, in the province of North Holland. In 2013, Zijpe and Harenkarspel merged into Schagen. Population centres The former municipality of Zijpe consisted of the following cities, towns, villages and/or districts: Burgerbrug, Burgervlotbrug, Callantsoog, Groote Keeten, Oudesluis, Petten, Schagerbrug, Sint Maartensbrug, Sint Maartensvlotbrug, 't Zand. Local government The municipal council of Zijpe consisted of 15 seats, which were divided as follows: BKV - 5 seats CDA - 5 seats PvdA - 3 seats VVD - 2 seats External links City Site (Dutch) Nieuws over Zijpe (Dutch) Alles over Schagen FM, de lokale omroep voor Schagen, Zijpe, Niedorp en Harenkarspel (Dutch) Schagen Former municipalities of North Holland Municipalities of the Netherlands disestablished in 2013
simplified_kindergarten: Zijpe was a small place. Now it is part of Schagen.

simplified_secondary_school: Zijpe was a municipality located in the North Holland province of the Netherlands. In 2013, it merged with Harenkarspel to form the larger municipality of Schagen. The area included several small towns and villages, such as Callantsoog and Petten. Before the merger, the local government consisted of fifteen council members from different political parties.

original_text: Zutendaal, in English sometimes also referred to as Sweet Valley, is a municipality located in the Belgian province of Limburg. On 1 January 2017 Zutendaal had a total population of 7,269. The total area is 32.07 km2, giving a population density of 227 inhabitants per km2. In addition to Zutendaal itself, the municipality includes the following population centres: Gewaai, Papendaal, Besmer, Broek, Stalken, Roelen, Daal, and Wiemesmeer. Etymology The first written mention of Zutendaal was in 1292 under the name of Suerbroeck, indicating that the land around the village was acidic and swampy. From 1345 the name changed to Zuetendael which literally translates to "Sweet Valley". Indicating there were a lot of freshwater sources around the village. Demographic evolution Bronnen:NIS, Opm:1806 tot en met 1981=volkstellingen; 1990 en later= inwonertal op 1 januari References External links - Available only in Dutch Municipalities of Limburg (Belgium)
simplified_kindergarten: Zutendaal is a sweet town. Many people live there.
simplified_primary_school: Zutendaal is a small town in Belgium. It is also called Sweet Valley. Long ago, the land was very wet and swampy. Now, over seven thousand people live in this green place.
simplified_secondary_school: Zutendaal is a Belgian municipality located in the province of Limburg. It is often called Sweet Valley because of its many freshwater sources. In 2017, the town had a population of over seven thousand people. The area includes several smaller centers like Wiemesmeer and Besmer. Historically, the land was described as swampy before the name changed in 1345. Today, it remains a notable part of the Limburg region.

original_text: Year 486 (CDLXXXVI) was a common year starting on Wednesday (link will display the full calendar) of the Julian calendar. At the time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Basilius and Longinus (or, less frequently, year 1239 Ab urbe condita). The denomination 486 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Europe Battle of Soissons: Frankish forces under King Clovis I defeat the Gallo-Roman kingdom of Soissons (Gaul). Roman rule under Syagrius ends. The land between the Somme and the Loire becomes a part of the Frankish Empire. Syagrius flees to the Visigoths (under King Alaric II), but Clovis threatens war, and he is handed over for execution. Clovis I establishes his new residence at Soissons. He appoints Ragnachar, Frankish petty king (regulus), as his deputy ruler. By topic Religion Persian Christians who follow Nestorianism gather in the second Council of Seleucia (modern Turkey). Births Deaths Syagrius, Roman official in Gaul (approximate date) References
simplified_kindergarten: A king won a big fight. He lived in a new house.
simplified_primary_school: In the year 486, King Clovis won a big battle. He defeated the last Roman leader in France. The king moved to a new city to rule. The Roman leader was caught and killed.
simplified_secondary_school: The year 486 was an important time when King Clovis I defeated the last Roman ruler in Gaul. After winning the Battle of Soissons, Clovis took over the land between two major rivers. The defeated leader, Syagrius, tried to hide with the Visigoths but was eventually executed. This victory allowed the Frankish Empire to expand its power across the region. Meanwhile, in the East, Persian Christians gathered for an important meeting called the Council of Seleucia.

original_text: UP Health System - Marquette (formerly Marquette General Hospital) is an American hospital and regional medical center located in Marquette and serving the Upper Peninsula of Michigan. The hospital is a Level II trauma center as verified by the American College of Surgeons. The hospital trains medical students from the Michigan State University College of Human Medicine, as well as residents in the family medicine residency program. Prior to 1973, Marquette had two hospitals: St. Mary's Hospital and St. Luke's Hospital. Marquette General Hospital was formed in 1973 when the two Marquette hospitals merged. In 2012, Michigan Attorney General Bill Schuette approved Marquette General Hospitals purchase by for-profit organization Duke LifePoint. It was the fourth hospital to be acquired by the company, after Maria Parham Medical Center in Henderson, North Carolina, Person Memorial Hospital in Roxboro, North Carolina, and Twin County Regional Healthcare in Galax, Virginia. References External links Hospitals in Michigan Buildings and structures in Marquette, Michigan Trauma centers
simplified_kindergarten: This is a big hospital. It helps sick people.

simplified_secondary_school: UP Health System - Marquette is a major medical center located in Michigan's Upper Peninsula. It serves as a Level II trauma center and trains medical students from Michigan State University. The hospital was created in 1973 when St. Mary's and St. Luke's hospitals merged together. In 2012, a company called Duke LifePoint purchased the facility with approval from the state. It was the fourth hospital acquired by this organization.

original_text: Burnley are keen on a deal for West Bromwich Albion midfielder Graham Dorrans. Manager Sean Dyche had initially considered a move for Cardiff City's Icelandic international Aron Gunnarsson . But Albion are willing to listen to bids for Dorrans and his added Premier League experience is appealing for Burnley. Graham Dorrans fires a shot towards goal during West Brom's league match against West Ham last month . Dorrans (right) tackles Craig Baxter during West Brom's FA Cup match against Gateshead . Dorrans, 27, has been at West Brom since 2008 but manager Tony Pulis wants four new players ideally and is willing to sacrifice the Scotland international to strengthen other areas. Pulis has been rebuffed in attempts to sign Rickie Lambert from Liverpool and has cooled his interest for Blackburn's Rudy Gestede while Demba Ba's wages make any move difficult. Rickie Lambert (centre) in action for Liverpool against AFC Wimbledon in the FA Cup last week .

Pulis wants to raise money to bring in four new players for his own squad. He has struggled to sign other targets like Rickie Lambert due to high costs and wages.

original_text: Alvaro Negredo has been named in Valencia's squad for the first time since breaking his foot before his loan move from Manchester City. The Spanish forward could now make his long-awaited debut for the club against regional rival Elche at Mestalla Stadium on Saturday. 29-year-old Negredo scored 23 goals in 48 games as Manchester City won the Premier League and Capital One Cup last season. Alvaro Negredo poses during his unveiling as a Valencia player last month . The Spanish forward has not played for Valencia after breaking his foot in a pre-season game in July . But the former Sevilla man has been sidelined since breaking his right foot in one of City's pre-season games in July. Negredo is currently at Valencia on a one-year loan deal that includes an obligatory purchase clause at the end of the deal. The La Liga club are currently sitting fourth in the division, five points off leaders Barcelona after eight games. Negredo celebrates with the Capital One Cup after helping City to the trophy last season .
simplified_kindergarten: The soccer player is better now. He will play a big game.

simplified_secondary_school: Alvaro Negredo has joined Valencia's squad for the first time since breaking his foot in July. The Spanish striker joined the club on loan from Manchester City. He previously scored many goals to help City win two trophies last season. Now he is ready to make his debut against Elche this weekend. Valencia currently holds fourth place in the league standings. If he plays well, the club must buy him at the end of the year.

original_text: Ensamma mamman ("single mom") is a Swedish comic strip created by Cecilia Torudd. Ensamma mamman first made a brief appearance in Dagens Nyheter in 1985. It was well received by the readers and returned in 1987 as a fixed feature. Torudd continued drawing it until 1991, when it was syndicated by Bull, and it has since been rerun in many newspapers. Ensamma mamman deals with a single mother and her two teenage children, her 14-year-old son Beppe and her daughter Mia, 17 years old. Ensamma mamman received the Urhunden Prize by the Swedish Association for Promotion of Comics, in 1989. Source Swedish comic strips 1985 comics debuts Comics about women Gag-a-day comics Female characters in comics Comics set in Sweden Comics characters introduced in 1985 Swedish comics characters
simplified_kindergarten: A mom and two kids live together. They are in a funny book.
simplified_primary_school: Ensamma mamman is a famous Swedish comic strip. It is about a single mother and her two teenage children. Cecilia Torudd created the story in 1985. Many people like the funny stories about this family.
simplified_secondary_school: Ensamma mamman is a popular Swedish comic strip created by Cecilia Torudd in 1985. The story follows a single mother living with her two teenage children, Mia and Beppe. It became a permanent feature in newspapers because readers enjoyed the relatable characters. In 1989, the series won a prestigious award called the Urhunden Prize. Although the original drawings ended in 1991, many newspapers still print old copies today.

original_text: Wondul Range is a national park in Queensland, Australia, 209 km west of Brisbane. It is located in the Toowoomba Region local government area of the Darling Downs. The park aims to conserve open woodland species typical of the Western Downs. It lies within the water catchment area of the Macintyre River and Weir Rivers in the Brigalow Belt South bioregion. Whetstone State Forest lies to the south and Bulli State Forest to the west, both adjacent to the national park. A total of five rare or threatened species have been identified within Wondul Range National Park. The park has no facilities. The main recreational activities undertaken in the park are bird watching and wildlife observation. See also Protected areas of Queensland References National parks of Queensland Darling Downs Protected areas established in 1992 1992 establishments in Australia
simplified_kindergarten: The park has many trees. You can see pretty birds here.
simplified_primary_school: Wondul Range is a big park in Australia. It protects many trees and plants. There are five rare animals living there. People visit the park to watch birds and wildlife.
simplified_secondary_school: Wondul Range National Park is located in Queensland, Australia, west of Brisbane. This park was created to protect woodland plants and animals typical of the region. It is home to five species that are rare or threatened. There are no visitor facilities, so people mostly go there for bird watching. The park is surrounded by state forests and helps protect the local water supply.

original_text: Deoxycytidine is a deoxyribonucleoside, a component of deoxyribonucleic acid. It is similar to the ribonucleoside cytidine, but with one hydroxyl group removed from the C2' position. Deoxycytidine can be phosphorylated at C5' of the deoxyribose by deoxycytidine kinase, converting it to deoxycytidine monophosphate (dCMP), a DNA precursor. dCMP can be converted to dUMP and dTMP. It can also be used as a precursor for 5-aza-2′-deoxycytidine, a treatment for MDS patients. This compound slows the cell cycle by interfering with the methylation of the P15/INK4B gene, increasing the expression of P15/INK4B protein which subdues the transformation of MDS to leukemia. References Nucleosides Pyrimidones Hydroxymethyl compounds
simplified_kindergarten: Small parts help build your body. They help you stay healthy.
simplified_primary_school: Deoxycytidine is a tiny part used to build your DNA. It helps your body grow and stay strong. Doctors use it to make medicine for sick people. This medicine helps stop bad cells from growing too fast.
simplified_secondary_school: Deoxycytidine is a nucleoside that serves as a vital building block for DNA. It is converted into other molecules that help create genetic material. Scientists also use it to create medicine for patients with bone marrow disorders. This treatment works by slowing down the growth of harmful cells and preventing cancer.

original_text: (CNN) -- The managerial merry-go-round has stopped for Roberto Mancini -- the Italian has been named as the new boss of Galatasaray. The man who failed to defend Manchester City's Premier League title last season now faces the same task with the Turkish champions. The Istanbul based club have signed a three year deal with Mancini, who is expected to be at the helm for their Champions League group tie against Juventus on Wednesday. Mancini was sacked by Manchester City in May, a year to the day after guiding the club to its first title in nearly half a century. The trophies aside, his time at City is likely to be remembered for several rows with star players, most notably Carlos Tevez and Mario Balotelli. The Italian replaces former Fatih Terim, a defensive legend turned manager for Galatasaray, who failed to agree a new deal with the club.
simplified_kindergarten: A new man will lead the team. He helps the players win.

simplified_secondary_school: Roberto Mancini has been appointed as the new manager of Galatasaray on a three-year contract. The Italian coach previously led Manchester City to a title before being dismissed last May. He is replacing Fatih Terim, who could not agree on a new deal with the Turkish club. Mancini is expected to lead the team during their upcoming match against Juventus. While he won trophies in England, he was also known for having disagreements with some famous players.

original_text: Former Celtic striker Henrik Larsson has been appointed manager of Helsingborg in the Swedish league. The announcement was made at news conference on Monday. Larsson, 43, started coaching smaller teams in Sweden after retiring as a player in 2009. Most recently he coached top-flight debutant Falkenberg. Former Celtic striker Henrik Larsson has been appointed as the manager of Helsingborg in the Swedish league . Widely considered as one of Sweden’s greatest footballers, Larsson started and finished his high-level career in Helsingborg. In between, he played for Feyenoord, Celtic and Barcelona. He also joined Manchester United on a three-month loan in 2007. Helsingborg has not placed better than fifth since winning the Swedish league in 2011. The team finished a disappointing ninth in the recently ended 2014 season, 23 points behind winner Malmo. In his playing days, Larsson wore the shirts of clubs like Feyenoord, Celtic, Barcelona and Manchester United . Larsson shared an emotional farewell with the Helsingborg fans when he retired as a player in October 2009 . The Swede will now take charge of his old club, replacing Roar Hansen in the manager's seat at Helsingborg .
simplified_kindergarten: Henrik is a soccer leader. He helps his old team.
simplified_primary_school: Henrik Larsson is the new manager for his old soccer team. He was a very famous player before he became a coach. He played for big teams like Celtic and Barcelona. Now he wants to help his team win more games.
simplified_secondary_school: Henrik Larsson has been appointed as the new manager of Helsingborg in the Swedish league. The former Celtic striker began and ended his playing career at this club before retiring in 2009. He previously played for famous teams including Barcelona and Manchester United. Helsingborg finished in a disappointing ninth place during the most recent season. Larsson hopes to improve the team's performance after they struggled to win lately. He replaces Roar Hansen as the person in charge of the squad.

original_text: Vleteren () is a municipality located in the Belgian province of West Flanders. The municipality comprises the towns of Oostvleteren, Westvleteren and Woesten. On January 1, 2006, Vleteren had a total population of 3,636. The total area is 38.15 km2 which gives a population density of 95 inhabitants per km2. original_text: (CNN) -- A Northern California woman was arrested on suspicion of taking a bite out of two kids in the children's section of a bookstore, police said. Polly Beltramo, 46, of Palo Alto, is accused of biting a 2-year-old girl and a baby boy on the cheek at BookBuyers in Mountain View on Thursday, police said. The incident began when Beltramo allegedly bit the girl, whose mother was in the children's section of the store, witnesses said. Bookstore owner Rammurti Reed said he heard the girl screaming and saw a red welt on her cheek. "Suddenly a little boy, who was just a couple of months old, started screaming," Reed told CNN affiliate KGO-TV. "I went to him and his cheek was bleeding." Beltramo allegedly fled the store and was later found at a nearby coffee shop, police said. Beltramo has been arrested on felony child abuse, police said.
simplified_kindergarten: A mean woman hurt two kids. She bit them in a store.
simplified_primary_school: A woman bit two small children in a bookstore. The police caught her at a coffee shop near the store. She went to jail because she hurt the kids. The children were very scared and they cried a lot.
simplified_secondary_school: A California woman was arrested after she allegedly bit two young children at a bookstore. The victims included a two-year-old girl and a baby boy. Witnesses said the woman attacked the children on their cheeks before fleeing the scene. Police eventually found the suspect at a nearby coffee shop. She is now facing serious child abuse charges for her actions.

original_text: Year 269 BC was a year of the pre-Julian Roman calendar. At the time it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Gallus and Pictor (or, less frequently, year 485 Ab urbe condita). The denomination 269 BC for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Sicily The Mamertines, a body of Campanian mercenaries who have been employed by Agathocles, the former tyrant of Syracuse, capture the stronghold of Messana (Messina in north-eastern Sicily), from which they harass the Syracusans. The Syracusan military leader, Hieron, defeats them in a pitched battle at the Longanus River near Mylae, but Carthaginian forces intervene to prevent him from capturing Messana. His grateful countrymen then choose Hieron as their king and tyrant, to be known as Hieron II. Births Attalus I Soter, ruler of Pergamon, from 241 to 197 BC. He will be the first of the Attalid dynasty to assume the title of king (d. 197 BC). Deaths References
simplified_kindergarten: Long ago, a king won a fight. His people were very happy.
simplified_primary_school: In 269 BC, a leader named Hieron won a big battle. His people were happy and made him their king. In the same year, a famous ruler named Attalus was born. People used different names for this year in the past.
simplified_secondary_school: The year 269 BC was originally known by the names of the Roman consuls in power. In Sicily, a military leader named Hieron defeated a group of mercenaries at the Longanus River. Although Carthaginian forces stopped him from taking the city of Messana, his people crowned him King Hieron II. This year also marked the birth of Attalus I, who later became the first king of the Attalid dynasty. These historical events occurred before the modern calendar system was commonly used in Europe.

simplified_primary_school: Mimas is a name used for many different things. It is a moon of Saturn and a giant from old stories. It is also a town and a moth.
