original_text: (CNN) -- Hollywood actor Johnny Depp is arguably best-known for his role as maverick captain Jack Sparrow in the wildly popular "Pirates of the Caribbean" franchise. Now, he has taken things a step further with his own pirate-inspired ship in the Caribbean. It may not be as creepy as the "Pirates of the Caribbean's" "Black Pearl," but Depp's ship "Vajoliroja" has more than a hint of the swashbuckler about it. Depp has completely refitted the ship to his taste, with velvet wall hangings and furniture in rich burgundies and gold. Wannabe pirates can test their sea legs on Depp's yacht, which is available for charter, although it's not cheap. One week aboard the ship, which hosts up to 11 guests and eight crew, costs a cool $130,000. But help is on hand to distract visiting landlubbers from the dent in their booty: "Vajoliroja" boasts all the latest gadgets, including two speed boats, kayaks, wind surfs, water skis, wakeboards and more.
simplified_kindergarten: Johnny Depp has a big boat. It looks like a pirate ship.
simplified_primary_school: Johnny Depp is a famous actor who played a pirate. Now he has his own ship for his friends. It has red and gold rooms inside. You can pay to stay on the boat for a week.
simplified_secondary_school: Johnny Depp is famous for playing Captain Jack Sparrow in the pirate movies. He now owns a luxury yacht called the Vajoliroja. The ship is decorated with red velvet and gold furniture to look like a pirate ship. You can rent the boat for one week, but it costs $130,000. It has many fun things like speed boats, kayaks, and water skis for guests to use.

original_text: Japan defender Maya Yoshida has agreed a new three-year contract at Southampton, the club have announced. The 26-year-old has been with Southampton since August 2012, making 60 appearances and scoring three goals. Yoshida, who can play at right-back as well as in the centre, has now joined up with Japan ahead of the Asian Cup in Australia. Maya Yoshida (right), pictured with Arsenal's Theo Walcott, has signed a new contract with Southampton . Yoshida gets away from Chelsea's Filipe Luis (left) during Southampton's 1-1 draw with Jose Mourinho's side . Japan are set to play Palestine on January 12 and then have matches against Iraq and Jordan. Yoshida has played an important role under Saints boss Ronald Koeman this season, making 11 Premier League appearances and scoring once against Everton. The former VVV-Venlo defender came off the bench during Southampton's 2-0 win against Arsenal on New Year's Day, a result which kept them fourth in the Premier League table. Yoshida, pictured challenging Crystal Palace's Yannick Bolasie, has extended his deal until the summer 2018 .
simplified_kindergarten: The soccer player stays at his club. He will play more games now.
simplified_primary_school: Maya Yoshida plays soccer for Southampton. He signed a new deal to stay for three years. He is a very important player for his team. Now he is traveling to play for Japan.
simplified_secondary_school: Southampton defender Maya Yoshida has signed a new three-year contract that lasts until 2018. Since joining in 2012, he has played 60 games and scored three goals for the team. He is a key player for manager Ronald Koeman and helped the team reach fourth place. Yoshida is now joining the Japan national team for the Asian Cup in Australia. Japan will face teams like Palestine, Iraq, and Jordan in the tournament.

original_text: (CNN) -- Two F-16 fighter jets briefly took to the sky over Washington on Monday after a small passenger aircraft violated the capital's airspace restrictions, according to military officials. A portion of the White House grounds was briefly evacuated during the incident. The passenger plane -- a Cessna 182 -- was intercepted by the F-16s at 2:19 p.m. EST, a statement from the North American Aerospace Defense Command noted. The plane was escorted to an airport in nearby Manassas, Virginia, where it landed at 2:32 p.m. EST. The Cessna pilot was a student pilot who initially took off from Warrenton, Virginia, the Federal Aviation Administration confirmed. The Secret Service will interview the pilot, a spokesman for the agency told CNN.

simplified_secondary_school: Two F-16 fighter jets were sent to intercept a small plane over Washington on Monday. The small aircraft had entered restricted airspace, which caused a brief evacuation at the White House. Military jets escorted the plane to an airport in Virginia where it landed safely. The pilot was a student who will be interviewed by the Secret Service.

original_text: Tjoritja / West MacDonnell is a national park in the Northern Territory (Australia) due west of Alice Springs and 1234 km south of Darwin. It extends along the MacDonnell Ranges west of Alice Springs. The popular extended walk, the Larapinta Trail, runs east–west along the linear park, following the West MacDonnell Ranges. The park includes many tourist attractions along its 250 kilometre length including Ormiston Pound, the Ellery Creek Bighole, Glen Helen Gorge, Simpsons Gap, Standley Chasm, Mount Sonder, Serpentine Gorge, the Ochre Pits and Redbank Gorge. The Park is known as Tjoritja by the traditional owners of the land and is considered of great significance in the local Arrernte Aboriginal culture. It is home to several species of flora and fauna and is now utilised by people for a variety of recreational activities. Facilities at the park include swimming, camping, gas BBQ, bushwalking, caravan sites, etc. See also Protected areas of the Northern Territory References External links Official fact sheet and map National parks of the Northern Territory Protected areas established in 1992 1992 establishments in Australia
simplified_kindergarten: This park has big red rocks. You can walk and swim here.
simplified_primary_school: This big park is in Australia near Alice Springs. It has many beautiful places to swim and camp. People like to walk on a long trail there. The local people call this land Tjoritja.
simplified_secondary_school: Tjoritja, also known as West MacDonnell National Park, is located in Australia near Alice Springs. It spans 250 kilometers and follows a mountain range with many famous natural landmarks. The park is culturally significant to the local Arrernte Aboriginal people. Visitors enjoy many activities there, including hiking the Larapinta Trail, swimming, and camping. It provides a home for many unique plants and animals in the region.

original_text: (CNN) -- Bollywood movie star Dev Anand, a fixture in the Indian film industry for decades, has died of an apparent heart attack, CNN IBN reported Sunday. He was 88. Anand died in a London hotel Saturday night, his family said. The legendary Indian actor was known for his roles in a series of movies, including "Johnny Mera Naam," "Des Pardes" and "Hare Rama Hare Krishna." Prime Minister Manmohan Singh offered his condolences Sunday. "Dev Anand was a great artist who entertained generations of cinema lovers over five decades," Singh said in a statement, according to CNN IBN. "He was an embodiment of a life long passion for acting and film making. I join millions of his fans in mourning his death." The star, who was known for his good looks and leading roles in romantic movies, released his book, "Romancing with Life" in 2007.
simplified_kindergarten: A famous movie star died. He was a very kind man.
simplified_primary_school: Dev Anand was a famous movie star from India. He died in London at age 88. He acted in many popular films for a long time. Many people are sad because they loved his movies.
simplified_secondary_school: The legendary Bollywood actor Dev Anand passed away in London at the age of 88. He was a famous star for over fifty years and appeared in many classic films. Prime Minister Manmohan Singh praised him as a great artist with a lifelong passion for cinema. Millions of fans around the world are now mourning the loss of this romantic movie icon.

original_text: New Delhi, India (CNN) -- Two bomb blasts killed at least 15 people and wounded 119 others on Thursday in the southern Indian city of Hyderabad, the city's police commissioner, Anurag Sharma, said a day after the attack. Sharma didn't rule out the possibility of terror involvement in the city blasts. But he refused to identity any group as a suspect. The two bombs were planted on bicycles parked in a crowded spot, Sharma said. They were improvised explosive devices, he said, and they could have been triggered by a timer. Prime Minister Manmohan Singh called the strike a "dastardly attack" and said "the guilty will not go unpunished." He directed government agencies to extend all possible relief to authorities in Andhra Pradesh state. He also approved money for the next of kin of those killed and for others seriously injured.
simplified_kindergarten: Bad bombs hurt many people. The leaders will help them.
simplified_primary_school: Two bombs on bikes exploded in a busy city in India. Many people were hurt or killed by the blasts. The leader of India said he will punish the bad people. He is also giving money to help the families.
simplified_secondary_school: Two bombs hidden on bicycles exploded in Hyderabad, India, killing 15 people and injuring many more. Police believe the devices used timers, though they have not named a specific group responsible for the attack. Prime Minister Manmohan Singh condemned the act and promised that the guilty would be punished. He also ordered government agencies to provide relief and financial support to the victims and their families.

original_text: Year 102 (CII) was a common year starting on Saturday (link will display the full calendar) of the Julian calendar. At the time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Ursus and Sura (or, less frequently, year 855 Ab urbe condita). The denomination 102 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Roman Empire Lucius Julius Ursus Servianus and Lucius Licinius Sura become Roman consuls. Emperor Trajan returns to Rome after a successful campaign against Dacia, through which he reestablishes clear Roman sovereignty over King Decebalus. Trajan divides Pannonia into two provinces, sometime between this year and 107. The port of Portus is enlarged. Europe Asia Having organised the territories of the Tarim basin, Chinese General Ban Chao retires to Luoyang and dies shortly thereafter. Deaths Ban Chao, Chinese general of the Han Dynasty (b. AD 32) Clement I, bishop of Rome (approximate date) Yin, Chinese empress of the Han Dynasty References
simplified_kindergarten: A long time ago, a king won. He went back to his home.

simplified_secondary_school: In the year 102, Emperor Trajan returned to Rome after defeating Dacia and reclaiming Roman power. During this period, Trajan also expanded the port of Portus and divided the region of Pannonia. In Asia, the famous Chinese general Ban Chao retired to Luoyang and passed away shortly after. This year was originally known by the names of the Roman consuls serving at the time. The name '102' only became common during the medieval period when the current calendar system spread across Europe.

original_text: (CNN) -- Italian striker Giuseppe Rossi helped himself to a double as Villarreal secured their first Spanish Primera Liga win of the season with a 4-0 thumping of Espanyol on Sunday. The 23-year-old opened the scoring in the 23rd minute and added his second goal from the penalty spot. Midfielder Borja Valero and Brazilian international Nilmar were also on target as Villarreal warmed up in style for their Europa League match against Dinamo Zagreb next Thursday. In other matches on Sunday, former Danish legend Michael Laudrup is still waiting for his first victory as Real Mallorca coach, after his side lost 2-0 at Sporting Gijon. However, there was better news for Laudrup's former club Getafe. The Madrid-based side, who finished sixth in La Liga last season. secured their first win of this campaign with a 4-1 thumping of promoted Levante. The most remarkable match came at Zaragoza as the home side found themselves 5-0 down to Malaga after just 34 minutes. Zaragoza did eventually claw some respectability back but Malaga were comfortable for their 5-3 victory. Meanwhile, Saturday's matches saw a minor sensation as champions Barcelona were stunned 2-0 at home by newly-promoted side Hercules.
simplified_kindergarten: The yellow team won their game. Many players kicked the ball in.
simplified_primary_school: Villarreal won their first soccer game on Sunday. Giuseppe Rossi scored two goals for the team. Other teams like Malaga and Getafe won their games too. Barcelona lost their game even though they are champions.
simplified_secondary_school: Villarreal earned their first win of the season by defeating Espanyol 4-0. Giuseppe Rossi scored twice, helping his team prepare for their upcoming Europa League match. Meanwhile, Malaga defeated Zaragoza 5-3 in a high-scoring game that started with five early goals. Real Mallorca lost their match, but Getafe celebrated a strong 4-1 victory against Levante. The biggest surprise occurred on Saturday when the champions, Barcelona, lost 2-0 at home to Hercules.

original_text: "Debout Congolais" (; ) is the national anthem of the Democratic Republic of the Congo. It was originally adopted in 1960 upon independence from Belgium but was replaced by "La Zaïroise" when Congo changed its name to Zaire in 1971. It was finally reinstated when Congo was reorganised in 1997. The lyrics were written by historian and professor Joseph Lutumba, and the music was composed by Jesuit father , who also wrote and composed "La Zaïroise". References External links Democratic Republic of the Congo: "Debout Congolais" - Audio of the national anthem of the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with information and lyrics - National anthems.info - "La Zaïroise" (the former national anthem) National anthems.info - "Debout Congolaise" MP3 file Democratic Republic of the Congo music African anthems National symbols of the Democratic Republic of the Congo National anthem compositions in C major Democratic Republic of the Congo
simplified_kindergarten: This is a special song. It is for a big home.

simplified_secondary_school: The national anthem of the Democratic Republic of the Congo is called 'Debout Congolais.' It was first adopted in 1960 when the country gained independence from Belgium. The government replaced it in 1971, but the song was brought back in 1997. Joseph Lutumba wrote the lyrics, while Simon-Pierre Boka composed the music. Interestingly, Boka also created the song that was used when the country was named Zaire.

original_text: we develop a new general formulation to explore light - driven electron transport through a single - molecule device with multiple pathways . three individual systems are proposed including ( i ) a two - terminal molecular junction based on phenyl - acetylene macrocycle ( pam ) , ( ii ) pam with three terminals , and ( iii ) a parallel molecular circuit . the computations show that pam - based optoelectronic switches have robust large on - off ratios and weak - field operating conditions , which are not sensitive to asymmetric molecule - lead couplings . in addition , field - amplitude power laws for one- and two - photon assisted tunneling are evident in the computational results , and the laws can be proven by using perturbation analysis . for pam - based optoelectronic routers , we show that it is possible to manipulate the direction of electric current through the pam molecule by using a weak linearly polarized laser field . for parallel circuits made of molecular quantum dots , the condition of coherent destruction of tunneling is derived by using the rotating wave approximation and the high - frequency approximation .

original_text: we study the signals for a `` fermiophobic '' charged higgs boson present in an extension of the standard model with an additional higgs doublet and right handed neutrinos , responsible for generating dirac - type neutrino masses . we study the pair production of the charged higgs at the large hadron collider ( lhc ) , which can be relatively light and still allowed by experimental data . the charged higgs decays dominantly into a @xmath0 boson and a very light neutral scalar present in the model , which decays invisibly and passes undetected . we find that the signal for such a charged higgs is overwhelmed by the standard model background and will prove elusive at the 8 tev run of the lhc . we present a cut - flow based analysis to pinpoint a search strategy at the 14 tev run of the lhc which can achieve a signal significance of 5@xmath1 for a given mass range of the charged higgs .

Scientists hope this method will finally prove the existence of these particles at higher energy levels.

original_text: Telesto is a moon of Saturn. It was discovered by Smith, Reitsema, Larson and Fountain in 1980 from ground-based observations, and was provisionally designated . In the following months, several other apparitions were observed: , , and . In 1983 it was officially named after Telesto of Greek mythology. It is also designated as or Tethys B. Telesto is co-orbital with Tethys, residing in Tethys' leading Lagrangian point (). This relationship was first identified by Seidelmann et al. in 1981. Another moon, Calypso, resides in the other (trailing) Lagrangian point of Tethys, 60 degrees in the other direction from Tethys. The Saturnian system has two additional trojan moons. Exploration The Cassini probe performed a distant flyby of Telesto on October 11, 2005. The resulting images show that its surface is surprisingly smooth, devoid of small impact craters. Citations References External links NASA - Telesto Profile NASA's Solar System Exploration The Planetary Society: Telesto Moons of Saturn Trojan moons 19800408
simplified_kindergarten: Telesto is a small moon. It lives near a big planet.
simplified_primary_school: Telesto is a moon that travels around the planet Saturn. Scientists found it in 1980 using telescopes on Earth. It shares its path with a larger moon called Tethys. Its surface is very smooth and has very few holes.
simplified_secondary_school: Telesto is a moon of Saturn that was discovered by astronomers in 1980. It is known as a trojan moon because it shares an orbit with the larger moon Tethys. Another moon named Calypso orbits on the opposite side of Tethys. In 2005, the Cassini spacecraft took photos of Telesto during a flyby. These images revealed a surprisingly smooth surface with very few impact craters.

original_text: In Mesopotamian myths, the alal was a kind of demon that, to tempt men, came out of the Underworld and took various forms, temptations that the inhabitants of Babylonia were able to reject by means of amulets. The Chaldean-Assyrian art represents these spirits in the form of horrible monsters, as in the bas-reliefs of the Palace of Ashurbanipal in Nineveh (now Iraq), today in the British Museum) and in small bronzes and clay tablets cooked in the shape of a cylinder, cone, or stamp. Generally these demons are seen as theriocephalou, with a human body and the head of a lion with open jaws, the ears of a dog and mane of a horse. The feet are frequently replaced by bird claws of prey. The goddess to which the alal obeyed was called Alat, and was the wife of Nergal, god of war, and sister of Astarte. References See also Pazuzu Lamashtu Mesopotamian mythology Demons
simplified_kindergarten: A scary monster lived underground. People used magic charms to stay safe.
simplified_primary_school: In old stories, a monster called the alal tried to trick people. People wore special jewelry to keep the bad spirits away. Artists made statues of these monsters with lion heads and bird feet. They worked for a goddess who was married to the god of war.
simplified_secondary_school: In Mesopotamian mythology, the alal was a demon from the Underworld that tried to tempt people. Ancient Babylonians used protective amulets to reject these evil spirits. Artists often depicted these creatures as monsters with human bodies and lion heads. They usually had open jaws, dog ears, and sharp bird claws instead of feet. These demons served a goddess named Alat, who was the wife of the war god, Nergal. Today, you can see carvings of these spirits in the British Museum.

original_text: a growing number of observations reveal a subset of type ia supernovae undergoing circumstellar interaction ( sne ia - csm ) . we present unpublished archival @xmath0 data on sne ia - csm 2002ic and 2005gj obtained @xmath1 and 500 days post - discovery , respectively . both sne show evidence for late - time mid - infrared ( mid - ir ) emission from warm dust . the dust parameters are most consistent with a pre - existing dust shell that lies beyond the forward - shock radius , most likely radiatively heated by optical and x - ray emission continuously generated by late - time csm interaction . in the case of sn 2005gj , the mid - ir luminosity more than doubles after 1 year post - discovery . while we are not aware of any late - time optical - wavelength observations at these epochs , we attribute this rebrightening to renewed shock interaction with a dense circumstellar shell . +
simplified_kindergarten: Stars can blow up. They make bright dust.
simplified_primary_school: Some exploding stars hit big clouds of space dust. This makes the dust very hot and bright. Scientists found two stars that stayed bright for a long time. The heat comes from the star hitting the dust shell.
simplified_secondary_school: Scientists are studying a special type of exploding star that interacts with surrounding gas and dust. This glow happens because the explosion's energy heats up a large shell of nearby dust. In one case, the star actually got brighter after a year. This suggests the explosion hit a new, thick layer of material in space.

original_text: Hardwick is a census-designated place (CDP) in Baldwin County, Georgia, United States. The population was 3,329 at the 2020 census, down from 5,819 in 2000, at which time it was listed as Midway-Hardwick. It is laid out around Central State Hospital. It is part of the Milledgeville Micropolitan Statistical Area. The zip code for Hardwick is 31034. Hardwick was home to Oglethorpe University during the 19th century. Geography Hardwick is located at (33.052571, -83.237130). According to the United States Census Bureau, the CDP has a total area of , of which , or 0.20%, is water. Demographics 2020 census As of the 2020 United States census, there were 3,513 people, 1,401 households, and 648 families residing in the CDP. Government and infrastructure Baldwin State Prison (previously Georgia Women's Correctional Institution) of the Georgia Department of Corrections is located in Milledgeville, near Hardwick. Notable person William Usery, Jr., United States Secretary of Labor (1976–1977) References Census-designated places in Baldwin County, Georgia Census-designated places in Georgia (U.S. state) Milledgeville micropolitan area, Georgia
simplified_kindergarten: Hardwick is a small town. Many people live there.

simplified_secondary_school: Hardwick is a community located in Baldwin County, Georgia. The population has changed over the years, with about 3,500 residents recorded in the 2020 census. The town is built around Central State Hospital and is near Baldwin State Prison. In the 19th century, Hardwick was the original home of Oglethorpe University. It is part of the Milledgeville area and uses the zip code 31034. A notable person from this area is William Usery, Jr., who served as the U.S. Secretary of Labor.

original_text: Paper-and-pencil games or paper-and-pen games (or some variation on those terms) are games that can be played solely with paper and pencils (or other writing implements), usually without erasing. Some popular examples of pencil-and-paper games include Tic-tac-toe, Sprouts, Dots and Boxes, Hangman, M.A.S.H., Paper soccer, and Spellbinder. The term is unrelated to the use in role-playing games to differentiate tabletop games from role-playing video games. Board games where pieces are never moved or removed from the board once being played, particularly abstract strategy games like Gomoku and Connect Four, can also be played as pencil-and-paper games. External links A Zen Guide to Paper and Pen Games Pencil and Paper Games
simplified_kindergarten: You only need paper and pencils. You can play fun games!
simplified_primary_school: You only need paper and a pen to play these games. You can play Tic-tac-toe or Dots and Boxes with a friend. Most of these games do not need an eraser. You can even play some board games on paper.
simplified_secondary_school: Paper-and-pencil games are activities played using only writing tools and paper. Common examples include Tic-tac-toe and Hangman, where players rarely need to erase their marks. This category is different from tabletop role-playing games that use similar materials. Some strategy board games, like Connect Four, can also be played this way if pieces stay in one place.

original_text: Greene County is the name of fourteen counties in the United States, each named in honor of American Revolutionary War general Nathanael Greene: Greene County, Alabama Greene County, Arkansas Greene County, Georgia Greene County, Illinois Greene County, Indiana Greene County, Iowa Greene County, Mississippi Greene County, Missouri - The most populated county with the name. Greene County, New York Greene County, North Carolina Greene County, Ohio Greene County, Pennsylvania Greene County, Tennessee Greene County, Virginia Green County, Kentucky Green County, Wisconsin See also Green County (disambiguation)
simplified_kindergarten: Fourteen places have the same name. They honor a brave man.
simplified_primary_school: Fourteen places in America are named Greene County. They are named after a famous general named Nathanael Greene. Most people with this address live in Missouri.
simplified_secondary_school: There are fourteen counties in the United States named in honor of Nathanael Greene, a Revolutionary War general. While many states have a Greene County, the one in Missouri has the highest population. Other states with this county name include Alabama, Georgia, and New York.

original_text: In Norse mythology Mundilfari (Old Norse: ; rendered variously Mundilfari, Mundilföri and Mundilfœri) (Old Norse, possibly "the one moving according to particular times") is the father of Sól, goddess associated with the Sun, and Máni, associated with the Moon. Mundilfari is attested in the Poetic Edda poem Vafþrúðnismál stanza 23, and in chapter 11 of the Prose Edda book Gylfaginning. Etymology The name appears in various forms in attestations for the figure, some of them significantly different, and various theories have been proposed for the name. John Lindow states that if the first element, mundil- is related to mund, meaning "period of time," then the name may be a kenning for the Moon, as Rudolf Simek theorizes. See also Hyperion (Titan) World Mill Notes References Lindow, John (2001). Norse Mythology: A Guide to the Gods, Heroes, Rituals, and Beliefs. Oxford University Press. Simek, Rudolf (2007) translated by Angela Hall. Dictionary of Northern Mythology. D.S. Brewer Characters in Norse mythology
simplified_kindergarten: A father has two kids. They are the sun and moon.
simplified_primary_school: Mundilfari is a father in old stories. His daughter is the bright Sun. His son is the glowing Moon. He helps them move across the sky.
simplified_secondary_school: In Norse mythology, Mundilfari is the father of the Sun goddess Sól and the Moon god Máni. His name might mean someone who moves according to specific times. These stories appear in old poems and books called the Eddas. Some experts believe his name is a special description for the Moon itself.

original_text: very successful hadronization model for the production of mesons in the jets of initial quarks was proposed thirty years ago by field and feynman . the model is used so far in the design of experiments and for comparison with the experimental data . in this work it is shown that correlation function in that model , as a function of the energies of two final hadrons has interesting properties ( energies are taken in fractions of initial quark energy , and equal @xmath0 and @xmath1 , respectively ) . in particular , for some pairs of mesons there are kinematic regions over variable @xmath1 ( at fixed values of @xmath0 ) , where it is positive and unexpectedly large . the experimental investigation of the correlation functions in these kinematic regions has twofold interest , from one side it can help to investigate some delicate properties of quark jets and from the other side can help to verify the physical suppositions of model , and to obtain more precise values of parameters .
simplified_kindergarten: Small parts make big things. Scientists use a special map.
simplified_primary_school: Long ago, two men made a model about tiny particles. This model helps scientists see how small parts move together. We can use it to learn more about how things work. New tests show that these parts can act in surprising ways.
simplified_secondary_school: Thirty years ago, Field and Feynman created a model to explain how quarks produce mesons. Scientists still use this model today to design experiments and analyze data. This study shows that the energy levels of these particles have very interesting patterns. For certain pairs of mesons, the connection between them is unexpectedly strong. Studying these specific areas helps us understand the hidden properties of quark jets. It also allows researchers to check if the model is correct and improve its settings.

original_text: A drawbridge mentality is the attitude of people who have migrated to a more exclusive or more "unspoilt" community and then campaign to preserve the tranquility of that community by opposing further inward migration by people or businesses and, possibly, any development or refurbishment, including plans put forward by those already located there. The term can imply a selfish attitude and can be taken as an insult by people who have strong affection for their home locality and wish to protect it from any changes. It is closely related to the NIMBY attitude. A drawbridge was historically the hinged bridge at a castle's gates providing entry across a defensive moat. Raising the drawbridge to a vertical position was therefore one's means by which intruders could be shut out of the castle. See also BANANA Isolationism I'm alright, Jack Lifeboat ethics Portcullis Precommitment Siege mentality Splendid Isolation Tragedy of the commons References Sustainability Political terminology
simplified_kindergarten: Some people move to a nice home. They want to keep others out.
simplified_primary_school: Some people move to a quiet town and want to stay alone. They try to stop new buildings or new neighbors from coming. This is called a drawbridge mentality because it shuts others out. It can seem selfish to people who want to move there.
simplified_secondary_school: A drawbridge mentality describes people who move to a nice area and then try to stop others from joining. They often oppose new businesses or housing to keep their community exactly the same. This term comes from old castles that raised their bridges to keep strangers away. While these residents want to protect their homes, others see this attitude as selfish. It is very similar to the NIMBY movement, which stands for 'not in my backyard.'

original_text: Audio compression may refer to: Audio compression (data), a type of lossy or lossless compression in which the amount of data in a recorded waveform is reduced to differing extents for transmission respectively with or without some loss of quality, used in CD and MP3 encoding, Internet radio, and the like Dynamic range compression, also called audio level compression, in which the dynamic range, the difference between loud and quiet, of an audio waveform is reduced
simplified_kindergarten: We make music files small. We make loud sounds softer.
simplified_primary_school: Audio compression makes music files smaller so they fit on your phone. It can also make loud and quiet sounds more even. This helps us listen to music easily on the internet.
simplified_secondary_school: Audio compression can involve reducing file sizes to save space for streaming and storage. Some methods keep all the quality, while others lose some detail. It also refers to balancing volume by making the difference between loud and quiet sounds smaller.

original_text: Eugenie Bouchard's quest to match her stellar 2014 record in Grand Slam tournaments started on a positive note when she beat Anna-Lena Friedsam of Germany 6-2, 6-4 in the first round of the Australian Open. Bouchard, who reached the semi-finals at three majors last year and lost in the Wimbledon final, broke Friedsam's serve in the ninth game of the second set and held serve in the next to clinch the match when the German player hit a forehand wide. At the All England Club last year, Bouchard became the first Canadian, male or female, to reach a Grand Slam final. Eugenie Bouchard made serene progress into the second round of the Australian Open . Bouchard celebrates her straight-sets win over Anna-Lena Friedsam as she reached the next round . Bouchard has proved to be a hit with supporters after enjoyuing a stellar year on the tour in 2014 . Friedsam had no answer to Bouchard's game as she crashed out at the first hurdle in Melbourne .
simplified_kindergarten: The girl won her game. She hit the ball well.
simplified_primary_school: Eugenie Bouchard won her first big tennis match in Australia. She played very well and beat a player from Germany. Many fans cheered because she is a great star. Now she will move to the next round.
simplified_secondary_school: Eugenie Bouchard started the Australian Open with a victory against Anna-Lena Friedsam from Germany. She won the match in two sets after breaking her opponent's serve late in the game. This win follows her successful 2014 season where she reached the final at Wimbledon. Bouchard remains popular with fans as she continues her progress in this major tournament.

original_text: these are the notes for a course at the 18th brazilian school of probability held from august 3rd to 9th , 2014 in mambucaba . the aim of the course is to introduce the basic problems of non linear pdes with stochastic and irregular terms . we explain how it is possible to handle them using two main techniques : the notion of energy solutions @xcite and that of paracontrolled distributions , recently introduced in @xcite . in order to maintain a link with physical intuitions , we motivate such singular spdes via a homogenization result for a diffusion in a random potential .
simplified_kindergarten: Teachers wrote a book about math. It helps people learn new things.
simplified_primary_school: These notes are from a math class in Brazil. Students learned how to solve hard problems with random numbers. They used two main ways to find the right answers.
simplified_secondary_school: These notes come from a 2014 course in Brazil about complex math equations. The lessons teach students how to handle equations that include random or irregular parts. They use two specific methods called energy solutions and paracontrolled distributions. The course also shows how these math ideas connect to real physics.

original_text: (CNN) -- Google is well known for its elaborate campuses, offering employees extensive food choices, free haircuts, subsidized massages and plenty of ping pong. Some of the perks are built into its work spaces, such as a rock-climbing wall at its Boulder, Colorado, office or an indoor bicycle lane in Amsterdam. But overall, the tech industry has been setting the bar high for office aesthetics, using bright, open spaces and striking art. Here is a look inside -- and, in some cases, outside -- the offices of a few major technology companies.
simplified_kindergarten: Google has fun offices. They have toys and good food.
simplified_primary_school: Google has fun offices for its workers. They have free food and games like ping pong. Some offices even have climbing walls. Many tech companies use bright colors and art to make work fun.
simplified_secondary_school: Google is famous for its creative offices that offer employees free food, massages, and games. Some locations even feature unique additions like rock-climbing walls or indoor bike lanes. Many technology companies now design beautiful work spaces with bright colors and modern art. These impressive offices help set a high standard for the entire tech industry.

original_text: selected results of original numeric simulations of non - linear magnetostatic spin waves and microwave - frequency magnetic chaos in ferrite films are expounded , as third part of the work whose first two parts are recent arxive preprints 1204.0200 and 1204.2423 . especially we consider crucial role of parametric processes in creating the chaos and simultaneously obstacles to its synchronization , and examine some possibilities of good enough synchronization ( to an extent allowing its use for direct secure communication in microwave band ) .
simplified_kindergarten: Magnets make tiny waves. They move in messy ways.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists studied how magnets make messy waves. These waves move in strange patterns. The team wants to use the waves to send secret messages. This is the third part of their big project.
simplified_secondary_school: This study explains how magnets create messy waves called magnetic chaos. Researchers looked at how certain processes start this chaos and why it is hard to control. They also tested ways to sync these waves together. If they succeed, these waves could help send secure messages over microwave frequencies.

original_text: In telecommunication, a password length parameter is a basic parameter the value of which affects password strength against brute force attack and so is a contributor to computer security. One use of the password length parameters is in the expression , where is the probability that a password can be guessed in its lifetime, is the maximum lifetime a password can be used to log into a system, is the number of guesses per unit of time, and is the number of unique algorithm-generated passwords (the 'password space'). The degree of password security is determined by the probability that a password can be guessed in its lifetime. See also Key stretching Password cracking References Computer network security Password authentication
simplified_kindergarten: Long passwords keep your toys safe. Use many letters to be strong.
simplified_primary_school: A long password is very hard to guess. This helps keep your computer safe from bad people. It is important to use many letters and numbers. This makes it harder for a machine to break in.
simplified_secondary_school: In computer security, the length of a password is a key factor in its strength. Longer passwords protect systems against brute force attacks because they are much harder to guess. Security is often measured by the probability that a password can be discovered during its lifetime. Factors like the number of possible combinations and the speed of guessing attempts determine this risk. Using complex parameters ensures that unauthorized users cannot easily gain access to private data. Therefore, increasing password length is a simple way to improve overall network authentication.

original_text: we present an ab initio study of the electronic stopping power of protons in copper over a wide range of proton velocities @xmath0 where we take into account non - linear effects . time - dependent density functional theory coupled with molecular dynamics is used to study electronic excitations produced by energetic protons . a plane - wave pseudopotential scheme is employed to solve the time - dependent kohn - sham equations for a moving ion in a periodic crystal . the electronic excitations and the band structure determine the stopping power of the material and alter the interatomic forces for both channeling and off - channeling trajectories . our off - channeling results are in quantitative agreement with experiments , and at low velocity they unveil a crossover region of superlinear velocity dependence ( with a power of @xmath1 ) in the velocity range @xmath2 , which we associate to the copper crystalline electronic band structure . the results are rationalized by simple band models connecting two separate regimes . we find that the limit of electronic stopping @xmath3 is not as simple as phenomenological models suggest and it plagued by band - structure effects .
simplified_kindergarten: A tiny ball moves in metal. It hits many small things.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists studied how small particles move through copper metal. They used computers to see how the metal slows these particles down. The results matched real tests very well. They found that the metal's shape changes how the particles move.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers studied how protons lose energy when they travel through copper at different speeds. They used advanced computer models to track how electrons in the metal react to the moving protons. The study found that the copper's internal structure affects the protons more than older models suggested. Their results for random paths matched experimental data quite well. At low speeds, they discovered a unique pattern caused by the metal's electronic bands. This shows that stopping power is more complex than scientists previously thought.

original_text: (CNN) -- Real estate mogul and television celebrity Donald Trump, who generated a media publicity blitz by saying he might run for president in 2012, announced Monday that he will instead remain a businessman. "After considerable deliberation and reflection, I have decided not to pursue the office of the presidency," Trump said in a statement. With his typical bravado, Trump maintained that he would have won the Republican primary and the general election, but recognized that "running for public office cannot be done half-heartedly." RNC chairman: "It's his choice" | Was he ever serious? "Ultimately, however, business is my greatest passion and I am not ready to leave the private sector," Trump said in the statement. Trump's flirtation with running for the Republican nomination included his demand that President Barack Obama make public his full birth certificate from Hawaii. Mike Huckabee takes himself out of contention . Obama previously had provided a shorter version that serves as the public record in Hawaii, but amid the media glare on the issue raised in part by Trump's repeated questioning of whether the so-called long version of the document existed, the president released it last month.
simplified_kindergarten: A rich man is stay-ing home. He will not be pres-i-dent.
simplified_primary_school: Donald Trump is a rich man on TV. He said he might run for president. Now he says he will stay a businessman instead. He loves his job too much to leave.
simplified_secondary_school: Donald Trump announced that he will not run for president in 2012. He decided to remain a businessman because he is not ready to leave his career. Trump claimed he could have won the election if he had participated. Before this choice, he famously pressured President Obama to release his full birth certificate. Obama eventually released the document after Trump and the media questioned its existence.

original_text: Fionn (, ) is a masculine given name in Irish and Scottish Gaelic. It is derived from a byname meaning "white" or "fair-haired". It is the modern variant of Old and Middle Irish: Find and Finn. Notable people with the name include: Fionn Carr, Irish rugby union player Fionn Fitzgerald, Irish football player Fionn MacColla, Scottish novelist Fionn mac Cumhaill, figure from Irish mythology Fionn McLoughlin, Irish rugby union player Fionn O'Shea, Irish actor Fionn Regan, Irish folk musician Fionn Whitehead, English actor See also Fionn Bheinn, Scottish mountain Irish-language masculine given names Scottish Gaelic masculine given names
simplified_kindergarten: Fionn is a name for boys. It means white hair.
simplified_primary_school: Fionn is a name for boys from Ireland and Scotland. It means a person with white or fair hair. Many famous athletes and actors have this name. It comes from very old stories about heroes.
simplified_secondary_school: Fionn is a popular Irish and Scottish name for males. The name comes from an old word meaning white or fair-haired. It is a modern version of the ancient names Find and Finn. Many well-known people, including actors and rugby players, share this name. One famous example is Fionn mac Cumhaill, a hero from Irish mythology.

original_text: AAAS may refer to: American Academy of Arts and Sciences, a learned society and center for policy research; the publisher of the journal Dædalus American Association for the Advancement of Science, an organization that supports scientific collaboration, education, and outreach; the publisher of the journal Science Association for Asian American Studies, an organization that promotes teaching and research in Asian American studies Associate of the American Antiquarian Society AAAS (gene), a human gene responsible for triple-A syndrome (achalasia, adrenocortical insufficiency, alacrimia)

simplified_primary_school: AAAS is a name for many different groups. Some groups study science and others study history. One group even makes books about new ideas. Scientists also use AAAS as a name for a part of our bodies.
simplified_secondary_school: AAAS is an acronym used by several different organizations and even a specific human gene. The American Association for the Advancement of Science is well-known for publishing the famous journal called Science. Another group, the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, focuses on policy research and education. There is also an association dedicated to Asian American studies and research. Finally, AAAS refers to a gene that can cause a health condition known as triple-A syndrome.

original_text: By . Simeon Gholam . Follow @@SimGholam . Borussia Dortmund fans are well-known for their impressive vocal displays during matches, and they proved their reputation once again as they joined in a superb rendition of You'll Never Walk Alone at Anfield on Sunday. The German club were on Merseyside for a pre-season friendly, but the 1,000+ fans who made the journey were determined to make themselves heard. Having taken the Anfield anthem to their hearts in recent years, the noise before the game was sensational, and it was captured in the ground by Borussia Dortmund's official Instagram account. Unfortunately for the travelling fans, however, the result did not match the support as Dortmund went down 4-0 to a rampant Liverpool attack. Daniel Sturridge opened the scoring after 10 minutes, before Dejan Lovren netted from a corner just a few minutes later. Phillippe Coutinho and Jordan Henderson then added to the scoreline after half time, but it did not dampen the German fans spirits, who sang right through until the end.
simplified_kindergarten: The fans sang a loud song. They were very happy.
simplified_primary_school: Dortmund fans went to a big game in England. They sang a famous song very loudly before the match. Liverpool won the game with four goals. The fans stayed happy and sang until the end.
simplified_secondary_school: Borussia Dortmund fans showed their famous spirit by singing loudly during a friendly match at Anfield. Over one thousand supporters traveled to England and performed a great version of the Liverpool anthem. Despite the loud support, Liverpool won the game 4-0 with goals from Sturridge and others. However, the German fans never stopped singing even though their team lost.

original_text: Robert Pacho (August 1, 1911 – May 1, 1978) was an American professional boxer who competed from 1928 to 1941, twice challenging for the welterweight world title in 1939. After Bert Colima's career was over, Pacho was Mexican fans' most popular boxer. Early life Pacho broke into the professional boxing ranks while working as a farm mechanic in El Centro, California. Professional career Pacho fought many well known fighters during his career, including legends Barney Ross, and Henry Armstrong. His career lasted from 1929–1941 and his professional record was 78-67-15 with 37 Knockouts. His two bouts with Henry Armstrong were for the world Welterweight title, one held in Havana, Cuba. Pacho lost both by fourth round technical knockout. References External links American boxers of Mexican descent Boxers from Arizona Welterweight boxers 1911 births 1978 deaths American male boxers
simplified_kindergarten: Robert was a strong boxer. He liked to win big games.
simplified_primary_school: Robert Pacho was a famous boxer from America. He worked on a farm before he started fighting. He fought many great champions for a long time. Many fans in Mexico loved to watch him play.
simplified_secondary_school: Robert Pacho was a professional American boxer who competed between 1928 and 1941. He grew up in California and worked as a mechanic before his sports career. Pacho became very popular with Mexican fans after another famous fighter retired. During his career, he fought legends like Barney Ross and Henry Armstrong. He challenged for the world welterweight title twice in 1939. Although he lost those big fights, he finished with seventy-eight career wins.

original_text: Pavement may refer to: Pavement (architecture), an outdoor floor or superficial surface covering.. simplified_kindergarten: A road is a hard floor. People walk on it.

In geology, it describes natural rock formations that look like man-made floors. The term is also the name of an American indie rock band. Additionally, it can refer to a specific street in England or a youth magazine.

original_text: The New York Saints are a former member of the National Lacrosse League. They played at the Nassau Coliseum in Uniondale, New York, from 1989 to 2003. They became an inactive team after the 2002–03 season and were officially defunct in 2006. The Saints had played in the Major Indoor Lacrosse League from 1987 to 1988 in New Jersey (at Brendan Byrne Arena) as the New Jersey Saints. The Saints won the 1988 MILL championship. simplified_kindergarten: A sports team played with sticks. They were called the Saints.
simplified_primary_school: The New York Saints were a professional lacrosse team. They played games in New York for many years. The team won a big championship in 1988. They stopped playing in 2003 because the team closed down.
simplified_secondary_school: The New York Saints were a professional lacrosse team that competed in the National Lacrosse League. They originally started in New Jersey and won a championship title in 1988. From 1989 until 2003, the team played their home games at the Nassau Coliseum in New York. The team became inactive after the 2003 season and officially finished in 2006. They are now remembered as a former part of Long Island sports history.

original_text: In criminology the term situational offender is used in several meanings, their common denominator being nontypical character of the offense in question for the person according to some criteria. General criminology Following the classical study of Martin R. Haskell and Lewis Yablonsky Criminology - Crime and Criminality (1974), a situational offender, as opposed to a career criminal, is a person who committed a crime under certain circumstances, but normally is not inclined to commit crimes and is unlikely to repeat the offense. Sex crimes In sex crimes, a situational sex offender is one whose offense is associated with situational sexual behavior, i.e., sexual behavior different from the person's usual habits. This term is in an opposition to the preferential offender, whose offense is associated with the person's preferential behavior. For example, a preferential child molester is exclusively involved with children, whereas the situational ones are normally engaged in sexual behavior within their peer group. References Criminology
simplified_kindergarten: A good person made a mistake. They will not do it again.
simplified_primary_school: A situational offender is someone who usually follows the rules. They committed a crime because of a special situation. They are not like criminals who break the law often. They will likely stay out of trouble now.
simplified_secondary_school: In criminology, a situational offender is someone who commits a crime due to specific circumstances rather than a habit. Unlike career criminals, these individuals are not naturally inclined to break the law and are unlikely to repeat their mistakes. This term also applies to specific crimes where a person acts against their normal behavior. For example, some people might act wrongly in a unique situation even if they usually behave correctly with their peers.

original_text: A jug is a type of container commonly used to hold a liquid. original_text: The Gerald Lampert Memorial Award is made annually by the League of Canadian Poets to the best volume of poetry published by a first-time poet. Winners and nominees References External links League of Canadian Poets Canadian poetry awards First book awards Awards established in 1981 1981 establishments in Canada
simplified_kindergarten: A new writer wins a prize. They get money for their book.

original_text: Balaenicipitidae is a family of birds in the order Pelecaniformes, although it was traditionally placed in Ciconiiformes. original_text: Anab (Anav) is a city mentioned in the Bible. It was one of the cities in the mountains of Judah from which Joshua expelled the Anakim. History Anav was located at the site of today's Khirbet Anab. It lies among the hills, south-south-west of Hebron, in the West Bank. Anab is mentioned in Joshua 11:21 -- "And Joshua came at that time, and cut off the Anakim from the hill-country, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill-country of Judah, and from all the hill-country of Israel; Joshua utterly destroyed them with their cities." -- Jewish Publication Society translation (1917). Later, Joshua 15:50 lists Anab as one of a number of sites incorporated into the hilly parts of the territory of the Tribe of Judah. References Book of Joshua Hebrew Bible cities Anakim Archaeological sites in the West Bank Tribe of Judah
simplified_kindergarten: Anab is an old city. It sits on a big hill.
simplified_primary_school: Anab is a very old city in the mountains. A leader named Joshua won a battle there a long time ago. Today, the city is near a place called Hebron. It is part of a famous story in the Bible.
simplified_secondary_school: Anab is an ancient biblical city located in the mountains of Judah. According to the Book of Joshua, Joshua expelled a group called the Anakim from this area. Today, the site is known as Khirbet Anab and is situated southwest of Hebron. The Bible also lists Anab as part of the territory given to the Tribe of Judah. It remains an important site for history and archaeology in the region.

original_text: Bromide can refer to: In language: Bromide (language), a figure of speech meaning a tranquilizing phrase, cliché or platitude used as a verbal sedative In chemistry: Bromide, the anion of bromine, or any ionic salt containing bromide as the only anion, or (as a common name) any covalent compound containing bromine in the -1 oxidation state. Potassium bromide, an anticonvulsant and sedative (most pharmacologic information is here) Sodium bromide, an anticonvulsant and sedative In photography Bromide paper, paper coated with an emulsion of silver bromide, used primarily for photographic prints In Japanese culture Bromide (Japanese culture), commercial photographic portraits of celebrities including geisha, singers, actors and sports-people In geography Bromide, Oklahoma, a small village in the southern central part of the state. Bromide Basin, a basin in Utah
simplified_kindergarten: A bromide can be a word. It can also be a place.

simplified_secondary_school: The term bromide has several meanings in science, language, and culture. In chemistry, it refers to compounds used in medicine as sedatives or in photography. In language, a bromide is a boring phrase used to calm people down. It is also the name of a town in Oklahoma and a type of celebrity portrait in Japan.

original_text: (CNN) -- Investigators in the case of a missing Indiana University student hope forensic evidence will help them determine whether a decomposing body found in a creek Monday north of Indianapolis is connected with the disappearance of Lauren Spierer. Bloomington police are in contact with the Indianapolis Metropolitan police, the agency that recovered the body, but there is not enough information for police to "determine the identity or a basic description of the body," Lt. William Parker said in a statement. Parker hopes a pending forensic examination will provide more information, his statement said. CNN affiliates reported an autopsy on the female body will be performed Tuesday. Bloomington police have been searching for Spierer, 20, since June 2, when she was last seen leaving a sports bar in Bloomington after a night out with friends. In mid-June, police had found possible new clues to Spierer's disappearance -- images of a white pick-up truck caught on a security surveillance video. But they ruled out a connection and were back to square one. Officials have received thousands of tips but none so far have yielded anything concrete.
simplified_kindergarten: A girl is missing. Police want to find her.
simplified_primary_school: A girl named Lauren is missing. Police found a body in a river. They will check if it is Lauren. They are still looking for more clues.
simplified_secondary_school: Police are investigating a body found in a creek near Indianapolis. They want to see if it is Lauren Spierer, who disappeared in June. Doctors will perform an autopsy to identify the person. Investigators previously looked at video of a truck, but it did not help. They have many tips but still need more information.

original_text: (CNN) -- More than 4,000 passengers and crew were stuck on a cruise ship since an engine room fire cut power and left the ship listing four days ago. Passengers confirmed uncomfortable conditions aboard the Carnival Triumph, saying urine and feces streamed in the halls and down walls after toilet facilities failed. The smells from waste and spoiling food, as well as a lack of air conditioning, have made it a trip to remember. As the ship was slowly pulled to a dock in Mobile, Alabama, frustrated passengers shared their thoughts and stories on social media sites such as Twitter, Facebook and Instagram. You can read their stories below. . If you're unable to see the Storify page above, please click this link.
simplified_kindergarten: The big boat broke. People want to go home.
simplified_primary_school: A large ship got stuck at sea after a fire. There was no power and the toilets broke. People were unhappy because the boat was dirty and smelly. Now, big boats are pulling it to land.
simplified_secondary_school: Over 4,000 people were stuck on a cruise ship after an engine fire caused a power failure. Conditions on the ship became very difficult because the toilets stopped working and food began to spoil. Without air conditioning, passengers had to deal with bad smells and heat for four days. The ship is now being towed to a dock in Alabama. Many frustrated passengers are using social media to share their stories about the trip.

original_text: The year 618 BC was a year of the pre-Julian Roman calendar. In the Roman Empire, it was known as year 136 Ab urbe condita . The denomination 618 BC for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events King Qing of Zhou succeeds his father King Xiang of Zhou as ruler of the Chinese Zhou Dynasty. Births Deaths Duke Gong of Cao, ruler of the state of Cao References
simplified_kindergarten: Long ago, new kings were born. They ruled big lands.
simplified_primary_school: In the year 618 BC, a new king started to rule China. This happened a very long time ago in history. People used different names for the years back then. Many things changed when new leaders took over.
simplified_secondary_school: The year 618 BC was part of the Roman calendar and was also called year 136 Ab urbe condita. This specific date became common during the medieval period when the Anno Domini system spread across Europe. In China, King Qing of Zhou took the throne after his father died. During this same time, the ruler of the state of Cao passed away.

original_text: we report on a concept inventory for special relativity : the development process , data analysis methods , and results from an introductory relativity class . the relativity concept inventory tests understanding of kinematic relativistic concepts . an unusual feature is confidence testing for each question . this can provide additional information ; for example high confidence correlated with incorrect answers suggests a misconception . a novel aspect of our data analysis is the use of monte carlo simulations to determine the significance of correlations . this approach is particularly useful for small sample sizes , such as ours . our results include a gender bias that was not present in other assessment , similar to that reported for the force concept inventory .
simplified_kindergarten: Teachers made a big science test. It helps kids learn about fast light.
simplified_primary_school: Teachers made a new test about how things move very fast. Students had to say how sure they were about their answers. This helped find mistakes in how students think. The results showed that boys and girls got different scores.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers created a special test to see how students understand the rules of relativity. Students had to rate their confidence for every answer they gave. If a student is very confident but wrong, it shows they have a misunderstanding. The team used computer simulations to study the data because the class was small. Surprisingly, the results showed a difference between genders that other tests did not show.

original_text: The sixth suspect in the gang rape and killing of a 23-year-old woman is a minor and will be tried in juvenile court, police said Monday. The 17-year-old boy, whose name has not been released, was arrested by police in December and accused of taking part in the December 16 assault aboard a New Delhi bus. Police said the suspects attacked the woman and her male companion on a bus, robbed them and dumped them by the side of the road. The woman was badly injured in the attack. She died two weeks later after being flown to Singapore for treatment. The man survived. The five adult suspects are being tried in a "fast-track" court designed to bypass red tape. Hearings began last week, but details about what happens in court cannot be reported under a judge's ruling imposing restrictions on coverage of the case. The case has gripped India and led to calls for stricter laws on sexual assault and changes in cultural attitudes toward women in India, where most women have stories of sexual harassment and abuse on public transportation or on the streets, according to the Indian Council on Global Relations.
simplified_kindergarten: A bad thing happened on a bus. Police caught the people who did it.
simplified_primary_school: Six men hurt a woman on a bus in India. One boy is only seventeen so he goes to a different court. The woman died and many people are very sad and angry. They want new laws to keep women safe.
simplified_secondary_school: Police confirmed that the sixth suspect in a fatal bus attack is a minor who will face a juvenile court. The other five adults are already on trial in a special fast-track court. This tragic event caused many protests across India because people want better protection for women. The public is demanding stricter laws and a change in how women are treated in society.

original_text: Paul Alexandre René Janet (30 April 1823 – 4 October 1899) was a French philosopher and writer. Biography Born in Paris, he became professor of moral philosophy at Bourges (1845–1848) and Strasbourg (1848–1857), and of logic at the lycée Louis-le-Grand, Paris (1857–1864). In 1864 he was appointed to the chair of philosophy at the Sorbonne, and elected a member of the academy of moral and political sciences. He wrote widely on philosophy, politics and ethics, on idealistic lines: La Famille, Histoire de la philosophie dans l'antiquité et dans le temps moderne, Histoire de la science politique, Philosophie de la Revolution Française, etc. However, in the opinion of 1911 Encyclopædia Britannica, these writings are not characterised by much originality of thought. In philosophy he was a follower of Victor Cousin, and through him of G. W. F. Hegel. His principal work, Théorie de la morale, owes much to Immanuel Kant. Works The Materialism of the Present Day, a Critique of Dr. Büchner's System Notes References External links 1823 births 1899 deaths University of Bourges faculty University of Strasbourg faculty University of Paris faculty French philosophers French essayists École Normale Supérieure alumni French male essayists
simplified_kindergarten: Paul was a man from France. He wrote many books.
simplified_primary_school: Paul Janet was a famous French teacher and writer. He lived in Paris and taught at big schools. Paul wrote many books about how people should act. Some people thought his ideas were not very new.
simplified_secondary_school: Paul Janet was a French philosopher and professor who taught at the Sorbonne in Paris. He wrote many books about politics, history, and ethics. His work followed the ideas of other famous thinkers like Victor Cousin and Immanuel Kant. Although he wrote a lot, some critics felt his ideas lacked originality.

original_text: Fallen Leaf Lake, once called Dead Lake, is a lake in Camas, Washington. Once home to Dead Lake Cemetery, its graves were moved to Camas Cemetery in 1984. This property had been owned by the local Camas paper mill for decades. Most recently, the mill was owned by the Georgia-Pacific Corporation, a subsidiary of Koch Industries, Inc. In 2011, the property surrounding Fallen Leaf Lake was purchased from Koch RP Holdings I, LLC by the City of Camas, Washington for use as a city park. References Camas, Washington Lakes of Clark County, Washington Lakes of Washington (state)
simplified_kindergarten: This lake has a new name. It is a pretty park now.
simplified_primary_school: Fallen Leaf Lake is a park in Washington. It used to have a cemetery, but the graves were moved. A big paper mill owned the land for many years. Now, the city owns the lake so people can visit it.
simplified_secondary_school: Fallen Leaf Lake is located in Camas, Washington, and was once known as Dead Lake. In 1984, the graves from a local cemetery on the property were moved to a different location. A large paper mill owned the land for many decades before it was sold to Georgia-Pacific. The City of Camas eventually purchased the land in 2011. Today, the area around the lake is used as a public city park for everyone to enjoy.

original_text: In telecommunication, the Aeronautical Emergency Communications System Plan (AECS) provides for the operation of aeronautical communications stations, on a voluntary, organized basis, to provide the President and the Federal Government, as well as multiple heads of state and local governments, or their designated representatives, and the aeronautical industry with an expeditious means of communications during an emergency. References Title 47 of the Code of Federal Regulations Telecommunications in the United States Emergency communication

It ensures that the President and local leaders can stay connected during a crisis.

original_text: (CNN) -- Rubber ducky, you ARE the one -- the chosen one, that is, for this year's induction into the National Toy Hall of Fame. The rubber duck shares the honor with the game of chess as a fellow 2013 inductee, announced today on the hall's website. Inductees are selected on the basis of several factors including icon-status, longevity and innovation. Rubber duck and chess squeaked and check mated past 10 other finalists, including Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, Pac-Man and My Little Pony to win this year's honor. Toys previously inducted into the Hall of Fame include Monopoly (1998), roller Skates (1999), jack-in-the-box (2005) and last year's inductees: Star Wars action figures and dominoes. The National Toy Hall of Fame is in Rochester, New York. Which toy got your vote? Tell us on CNN Living's Facebook page, or on Twitter @CNNLiving.
simplified_kindergarten: The yellow duck won a prize. It is a famous toy.
simplified_primary_school: The rubber duck and chess won a big award today. They are now in the Toy Hall of Fame. These toys are very old and popular. They beat other toys like Pac-Man to win.
simplified_secondary_school: The rubber duck and the game of chess have been inducted into the National Toy Hall of Fame. They were chosen based on their long history and iconic status. These two winners beat other finalists like the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles and My Little Pony. Past winners of this prestigious award include Monopoly, roller skates, and Star Wars action figures. The Hall of Fame is located in Rochester, New York.

original_text: (CNN)Scarlett Johansson has become the go-to woman for action movies. After major turns in movies such as "The Avengers" and "Lucy," Variety reports that the actress won the lead role in DreamWorks' live-action version of "Ghost in the Shell." Johansson's casting lit up social media on Monday because of the hardcore fan base from the original anime film from 1995, which was based on a manga series. The original film is considered a cyberpunk classic. It's about a future society where people have cyberbrains, allowing them to plug into computer networks. A police force is assigned specifically to track down hackers, including the movie's main character, Major Motoko Kusanagi. Part science fiction, part crime drama, it has been praised for what were then advanced animation techniques. DreamWorks' Steven Spielberg has been wanting to adapt the movie for many years, and Rupert Sanders is attached to direct.
simplified_kindergarten: Scarlett is a movie star. She plays a strong hero.
simplified_primary_school: Scarlett Johansson is a famous actress in action movies. She will star in a new film about a robot police officer. Many fans are excited because the story comes from a classic cartoon. Steven Spielberg wanted to make this movie for a long time.
simplified_secondary_school: Scarlett Johansson has been cast as the lead in the live-action version of Ghost in the Shell. This movie is based on a famous 1995 anime about a future where people connect their brains to computers. Fans of the original manga and film are talking about the news on social media. The story follows a police officer who hunts hackers in a high-tech world. Director Rupert Sanders will lead the project for DreamWorks.

original_text: the success of density functional theory for the description of the adsorption of atoms on surfaces is well established , and based on recent calculations using gradient corrections , it has been shown that it also describes well the dissociative adsorption of molecules at surfaces admittedly however , the data base for reactions at surfaces is still somewhat small . in the present paper the power of density functional theory calculations is demonstrated by investigations for two different adsorption systems , namely , one with a strongly electropositive adsorbate [ na on al(111 ) ] and one with a strongly electronegative adsorbate [ o on ru(0001 ) ] . in each case , new hitherto not expected adsorbate phases have been predicted by the theory : for na on al(111 ) the stability of a `` four - layer '' surface alloy was identified while for o on ru(0001 ) it was predicted that the formation of a @xmath0-o adlayer should be possible which implies that the apparent saturation coverage of @xmath1 is due to kinetic hindering .
simplified_kindergarten: Tiny bits stick to big things. Science helps us see them.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists use math to see how tiny pieces stick to surfaces. They found new ways that oxygen and salt stick to metal. This helps us understand how things change.
simplified_secondary_school: Density functional theory is a powerful tool used to predict how atoms and molecules stick to surfaces. Researchers recently used this method to study how sodium and oxygen interact with different metals. The study discovered new patterns that scientists did not expect to find before. For example, oxygen can form thicker layers than previously thought if given enough time. These findings show that computer calculations can reveal secrets about chemistry and materials.

original_text: we present a fully automatic method for music classification , based only on compression of strings that represent the music pieces . the method uses no background knowledge about music whatsoever : it is completely general and can , without change , be used in different areas like linguistic classification and genomics . it is based on an ideal theory of the information content in individual objects ( kolmogorov complexity ) , information distance , and a universal similarity metric . experiments show that the method distinguishes reasonably well between various musical genres and can even cluster pieces by composer .
simplified_kindergarten: A computer sorts music. It is very smart.
simplified_primary_school: A computer can group music without any help from people. This new tool works for songs, books, and science. It puts music into groups by the person who wrote it.
simplified_secondary_school: Scientists created a new way for computers to organize music automatically. This method uses data compression instead of expert musical knowledge. It works for music, languages, and even DNA research. The system can successfully identify different musical genres and group songs by their composers.

original_text: England have been caught up in a fresh team-sheet embarrassment. Just three days after sponsors Vauxhall tweeted a team-sheet which included players’ passport numbers, the line-ups ahead of Saturday’s game against Honduras named the suspended Raheem Sterling among the substitutes. Blunder: The suspended Raheem Sterling was named among the subs on the team-sheet . Sidelined: The injured Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain and Sterling sat out of the game against Honduras . Seeing red: Sterling was sent off during the 2-2 draw with Ecuador on Wednesday . The Liverpool forward was sent off during the 2-2 draw with Ecuador on Wednesday night, picking up a one-game ban to be served against the Hondurans. But the official team-sheet at the Sun Life Stadium in Miami included Sterling on the bench but had no place for Jack Wilshere, despite the Arsenal midfielder warming up on the pitch. Manager Roy Hodgson has indicated that Sterling would have started the game, hinting at his involvement for the World Cup opener against Italy next weekend. Passport numbers, incidentally, were not published on the team-sheet ahead of the Honduras game. Security breach: A picture of the England team-sheet against Ecuador was tweeted which contained the players' passport details .
simplified_kindergarten: The soccer team made a mistake. They wrote the wrong names.
simplified_primary_school: England's soccer team made a mistake on their list of players. They included Raheem Sterling even though he was not allowed to play. This happened because he got a red card in the last game. The team also shared private passport numbers by mistake earlier that week.
simplified_secondary_school: The England football team faced embarrassment after making several mistakes with their official team sheets. Before a match against Honduras, they accidentally listed Raheem Sterling as a substitute. Sterling was actually suspended from the game because he received a red card during a previous match against Ecuador. This error followed a serious security breach where a sponsor tweeted a list containing the players' private passport numbers. While Sterling sat out the game, manager Roy Hodgson suggested the young player remains an important part of his future plans.

original_text: By . Associated Press . Thirty coins stamped by the U.S. Mint in the 19th century to test designs and metals have sold at auction for more than a half-million dollars. Dallas-based Heritage Auctions had expected the coins to sell Thursday night for more than $300,000. Heritage spokesman Noah Fleisher says they sold for a total of $505,440. This image provided by Heritage Auctions shows one of the historic experimental coins from the collection of the late Texas businessman and philanthropist Harry W. Bass, Jr. that will be offered by Heritage Auctions at the 2014 World's Fair of Money on Aug. 7, 2014 near Chicago. (AP Photo/Heritage Auctions) The biggest price was $76,375 for an 1871 silver dollar struck in aluminum and believed to be unique. The coins from the collection of Dallas businessman and philanthropist Harry W. Bass Jr., who died in 1998 at age 71, were sold individually at the American Numismatic Association's World's Fair of Money near Chicago. The proceeds will go to the Harry W. Bass Jr. Foundation, which helps support organizations in areas such as education and the arts.
simplified_kindergarten: People bought very old coins. They paid a lot of money.
simplified_primary_school: Some very old and rare coins were sold at a big show. People paid over half a million dollars for thirty coins. One special silver coin sold for over seventy thousand dollars. The money will help schools and art groups.
simplified_secondary_school: Thirty rare experimental coins from the 19th century recently sold at auction for over $505,000. Heritage Auctions sold the collection, which belonged to the late businessman Harry W. Bass Jr. The most expensive item was a unique 1871 silver dollar made of aluminum that cost $76,375. These historic coins were sold individually during a large money fair held near Chicago. All the money earned from the sale will go to a foundation that supports education and the arts.

original_text: Krypton is a chemical element with symbol Kr and atomic number 36. simplified_kindergarten: Krypton is a real gas. It is also Superman's home.
simplified_primary_school: Krypton is a real gas used in science. It is also the name of Superman's home planet. People use the name for TV shows, music, and computers too.
simplified_secondary_school: Krypton is a chemical element with the symbol Kr and atomic number 36. It is famously known as the fictional home planet of Superman in comic books and television. The name also refers to various things like a Russian missile, a Romanian band, and computer programming languages. Additionally, Crypton with a 'C' can mean a type of stain-resistant fabric or a Japanese media company.
