original_text: AD 46 (XLVI) was a common year starting on Saturday of the Julian calendar. At the time, it was known as the Year of the Consulship of Asiaticus and Silanus (or, less frequently, year 799 Ab urbe condita). The denomination AD 46 for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Roman Empire The settlement at Celje gets municipal rights, and is named municipium Claudia Celeia. Dobruja is annexed into Roman Moesia. A census shows that there are more than 6,000,000 Roman citizens. After the death of its king, Thracia becomes a Roman province. Rome and its northeast border are reunited by the Danube Road. According to Orosius, there is a serious famine in Syria Central Asia A drought and an invasion of locusts hit the Mongolian steppes, causing a famine and a revolt at Xiongnu. Births Plutarch, Greek historian and biographer (approximate date) Deaths Marcus Vinicius, Roman consul and governor (b. c. 5 BC) Rhoemetalces III, Roman client king of Thrace (murdered) Servius Asinius Celer, Roman politician (executed) References 0046 als:40er#46
simplified_kindergarten: Long ago, Rome was very big. Many people lived there.
simplified_primary_school: In the year 46, the Roman Empire grew larger. They counted six million citizens. There was not enough food in some places. A famous writer named Plutarch was born then.
simplified_secondary_school: The year 46 AD was an important time for the Roman Empire as it expanded its borders. New lands like Thrace and Dobruja became part of Rome. A census recorded over six million citizens living across the empire. Meanwhile, people in Syria and Central Asia suffered from terrible famines and droughts. This year also marks the approximate birth of the famous Greek historian Plutarch.

original_text: (CNN) -- A Taiwanese fishing vessel has not been heard from since reporting that pirates were attacking it in the Indian Ocean several days ago, authorities said Thursday. The ship has a crew of 26 from Taiwan, China and Vietnam, the European Union Naval Force Somalia said in a statement. On Christmas morning, the vessel FV Shiuh Fu reported being under pirate attack about 120 miles off the northeast tip of Madagascar, the statement said, but "no further communication with the vessel has been possible" since then. Authorities said they do not have information about the condition of the ship or its crew. Pirates are now holding 26 vessels and 613 hostages off the coast of Somalia, where many pirates are based, the European Union Naval Force Somalia said.
simplified_kindergarten: The big boat is lost. Bad men took the boat.
simplified_primary_school: Pirates attacked a fishing boat in the ocean on Christmas. There are twenty-six people on the ship. No one can talk to the crew now. People are worried about the ship.
simplified_secondary_school: A Taiwanese fishing vessel disappeared after reporting a pirate attack in the Indian Ocean on Christmas. The ship carried twenty-six crew members from Taiwan, China, and Vietnam. Authorities lost all contact with the vessel near Madagascar and do not know if the crew is safe. Currently, pirates near Somalia are holding over six hundred hostages and many ships.

original_text: The Prorocentrales are a small order of dinoflagellates. They are distinguished by having their two flagella inserted apically, rather than ventrally as in other groups. One flagellum extends forward and the other circles its base, and there are no flagellar grooves. This arrangement is called desmokont, in contrast to the dinokont arrangement found in other groups. Accordingly, the Prorocentrales may be called desmoflagellates, and in some classifications were treated as a separate class Desmophyceae. All members have chloroplasts and a theca, which is composed of two large plates joined by a sagittal suture. This structure is shared with the Dinophysiales, and they are probably sister groups. This does not show up on rRNA trees, which also show two separate groups of Prorocentrum, but they leave their relationships mostly unresolved. References Dinophyceae Dinoflagellate orders
simplified_kindergarten: Tiny sea bugs live in water. They have two small tails.
simplified_primary_school: These tiny sea creatures have two tails at the top. Most other tiny bugs have tails on their bellies. They have hard shells made of two parts. They also have green parts to help them make food.
simplified_secondary_school: The Prorocentrales are a unique group of tiny organisms called dinoflagellates. Unlike other groups, their two tails are located at the very top of their bodies. This special tail arrangement is known as desmokont. These organisms have green chloroplasts and a protective shell made of two large plates. Scientists believe they are closely related to another group called Dinophysiales. However, studies of their genetic material have not yet fully explained their history.

original_text: Caption may refer to: An element of comics where words appear in a separate box, see Glossary of comics terminology#Caption Caption (comics convention), a small press and independent comic convention held annually in Oxford, England Caption (law), arrest or apprehension Closed captioning, used to provide the text of a show's audio portion to those who may have trouble hearing it Subtitle (captioning), textual versions of the dialog in film and other visual media Intertitle, a piece text edited into a film to convey information like dialogue Photo caption, explanatory text about specific published photos , an HTML element, see HTML element#caption
simplified_kindergarten: Words help us read pictures. They tell us a story.

simplified_secondary_school: The word caption has several different meanings depending on the context. In comics, it refers to text in a separate box, while in movies, it provides dialogue for viewers. Captions also describe photos or serve as titles in legal documents and computer coding. There is even a famous comic book convention in England with this name.

original_text: transfer - ionization in fast collisions between a bare ion and an atom , in which one of the atomic electrons is captured by the ion whereas another one is emitted , crucially depends on dynamic electron - electron correlations . we show that in collisions with a highly charged ion a strong field of the ion has a very profound effect on the correlated channels of transfer - ionization . in particular , this field weakens electron emission into the direction opposite to the motion of the ion and strongly suppresses the emission perpendicular to this motion . instead , electron emission is redirected into those parts of the momentum space which are very weakly populated in fast collisions with low charged ions .

simplified_primary_school: Fast particles can crash into small atoms. One part gets caught while another part flies away. A strong pull changes where the parts go.
simplified_secondary_school: When a fast ion hits an atom, one electron is captured while another is released. This process depends on how the electrons interact with each other. A strong pull from the ion changes where the released electron travels. It stops the electron from moving sideways or backwards. Instead, the electron is pushed into new areas that are usually empty.

original_text: (CNN) -- Armed forces in the Democratic Republic of Congo beat back dozens of gunmen who stormed three key locations in the capital Monday. Armed men attacked the country's airport, state TV headquarters and army headquarters in Kinshasa. At least 34 assailants were killed, according to an Interior Ministry statement. The statement called on "victims of this terrorist attack ... to know that the assailants had no other purpose than to create panic and terror on the eve of celebrations so important to our culture." In response, it said that security had been beefed up in the capital. African Union Commission Chairwoman Nkosazana Dlamini-Zuma condemned the attacks, stressing the AU's commitment to support Congolese efforts to consolidate peace, security and stability.
simplified_kindergarten: Bad men tried to scare people. Brave soldiers stopped them.
simplified_primary_school: Bad men attacked a big city in the Congo on Monday. They went to the airport and the TV station. Soldiers fought them to keep the city safe. Now there are more guards to help people feel better.
simplified_secondary_school: On Monday, gunmen attacked the airport, army base, and TV headquarters in the Congo's capital. Government soldiers fought back and killed at least 34 of the attackers. Officials said the group only wanted to cause fear before a big holiday. Security has been increased in the city to protect the public. Leaders from other countries also promised to help the Congo stay peaceful and safe.

This structure helped manage the many different local communities across the northern part of the country.

original_text: A cruise ship worker groped a sleeping female passenger during an excursion to the Bahamas over the weekend, federal authorities charge. Prosecutors say the attack occurred Dec. 23, when 25-year-old Karan Seechurn used his room key to enter the woman's cabin without her knowledge or permission. She awoke to find Seechurn touching her genitalia and pushed him off. Federal prosecutors say 25-year-old Karan Seechurn, a cruise ship worker (not pictured), used his work key to enter a female passenger's cabin and groped her while she slept . The victim tried to run away, but he pulled her back —and threatened to burn down the ship if she reported what happened, court records show. Seechurn was detained early Saturday upon the ship's return from the Bahamas to its port of origin in Bayonne, New Jersey. He remains in the custody of US Marshals and is charged in Newark Federal Court with abusive sexual contact. He's represented by a public defender's office, which declined to comment. Seechurn is from Mauritius, an island in the Indian Ocean.
simplified_kindergarten: A mean man went into a room. He was very bad to a lady.
simplified_primary_school: A cruise ship worker used a key to enter a woman's room. He touched her while she was sleeping and scared her. The police caught him when the ship returned home. He is now in big trouble for his actions.
simplified_secondary_school: A cruise ship worker named Karan Seechurn is accused of attacking a passenger during a trip to the Bahamas. Authorities say he used his master key to enter a woman's cabin while she slept. When the victim woke up and tried to escape, he threatened her to keep her quiet. Police arrested him when the ship docked in New Jersey. He now faces federal charges for sexual contact and remains in custody.

original_text: (CNN) -- Seven miners were killed Saturday in an explosion at a coal mine in southwest China, state-run media reported. The blast occurred at about 2 a.m. local time at the Huangshuiba Coal Mine in the town of Yumo in the Guizhou Province, where 50 miners were working, the state-run Xinhua news agency reported, citing local officials. Rescuers pulled 45 miners out of mine, including two who later died from injuries sustained in the blast, the news agency reported. The bodies of five miners were found near the blast site, according to Xinhua. The cause of the accident was under investigation, authorities said, according to the news agency. CNN's Elwyn Lopez contributed to this report .
simplified_kindergarten: A big bang hurt seven men. They worked in a deep hole.
simplified_primary_school: Seven men died after a big blast in a coal mine. There were fifty people working underground when it happened. Most workers got out safely, but some were very hurt. Leaders are trying to find out why the blast happened.
simplified_secondary_school: Seven miners were killed following an explosion at a coal mine in southwest China on Saturday. The blast happened early in the morning while fifty people were working underground. Rescuers saved forty-five miners, but two of them died later from their injuries. Officials found five other bodies near the site of the accident. Authorities are now investigating the cause of the deadly explosion in the Guizhou Province.

original_text: pull - tabbing is an evaluation approach for functional logic computations , based on a graph transformation recently proposed , which avoids making irrevocable non - deterministic choices that would jeopardize the completeness of computations . in contrast to other approaches with this property , it does not require an upfront cloning of a possibly large portion of the choice s context . we formally define the pull - tab transformation , characterize the class of programs for which the transformation is intended , extend the computations in these programs to include the transformation , and prove the correctness of the extended computations . functional logic programming , non - determinism , graph rewriting , pull - tabbing to 0pt -4.5 in to 0pt draft we d mar 30 17:21:24 pdt 2011
simplified_kindergarten: Computers find the best way. They do not get stuck.
simplified_primary_school: Computers use a new way to solve hard puzzles. They do not make bad choices. This helps them find the right answer every time. It saves space and works fast.
simplified_secondary_school: Pull-tabbing is a new method for computers to solve complex problems without making mistakes. Older methods might choose a wrong path and fail to finish. This new approach avoids that risk without wasting computer memory. The authors explain how it works and prove that it is correct. This makes functional logic programming more efficient and reliable.

original_text: The year 549 BC was a year of the pre-Julian Roman calendar. In the Roman Empire, it was known as year 205 Ab urbe condita. The denomination 549 BC for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events By place Persia King Cyrus II (the Great) captures the Median capital of Ecbatana, conquering the nation of Media. Asia Emperor Suizei dies after a 32-year reign and is succeeded by his son Annei as emperor of Japan. Births Darius I, king of Persia (approximate date) Deaths Suizei, emperor of Japan (b. 632 BC) References
simplified_kindergarten: Long ago, a king won a city. A new leader started in Japan.
simplified_primary_school: In 549 BC, King Cyrus captured a big city called Ecbatana. He became the leader of a new land. In Japan, the old emperor died after many years. His son became the new emperor of the country.
simplified_secondary_school: The year 549 BC was an important time for many ancient leaders. In Persia, King Cyrus the Great captured the capital city of Ecbatana and conquered Media. Meanwhile, in Japan, Emperor Suizei died after ruling for thirty-two years. His son, Annei, then became the next emperor of the nation. This year is also when Darius the Great of Persia was likely born. Today, we use the name 549 BC because of calendar systems created much later.

original_text: A group of Crystal Palace supporters - known as the Holmesdale Fanatics - unveiled a banner which stated they were against a 38th game being played abroad. The fans, who congregate in the lower Holmesdale, displayed a banner during their side's Premier League game which was shown live on Sky. 'No to the 38th game abroad - English games on English soil,' read the banner. Crystal Palace supporters display 'English game on English soil' banner before their side's league clash . Premier league chief executive Richard Scudamore attended the match at Selhurst Park . Premier League chief executive Richard Scudamore attended the match at Selhurst Park which could have been one of the reasons why the group of Palace fans decided to display the banner. Palace supporters unveiled a banner before last month's Chelsea game which read 'Roman's dirty money is a disgrace that has plagued our game'. They also protested against Sky's live coverage of Transfer Deadline Day. Palace fans displayed a banner aimed at Chelsea owner Roman Abramovich in October .
simplified_kindergarten: The fans love their team. They want to play at home.

simplified_secondary_school: Crystal Palace fans displayed a banner protesting against plans to play league matches in other countries. They believe that English games should only happen on English soil. The league's chief executive was at the stadium when the fans showed their message. This group of supporters is known for using banners to protest against various issues. They previously criticized the owner of Chelsea and complained about television coverage. Their goal is to protect the traditions of their local football club.

original_text: Ernst Wetter (27 August 1877 – 10 August 1963) was a Swiss politician. He was elected to the Swiss Federal Council on 15 December 1938 and handed over office on 31 December 1943. He was affiliated to the Free Democratic Party. During his time in office he held the Department of Finance and was President of the Confederation in 1941. References External links 1877 births 1963 deaths People from Winterthur Swiss Calvinist and Reformed Christians Free Democratic Party of Switzerland politicians Members of the Federal Council (Switzerland) Finance ministers of Switzerland Members of the National Council (Switzerland)

During his career, he managed the Department of Finance and served as President of the Confederation in 1941. He was born in Winterthur and spent many years serving in the national government.

original_text: (CNN) -- A trio of rare Snow Leopard cubs have made their public debut at a zoo in Switzerland this week. The as yet unnamed triplets, born in Basel Zoo eight weeks ago, were displayed on Wednesday with their proud parents. Mayhan, the cubs' mother, was paired with father Pator in 2009 as part of a Europe-wide zoo breeding program. The pregnancy was confirmed in January this year. The zoo said the cubs, who are still being suckled by their mother, are expected to remain in Basel for their first three years but will later travel as ambassadors for their endangered species. Snow leopards are usually found in the Himalayan ranges of central Asia at altitudes of up to 6,000 meters. The species has been pushed to the brink of extinction by farmers protecting their livestock and hunters who sell its fur and body parts, which are prized by some traditional Chinese medicine practitioners. There are now an estimated 3,500 to 7,000 snow leopards left living in the wild, according to the Snow Leopard Trust.
simplified_kindergarten: Three baby cats are here. They are cute and spotted.
simplified_primary_school: Three rare snow leopard cubs were born in a Swiss zoo. They are staying with their mother and father for now. These big cats are in danger in the wild. People want to help them have more babies.
simplified_secondary_school: A zoo in Switzerland recently introduced three rare snow leopard cubs to the public. The triplets were born as part of a special breeding program to help save their species. In the wild, these cats live in high mountains but face many dangers from hunters. Experts estimate that only a few thousand snow leopards remain in their natural habitat. These cubs will stay at the zoo for three years before traveling to other locations.

Several important poets and writers were born during this year. We also remember the writers and thinkers who passed away then.

original_text: Louis Napoleon may refer to: Louis Bonaparte or Louis Napoléon Bonaparte, (1778–1846), King Louis I of Holland, brother of Napoleon I Napoléon Louis Bonaparte (1804–1831), King Louis II of Holland, second son of Louis Bonaparte (1778–1846) Napoléon Eugène, Prince Imperial or Napoléon IV, Prince Imperial, often referred to as Louis Napoléon (1856–1879), only child of Napoleon III Prince Louis Napoléon or Napoléon VI, Prince Imperial, (1914–1997), pretender to the French throne Louis-Napoleon Bonaparte or Napoleon III (1808–1873), Emperor of France 1852–1870

Because so many relatives shared these names, historians use dates to distinguish them.

original_text: Søndre Nordstrand (Southern Nordstrand) is a borough of the city of Oslo, Norway. It is the southernmost borough of Oslo, bordering Nordstrand. As of 2020 it has 39,066 inhabitants and the highest rate of immigrant population at 56%. It is the only borough of Oslo that has a majority-minority population. Neighborhoods in Søndre Nordstrand include: Holmlia Hvervenbukta Mortensrud Hauketo Prinsdal Bjørndal Klemetsrud References Boroughs of Oslo Ethnic enclaves in Norway
simplified_kindergarten: This is a place in a city. Many families live here together.

simplified_secondary_school: Søndre Nordstrand is a borough located in the southern part of Oslo, Norway. In 2020, the area had over 39,000 residents. It is unique because more than half of the population comes from immigrant backgrounds. Many different neighborhoods make up this borough, including Holmlia, Mortensrud, and Bjørndal.

original_text: By . Ben Nagle For Mailonline . Kevin Pietersen is enjoying some time away from cricket, by taking a breathtaking helicopter ride over the capital. The former England cricketer, whose international career finished in February when the ECB decided not to consider him for future selection, posted two pictures on his Instagram account to show his 78,000 followers the view. The first of which, taken next to the helicopter Pietersen flew in, shows the current Surrey player smiling before he boards his flight. The second photograph shows the view from the air, with Pietersen looking down over the Houses of Parliament, with stunning views across the rest of the capital. It is currently unclear what Pietersen's next permanent cricketing move will be, as the South African born batsman continues to 'freelance' playing 20/20 cricket around the world. Copter: Pietersen poses for a photo before boarding his helicopter flight over the capital . Stunning: The photo Pietersen posted from the skies shows an amazing view of London from above .
simplified_kindergarten: The man sees the big city. He flies in a helicopter.
simplified_primary_school: Kevin Pietersen is a famous cricket player. He took a helicopter ride over London. He shared pretty photos of the city from the sky. Kevin does not know which team he will play for next.
simplified_secondary_school: Former England cricketer Kevin Pietersen recently shared photos from a helicopter ride over London. He posted the images on Instagram to show his followers the view of Parliament. Since his international career ended, he has been playing for different teams around the world. It is still not clear where he will play permanently in the future.

simplified_primary_school: Space rocks move around black holes in a circle. They shake and bounce at certain speeds. This makes bright lights that scientists can see. These lights help us learn about stars.

These records help us understand how people mapped the world over many centuries.

original_text: Dallas (CNN) -- Authorities detained a person near the home of former President George W. Bush on Wednesday night after an incident in his Dallas-area neighborhood, a U.S. Secret Service spokesman said. The person who was detained was coming to visit a neighbor of the former president, according to Ed Donovan of the Secret Service. The person was authorized to come onto the street, Donovan said. The incident is being investigated by the Secret Service, and there was no perceived threat to the former president, according to Sgt. Warren Mitchell with the Dallas Police Department. "President and Mrs. Bush are OK and they appreciate the work of the United States Secret Service and the Dallas Police Department in responding to the incident," said Bush spokesman David Sherzer.
simplified_kindergarten: A man visited a neighbor's house. The police kept everyone safe.
simplified_primary_school: A person was stopped near George W. Bush’s house on Wednesday. They were only visiting a neighbor on the street. The police said the former president was never in danger. Mr. Bush and his wife are safe and happy.
simplified_secondary_school: Authorities detained an individual near the home of former President George W. Bush on Wednesday night. The Secret Service explained that the person was authorized to be there to visit a neighbor. Investigators confirmed there was never a threat to the former president or his family. A spokesperson said the Bushes are safe and appreciate the quick response from the police.

original_text: we identify classical solutions of a generalised group field theory model in 3 dimensions , and study the corresponding perturbations , deriving their effective dynamics . we discuss their interpretation as emergent matter fields . this allows us , on the one hand to test the proposed mechanism for emergence of matter as a phase of group field theory , and on the other hand to expose some limitations of the generalised group field theory formalism .

original_text: we propose a method for eliminating the truncation error associated with any subspace diagonalization calculation . the new method , called stochastic error correction , uses monte carlo sampling to compute the contribution of the remaining basis vectors not included in the initial diagonalization . the method is part of a new approach to computational quantum physics which combines both diagonalization and monte carlo techniques .

original_text: (CNN) -- Stargazers glimpsed a rare treat as a "blood moon" crossed the Earth's shadow Tuesday. The moon took on a devilish glow as it appeared in different phases from 2 to 4 a.m. ET. Hundreds of people from North and South America, where the blood moon was most visible, shared their images of the lunar spectacle with CNN iReport. "My dad and I have been planning to view this for many months, and it was truly a breathtaking experience," said high school student and astronomy enthusiast Ahan Malhotra from Miami. If rain or clouds obscured your lunar experience, don't fret. This episode is the first of four consecutive total lunar eclipses -- known as the tetrad -- that will occur in six-month intervals until September 2015. Unlike solar eclipses, lunar eclipses are safe to view with the naked eye and don't require special filters. Did you capture the blood moon in its best light? Share your images with CNN iReport, or upload your photos via CNN's mobile app. Top 5 tips for great astrophotography .
simplified_kindergarten: The moon turned red. It was very pretty.
simplified_primary_school: The moon looked red because of a special shadow. People in America saw it late at night. You can look at it safely without glasses. Three more red moons will happen soon.
simplified_secondary_school: People across the Americas watched a rare blood moon as it passed through Earth's shadow. This event is the first of four total lunar eclipses happening before September 2015. Many people shared photos of the red glow with CNN. Unlike solar eclipses, you can safely watch these events without using any special eye filters.

simplified_kindergarten: Air moves the Earth's center. It is hard to see.

simplified_secondary_school: Scientists studied how changes in air pressure affect the Earth's inner core. They used weather models to show that air causes the core to wobble slightly. This movement is called the Slichter mode. The team calculated the signal strength using a model of a spherical Earth. They found that the signal is currently too weak for our sensors to detect. Better instruments will be needed to measure these tiny changes in gravity.

original_text: EXIT were a performance art group during the mid-1970s. EXIT members Penny Rimbaud and Gee Vaucher later founded anarchist punk rock band Crass, adopting many of EXIT's experimental/multi media techniques into Crass' presentation. The group performed as part of the 1972 ICES festival at the Roundhouse. Rimbaud acknowledges that EXIT in turn had been involved with the Fluxus Movement (of which Yoko Ono was a member). He also states that Crass was more influenced by the avant-garde than by any rock & roll precedent. Discography EXIT The Mystic Trumpeter - Live at the Roundhouse 1972, The ICES Tapes (Exit Stencil Music Cat No. EXMO2, CD and book, 2013) References Crass Theatre companies in the United Kingdom
simplified_kindergarten: A group made art together. They played music at a big show.
simplified_primary_school: EXIT was a group that made art and music long ago. Two members later started a famous band called Crass. They used many cool ideas from their first group. They even played at a big festival in a round building.
simplified_secondary_school: EXIT was a performance art group active during the 1970s. Members Penny Rimbaud and Gee Vaucher later formed the anarchist punk band called Crass. This new band used many experimental techniques that they learned from EXIT. Rimbaud said that EXIT was connected to an art group called Fluxus. He also claimed that Crass was influenced more by modern art than by rock music. You can still find recordings of their 1972 festival performance today.

original_text: This schema, or explanatory outline for the novel Ulysses was produced by its author, James Joyce in 1920 in order to help a friend (Carlo Linati) understand the fundamental structure of the book. The schema has been split into two tables for better ease of reading. See also Gilbert schema for Ulysses Notes References External links Internet Ulysses Ulysses (novel)
simplified_kindergarten: A man wrote a list. It helps his friend read.

simplified_secondary_school: James Joyce created this outline in 1920 to help a friend understand the structure of his novel, Ulysses. The information is organized into two tables to make it easier for people to read today.

original_text: we outline a method for characterizing deviations from the properties of a standard model ( sm ) higgs boson . we apply it to current data in order to characterize up to which degree the sm higgs boson interpretation is consistent with experiment . we find that the sm higgs boson is consistent with the current data set at the @xmath0 confidence level , based on data of excess events reported by cms and atlas , which are interpreted to be related to the mass scale @xmath1 , and on published @xmath2 exclusion regions . we perform a global fit in terms of two parameters characterizing the deviation from the sm value in the gauge and fermion couplings of a higgs boson . we find two minima in the global fit and identify observables that can remove this degeneracy . an update for moriond 2012 data is included in the appendix , which finds that the sm higgs boson is now consistent with the current data set at only the @xmath3 confidence level ( which corresponds to @xmath4 tension compared to the best fit point ) .
simplified_kindergarten: Scientists look for a tiny speck. They found a very good match.

Early results showed the particle fit the model well, but later data created some tension. By looking at two specific traits, they found two possible answers. They now need more information to decide which answer is correct.

This jump helps estimate how much energy is needed to pin tiny magnetic circles. This pinning energy disappears at certain temperatures because of heat movements.

simplified_kindergarten: Scientists look at tiny fast things. They use a special math tool.

original_text: East Side, West Side: Tales of New York Sporting Life 1910–1960 () is a sports book written by Lawrence S. Ritter and published in 1998. It is a Total Sports production and was distributed by Andrews McNeel. The book centers, as its title suggests, on popular sports sites and figures of New York between 1910 and 1960. Among the personalities the book talks about in depth are Babe Ruth, Lou Gehrig, Joe Louis, Primo Carnera, Tony Canzoneri, Sugar Ray Robinson, Casey Stengel, Mickey Mantle, Joe DiMaggio, Lou Stillman, Jacob Ruppert and more. Sports covered in depth are baseball, basketball, boxing, hockey and horse racing, all of which were popular sports in New York during the 50 years covered by the book. 1992 non-fiction books Books about sports Sports in New York City
simplified_kindergarten: This book shows old New York. It has stories about fun games.
simplified_primary_school: This book is about sports in New York long ago. It tells stories about famous players like Babe Ruth. You can learn about baseball, boxing, and hockey. It was written by Lawrence Ritter in 1998.
simplified_secondary_school: East Side, West Side is a sports book by Lawrence Ritter about New York City. It focuses on the most popular sports and athletes between 1910 and 1960. The book includes detailed stories about famous stars like Babe Ruth and Joe DiMaggio. It covers many sports including baseball, boxing, and horse racing. This non-fiction work was published in 1998 to celebrate the city's rich athletic history.

original_text: Beijing (CNN) -- A van carrying flammable liquid rammed into a bus in China early Saturday, sparking an explosion that left 43 people dead, state media reported. Both drivers were among the fatalities in the accident in Hunan Province, according to Xinhua news agency. Another person in the van was also killed. Six people were injured, four seriously, according to state-run CCTV. The fate of the other passengers is unclear. The bus had a capacity of 53 people, Xinhua said. The accident destroyed five cars. Authorities extinguished the fire, but a rescue effort is under way. CCTV also reported that the bus operator was blacklisted by local authorities for violating safety rules.
simplified_kindergarten: Two big cars crashed. It made a big fire.
simplified_primary_school: A van hit a bus in China on Saturday. The crash made a big fire and many people died. Six people are hurt in the hospital now. Rescue workers are trying to help everyone.
simplified_secondary_school: A van carrying dangerous liquid crashed into a bus in China, causing a deadly explosion. At least 43 people died in the accident, including both drivers and a passenger in the van. Six people were injured, and emergency teams are still working at the scene. Experts found that the bus company had previously broken safety rules.

original_text: (CNN) -- A knife-wielding man attacked a woman in a supermarket, eventually cutting off her head and running away with it, government officials in Spain's Tenerife Island said Friday. The man entered a shopping center in the town of Arona, in the Cristianos area of Tenerife, and stole a knife from the supermarket before attacking the apparent stranger, a central government spokeswoman said. Shopping center security guards were alerted and chased the man, subduing him until police arrived. The man, whom national police in Tenerife identified as a Bulgarian, was apparently known in the shopping area, the government spokeswoman said. The British Foreign and Commonwealth office confirmed Friday that a British national had died in Tenerife, part of the Canary Islands. CNN's Al Goodman, Per Nyberg and Bharati Naik contributed to this report.
simplified_kindergarten: A bad man hurt a woman. The police caught him quickly.
simplified_primary_school: A man attacked a woman in a store with a knife. He tried to run away, but security guards caught him. The police say the man is from Bulgaria. The woman who died was from Britain.
simplified_secondary_school: A Bulgarian man attacked and killed a woman inside a supermarket on Tenerife Island. He stole a knife from the store and tried to escape after the attack. Security guards chased the man and held him until the police arrived. Officials confirmed that the victim was a British citizen visiting the island.

original_text: Lappi is a cheese made from partially skimmed cow's milk, very similar to Emmental except that it is pasteurized, and so is a little less flavorful, with smaller holes and a slightly firmer texture. It is so named because its recipe was developed in the Lapland region of Finland. See also List of cheeses External links Cook's Thesaurus: Semi-Soft Cheeses Finnish cheeses Cow's-milk cheeses
simplified_kindergarten: Lappi is a yellow cheese. It comes from a cold place.
simplified_primary_school: Lappi is a cheese from the cold Lapland region in Finland. It is made from cow's milk. It looks like Emmental but has smaller holes. This cheese is firm and tastes very good.
simplified_secondary_school: Lappi is a Finnish cheese that originated in the Lapland region. It is produced using partially skimmed cow's milk and is similar to Emmental cheese. Because the milk is pasteurized, it has a milder flavor and a firmer texture. You will also notice that it has smaller holes than typical Swiss cheeses.

original_text: Sloanbaataridae is a family of fossil mammals within the extinct order Multituberculata. Remains are known from the Upper Cretaceous of Mongolia. These small herbivores lived during the "age of the dinosaurs". This family is part of the suborder Cimolodonta. The family Sloanbaataridae was named by Kielan-Jaworowska, Z. in 1974. References Kielan-Jaworowska (1974), "Multituberculate succession in the Late Cretaceous of the Gobi Desert (Mongolia)", Palaeontologica Polonica, 30, p. 23-44. Kielan-Jaworowska, Z. & Hurum, J.H. (2001), "Phylogeny and Systematics of multituberculate mammals", Paleontology 44, p. 389-429. Much of this information has been derived from Mesozoic Mammals: Djadochtatherioidea, an Internet directory. Cimolodonts Late Cretaceous first appearances Late Cretaceous extinctions Late Cretaceous taxonomic families Prehistoric mammal families
simplified_kindergarten: Small furry animals lived long ago. They ate plants near big dinosaurs.
simplified_primary_school: This is a family of small animals from long ago. They lived in Mongolia when dinosaurs were alive. These animals ate plants to stay healthy. A scientist named the group in 1974.
simplified_secondary_school: Sloanbaataridae is an extinct family of small mammals that lived in Mongolia. These herbivores existed during the Late Cretaceous period alongside the dinosaurs. They belong to a larger group of mammals called multituberculates. A scientist named Zofia Kielan-Jaworowska first described this family in 1974. Most of our knowledge comes from fossils found in the Gobi Desert. Researchers use these old bones to understand how early mammals evolved and lived.

simplified_secondary_school: Graphical functions are a type of math used to study how particles interact. Researchers use these functions to calculate values called Feynman periods. They found that certain math families can be written as simple combinations of numbers. An algorithm was created so computers can solve these complex problems automatically. This theory was recently used to prove an important idea called the zig-zag conjecture.

simplified_kindergarten: Big stars eat and glow. They make bright light.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists studied small and big black holes in space. They found that black holes make radio waves and bright light. The size of the black hole changes how much light it makes. This helps us understand how all black holes work.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers studied the connection between light and energy in both small and giant black holes. They collected data from many galaxies to compare radio waves and X-ray brightness. The study found that a black hole's mass and its energy output are closely linked. This information helps scientists create a single theory to explain how different black holes behave.

original_text: By . Simon Jones . Newcastle are hopeful of closing a deal for France international striker Bafetimbi Gomis. Owner Mike Ashley and managing director Lee Charnley have been in negotiations with Gomis' representatives and it is understood only minor details need to be ironed out to conclude the transfer - which they hope can be completed on Monday. Galatasaray have also shown an interest in the 28-year-old former Lyon forward who is a free agent but was looking for wages of around £100,000 a week. Goalscorer: Newcastle are closing in on the free transfer of striker Bafetimbi Gomis from Lyon . Chase: Newcastle look to have won the race to sign Gomis (right) ahead of Turkish club Galtasaray . Marseille want to sign Newcastle defender Mapou Yanga-Mbiwa and will make an opening offer this week for the 25-year-old who cost £7.5million from Montpellier. Wanted man: Toon defender Mapou Yanga-Mbiwa (left) is a transfer target for Marseille .
simplified_kindergarten: The team wants a new player. He is a fast runner.
simplified_primary_school: Newcastle wants to sign a new player named Gomis soon. Another team wants him too, but Newcastle is winning the race. A different player named Mapou might leave to join a team in France. These big changes could happen this week.
simplified_secondary_school: Newcastle United is close to signing French striker Bafetimbi Gomis on a free transfer. Club leaders have been negotiating with his agents and hope to finalize the deal by Monday. Although Galatasaray was also interested in the former Lyon forward, Newcastle appears to have won the race. Meanwhile, Marseille is preparing an offer to sign Newcastle defender Mapou Yanga-Mbiwa. The French club is expected to start bidding for the 25-year-old later this week.

original_text: Ahikam (Hebrew אחיקם, "My brother has risen") was one of the five whom, according to the Hebrew Bible, Josiah sent to consult the prophetess Huldah in connection with the discovery of the book of the law. Biblical accounts He was the son of Shaphan, the royal secretary, and the father of Gedaliah, governor of Judea after the destruction of Jerusalem by the Babylonians. On one occasion, in a move described by Jonathan Magonet as taking the prophet into 'protective custody', he protected Jeremiah against the fury of Jehoiakim (Jeremiah 26:24) It was in the chamber of another son (Gemariah) of Shaphan that Baruch read Jeremiah's scroll in the hearing of all the people. References Hebrew Bible people
simplified_kindergarten: Ahikam was a kind man. He helped a good friend stay safe.
simplified_primary_school: Ahikam was a helper for King Josiah in the Bible. He was the son of a royal worker. He kept a prophet named Jeremiah safe from a mean king. His son became a leader for the people later.
simplified_secondary_school: Ahikam was an important official who served King Josiah according to the Hebrew Bible. He was one of the men sent to visit the prophetess Huldah after a holy book was found. As the son of Shaphan, he came from a family of royal secretaries. Ahikam is best known for protecting the prophet Jeremiah from the anger of King Jehoiakim. His son, Gedaliah, eventually became the governor of Judea after Jerusalem was destroyed by the Babylonians.

original_text: (CNN) -- Scratch Christian Bale off the list of actors supposed to play Steve Jobs. Bale was rumored to be the finalist to play the Apple co-founder in the Aaron Sorkin-written film biography but decided he wasn't right for the part, said The Hollywood Reporter. The Jobs biopic has been in the works since at least 2012, when Sony announced that Sorkin would write a film based on Walter Isaacson's best-selling biography. Bale is the second notable performer to turn down the Jobs role. Leonardo DiCaprio was also pegged for the film. The Hollywood Reporter noted that neither Bale nor DiCaprio had begun negotiations. According to Variety, Seth Rogen is being talked about for the part of Steve Wozniak, Jobs' Apple co-founder. The film is to be directed by Danny Boyle, who won an Oscar for "Slumdog Millionaire." Filming was scheduled to begin this winter.
simplified_kindergarten: A man will not be in a movie. He is not the right person.
simplified_primary_school: Christian Bale will not play Steve Jobs in a new movie. He decided he was not right for the part. This is the second famous actor to say no. Now, the filmmakers must find someone else to be the star.
simplified_secondary_school: Christian Bale has decided not to play Steve Jobs in the upcoming film written by Aaron Sorkin. Although he was a top choice, Bale felt he was not right for the role. He is the second major actor to decline the part after Leonardo DiCaprio. The movie is based on a famous biography and will be directed by Danny Boyle. Other actors like Seth Rogen might still join the cast as Steve Wozniak. Filming was supposed to start this winter, but the lead role remains empty.

simplified_kindergarten: A location is a place. It is where things stay.
simplified_primary_school: A location is a specific place on a map. People use this word for movie sets and song titles. It can also mean where you put your hands to sign.
simplified_secondary_school: A location is a fixed point on Earth, but the word has many meanings. In the arts, it refers to popular songs or places where movies are filmed. It is also used in science and math to describe where things are placed or shifted.

original_text: Porgy is a novel written by the American author DuBose Heyward and published by the George H. Doran Company in 1925. The novel tells the story of Porgy, a crippled street beggar living in the black tenements of Charleston, South Carolina, in the 1920s. The character was based on Charlestonian Samuel Smalls. In some of the novel's passages, black characters speak in Gullah, a creole language that had developed among enslaved African Americans during the slavery years on the Sea Islands. The novel was adapted for a 1927 play of the same name by Heyward and his wife, playwright Dorothy Heyward. Even before completing the play, Heyward was in discussions with composer George Gershwin for an operatic version of his novel. This was produced in 1935 as Porgy and Bess (renamed to distinguish it from the play). References External links Full text of Porgy, plus criticism and commentary by Kendra Hamilton, hosted by the University of Virginia 1925 American novels American novels adapted into plays Novels set in South Carolina Porgy and Bess
simplified_kindergarten: Porgy is a man in a book. He lives in a big house.
simplified_primary_school: Porgy is a famous book about a man who lives in South Carolina. He is a beggar who lives in a poor neighborhood. Many people liked the story, so it became a play and a famous opera. The author based the main character on a real person named Samuel.
simplified_secondary_school: Published in 1925, Porgy is a novel by DuBose Heyward about a disabled beggar living in Charleston. The story is based on a real man named Samuel Smalls and features characters who speak a local language called Gullah. Heyward and his wife later turned the book into a successful stage play. Eventually, Heyward worked with George Gershwin to create the famous opera known as Porgy and Bess.

original_text: In communication, a code word is an element of a standardized code or protocol. Each code word is assembled in accordance with the specific rules of the code and assigned a unique meaning. Code words are typically used for reasons of reliability, clarity, brevity, or secrecy. See also Coded set Commercial code (communications) Compartmentalization (information security) Duress code Error correction and detection Marine VHF radio Password Safeword Spelling alphabet References UNHCR Procedure for Radio Communication External links UNHCR Procedure for Radio Communication Data transmission Cryptography
simplified_kindergarten: A secret word helps friends talk. It has a special meaning.
simplified_primary_school: A code word is a special word with a secret meaning. It helps people send messages clearly and quickly. People use these words to keep their information safe.
simplified_secondary_school: In communication, a code word is a specific element used within a standardized system. Each word follows certain rules and has a unique meaning for the users. These words are helpful because they make messages shorter, clearer, and more secure. People use them in radio calls, passwords, and data transmission to prevent errors and keep secrets.

original_text: (CNN) -- An armed group in strife-torn eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo has kidnapped eight international Red Cross staffers -- seven Congolese and a Swiss delegate -- the aid group said Tuesday. The eight International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) personnel, who had just completed an assessment of displaced people in the Fizi area, have been held since April 9 by Mai Mai Yakutumba, a militia operating in South Kivu province. "We demand that the group holding our personnel release them as quickly as possible," said Franz Rauchenstein, head of the ICRC delegation in the Congo. "We are in contact with our colleagues, and their families have been briefed on the situation." Violent ethnic clashes in the South Kivu, related to the 1994 genocide in neighboring Rwanda, have forced thousands of civilians from their homes in recent months, the ICRC said. "It is in order to protect and assist armed-conflict victims that we have been carrying out our activities in the area," Rauchenstein said. "We continue to insist that the strictly neutral, impartial and humanitarian nature of our work be recognized, and that our colleagues be able to return to their loved ones soon."
simplified_kindergarten: Bad men took some helpers. We want them back home.
simplified_primary_school: Eight helpers were taken by a group in the Congo. These people were there to help families after a war. Their boss wants them to come home soon. Their families know about the problem and are waiting.
simplified_secondary_school: Eight Red Cross workers were kidnapped by a militia group in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The group includes seven local workers and one Swiss member who were helping people displaced by war. The Red Cross is currently in contact with the workers and has updated their families. Leaders are demanding their quick release so they can return home safely. The organization remains neutral and only wants to assist victims of violent ethnic clashes in the region.

original_text: Middlesbrough hope to sign Tomas Kalas on loan from Chelsea when he returns from his spell with Cologne this week. The 21-year-old centre-back has been a target for QPR also but is interested in joining up with Aitor Karanka at Boro as they push for a place in the Premier League. Chelsea have a good relationship with Middlesbrough where former chief executive Peter Kenyon is helping to advise with transfers. Tomas Kalas will return from Cologne this week and Middlesbrough hope to sign him on loan . The Czech defender made his debut for Chelsea during the 2-0 win against Liverpool at Anfield last season . Striker Patrick Bamford is already on loan at Boro for the season as is Kenneth Omeruo, who featured at the World Cup with Nigeria last summer. Kalas famously made his Premier League debut for Chelsea in the 2-0 win against Liverpool at Anfield last season, a result that dealt a major blow to the Reds' title aspirations. But a lack of playing time in the Bundesliga could see the Czech Republic international return to England prematurely.
simplified_kindergarten: The soccer player wants a new team. He will play for a new coach.

He previously helped Chelsea win a big game against Liverpool last season. Other Chelsea players are already at Middlesbrough because the two clubs have a good relationship. Kalas wants to work with coach Aitor Karanka to help the team reach the Premier League.

original_text: Former head teacher James Stewart has been arrested over £40,000 of expenses claims . The former head of a failing school has been arrested over £40,000 of expenses claims – including a wine club subscription and Virgin Media package for his home. James Stewart, who made £120,000 a year as the academy's principal, was quizzed by police after a Government watchdog said he received £39,026 of 'irregular' payments. It said the sum included £25,544 spent on 'alcohol, hospitality, shopping, gift cards, home appliances/furnishings'. Of this, £3,593.62 was 'directly attributable' to alcohol, including the wine club subscriptions. Investigators said the 'potentially irregular expenditure' also included £2,934.84 in direct debits to mobile phone companies and to Virgin Media for a home package. Mr Stewart, 69, quit as head of Sawtry Community College in Huntingdon, Cambridgeshire, in the summer after a critical Ofsted report which put the school in special measures. He did not respond last night to a request for comment. Mr Stewart, 69, quit as head of Sawtry Community College (pictured) in Huntingdon, Cambridgeshire, in the summer after a critical Ofsted report which put the school in special measures . Sorry we are not currently accepting comments on this article.
simplified_kindergarten: A school leader took money. He must talk to the police.
simplified_primary_school: A principal named James Stewart was arrested by the police. He used school money to buy things for his own home. He bought wine and paid for his home television. He left the school because it was not doing well.
simplified_secondary_school: James Stewart, a former head teacher, was arrested for spending £40,000 of school funds on personal items. A government watchdog discovered that he used the money for a wine club and home media packages. He previously earned £120,000 a year before resigning from his school in Cambridgeshire. Investigators found that thousands of pounds were spent on alcohol, shopping, and home appliances. Mr. Stewart left the college after it received a very poor official report. He has not commented on the police investigation regarding these irregular payments.

original_text: Brizo (Greek: Βριζώ; derived from Ancient Greek word βρίζω meaning "to slumber") is an ancient Greek goddess who was known as the protector of mariners, sailors, and fishermen. She was worshipped primarily by the women of Delos, who set out food offerings in small boats. Brizo was also known as a prophet specializing in the interpretation of dreams. References Greek sea goddesses Mythological Greek seers Oracular goddesses

She was also a prophet who could explain the meaning of dreams. Her name comes from an old word that means to slumber.

original_text: By . Sam Turner . The Jamie Osborne-trained Toast Of New York will bid to become just that when he attempts to win the inaugural running of the US$1.25 million (£730,000) Belmont Derby on Saturday night. The English raider, who sprung to prominence with two huge margin wins at Wolverhampton last winter, announced himself on the world stage in March when running away with the Group 2 UAE Derby in Dubai for rider Jamie Spencer. Installed as 3-1 favourite on the morning line in the States, Toast Of New York has delighted Osborne since crossing the Atlantic. Drink to that: England raider Toast of New York aims to repeat his winning feat in Dubai at the Belmont Derby . 'He looks fantastic and he’s been eating and drinking well since shipping to New York,' he said. 'We’re going slightly into the unknown with him since his best races have come on synthetic surfaces but he trains well on turf so I don’t foresee it being a problem.' Toast Of New York will be joined in the field by the Aidan O’Brien-trained Adelaide.
simplified_kindergarten: The fast horse wants to win. He is very happy today.
simplified_primary_school: A fast horse named Toast Of New York is in a race. He won a big race in Dubai before. Now he is in New York to win again. His trainer says he looks great and feels healthy.
simplified_secondary_school: A horse named Toast Of New York is competing in the Belmont Derby this Saturday. After winning a major race in Dubai, he is now the favorite to win in America. His trainer, Jamie Osborne, says the horse has traveled well and is eating healthily. Although he usually races on different ground, his team believes he will perform well on grass. Another famous horse named Adelaide will also compete in the race.

original_text: motion camouflage is a stealth strategy observed in nature . we formulate the problem as a feedback system for particles moving at constant speed , and define what it means for the system to be in a state of motion camouflage . ( here we focus on the planar setting , although the results can be generalized to three - dimensional motion . ) we propose a biologically plausible feedback law , and use a high - gain limit to prove accessibility of a motion camouflage state in finite time . we discuss connections to work in missile guidance . we also present simulation results to explore the performance of the motion camouflage feedback law for a variety of settings .

Computer tests show that this strategy works well in many different situations.

original_text: Subsonic may refer to: Motion through a medium Any speed lower than the speed of sound within a sound-propagating medium Subsonic aircraft, a flying machine that flies at air speeds lower than the speed of sound Subsonic ammunition, a type of bullet designed to avoid creating a loud shockwave when fired Subsonic flight, an aircraft flight at air speeds lower than the speed of sound in air Subsonic and transonic wind tunnels Other uses Subsonic (EP), an EP by Younha Subsonic (album), an album by Vigleik Storaas Trio See also Infrasound, sound at frequencies below the normal threshold of human hearing

original_text: Castroreale (Sicilian: Castruriali) is a village in the Metropolitan City of Messina, Sicily, southern Italy. It has around 2,702 inhabitants but over 80 churches, with some houses dating to the 13th century. It is from Barcellona Pozzo di Gotto and from Messina. It has a tower, last remain of a castle, built by Frederick II of Aragon in 1324. The name Castroreale comes from Latin, and means "royal fortress". People Giuseppina Vadalà Pina Menichelli (1890–1984) Sources Municipalities of the Metropolitan City of Messina

original_text: Grootegast (; ) is a village and former municipality in the northeastern Netherlands. It is the sister city of Kingston, Tasmania. The municipality was merged into the municipality of Westerkwartier on 1 January 2019. Geography The former municipality contained the population centres: Doezum, Enumatil, Faan, Grootegast, Kornhorn, Lutjegast, Niekerk, Oldekerk, Opende, Sebaldeburen. In some of these villages they still speak the West Frisian language. The village of Grootegast Grootegast was the main village of the former municipality. The name of the village refers to a 'gast' or 'gaast', a higher, sandy ridge in an otherwise swampy area. Groot is Dutch for 'large'. Grootegast is the birthplace of the theologian Cornelius Van Til. Lutjegast Lutjegast is a village in the former municipality of Grootegast. In the Groningen dialect of Low German 'Lutje' means small or little. Lutjegast was the birthplace of the explorer Abel Tasman. Although the house of his birth no longer exists, he is memorialised with a monument, plaque and street name. Until 1829 a mansion named Rikkerdaborg stood in Lutjegast. References External links Westerkwartier (municipality) Former municipalities of Groningen (province) Populated places in Groningen (province) Municipalities of the Netherlands disestablished in 2019
simplified_kindergarten: Grootegast is a big village. It sits on high sand.
simplified_primary_school: Grootegast is a village in the Netherlands. It was once its own municipality. The name means large sand ridge. Some people there still speak a special language called West Frisian.
simplified_secondary_school: Grootegast is a village located in the northeastern Netherlands. It was a separate municipality until it merged with Westerkwartier in 2019. The name refers to a large sandy ridge found in a swampy area. Several nearby villages still speak the West Frisian language today. Famous people from this area include the theologian Cornelius Van Til. The explorer Abel Tasman was born in the small village of Lutjegast nearby. Tasman is still remembered there with a monument and a street name.

original_text: (CNN) -- Police in Malaysia are holding 11 people on suspicion of involvement in a militant group responsible for planning acts of terror, police spokeswoman Asmawati Ahmad said Sunday. There is no indication that the group, arrested last week, has any connection to the missing Malaysia Airlines Flight 370, she said. The detainees are also suspected of having links to terrorists in other countries. Police plan to detain other suspects as a preemptive measure, Asmawati said. The arrests were made in the city of Selangor, just outside the capital Kuala Lumpur, and in the state of Kedah, which borders on Thailand. The neighboring country has closed and closely monitored the border with that region of Malaysia in a crackdown on terrorism, according to the CIA World Factbook. The violence in Thailand stems from ethnic Malay Muslim separatists. This year, they began targeting Thai Buddhist woman, killing them and mutilating their bodies, Human Rights Watch has reported. CNN's Mitra Mobasherat contributed to this report .
simplified_kindergarten: Police caught bad people. They want to keep us safe.
simplified_primary_school: Police in Malaysia caught eleven people last week. These people were planning to hurt others. They are not linked to the missing plane. The police want to stop more bad things from happening.
simplified_secondary_school: Police in Malaysia have arrested eleven people suspected of belonging to a militant group. These individuals were allegedly planning acts of terror and have links to international terrorists. Officials stated there is no connection between this group and the missing Malaysia Airlines flight. Arrests occurred near the capital and close to the border of Thailand. Thailand has increased border security to help stop these violent groups. This crackdown follows reports of recent attacks targeting people in the region.

original_text: Gwenllian (or Gwenllïan) (Welsh, a combination of gwen "fair, blessed, white" and llian "flaxen") was the name of several ladies who lived in medieval Wales. The two best known have, for different reasons, become symbols of Welsh patriotism and/or independence. It is also the name of a song by prominent Welsh songwriter Meic Stevens and Anglesey rock band Calfari. Gwenllian ferch Gruffydd (1097–1136) Her patriotic revolt and subsequent death in battle at Kidwelly Castle contributed to the Great Revolt of 1136 Gwenllian ferch Llywelyn (1282–1337) The only child of Llywelyn ap Gruffudd, the last native Prince of Wales Welsh feminine given names
simplified_kindergarten: Gwenllian was a brave lady. She loved her home.
simplified_primary_school: Gwenllian is a famous name from old Wales. One Gwenllian was a brave leader in a big battle. Another was the daughter of the last Welsh prince. Today, people write songs about them because they are heroes.
simplified_secondary_school: Gwenllian is a traditional Welsh name meaning fair or blessed. Two historical women with this name became important symbols of Welsh independence. Gwenllian ferch Gruffydd died leading a revolt at Kidwelly Castle in 1136. Another Gwenllian was the only child of the last native Prince of Wales. Their legacies inspired modern music by artists like Meic Stevens.

original_text: (CNN) -- A 25-foot dead whale washed ashore on New York's Jones Beach Island on Thursday morning, a New York State Parks official said. George Gorman, director of recreational services for New York State Parks, told CNN that officials believe it's a humpback whale. Jones Beach Island is a barrier island bordering the south of Long Island and a popular recreational spot for New Yorkers. The New York State Marine Mammal and Sea Turtle Stranding Program -- a branch of the Riverhead Foundation for Marine Research and Preservation that rescues and rehabilitates marine animals -- was called to examine the whale and remove it from the beach. Riverhead officials estimate that the animal weighs 20 to 25 tons. The group is moving the animal out of the path of the incoming tide with a tractor. An necropsy will be performed Friday and then officials will determine where to bury the remains.
simplified_kindergarten: A big whale is on the sand. It is very heavy.
simplified_primary_school: A big dead whale washed up on a New York beach. It is a humpback whale that weighs many tons. Workers used a tractor to move it away from the water. Doctors will study the whale to see why it died.
simplified_secondary_school: A 25-foot humpback whale was found dead on Jones Beach in New York on Thursday morning. State officials believe the animal weighs between 20 and 25 tons. Experts from a rescue group used a tractor to move the whale away from the tide. They will perform an exam on Friday to learn more before burying the remains.

original_text: Achall, daughter of Cairbre Nia Fer, king of Tara, and his wife Fedelm Noíchrothach, is a minor character from the Ulster Cycle of Irish mythology. After her brother Erc was killed by Conall Cernach, she died of grief on a hill near Tara, which was named Achall after her. The legendary High King of Ireland Túathal Techtmar is said to have taken power after defeating the previous High King, Elim mac Conrach, in battle on the hill of Achall. According to The Expulsion of the Déisi, another legendary High King, Cormac mac Airt, lived on the hill of Achall after he lost an eye, his physical imperfection meaning he could no longer rule at Tara. The hill is now known as Skryne. References Ulster Cycle
simplified_kindergarten: A girl loved her brother. She felt very sad.
simplified_primary_school: Achall was a princess from a famous Irish story. She was very sad because her brother died. She died on a hill that people named after her. This hill is now called Skryne.
simplified_secondary_school: Achall was a character in Irish mythology and the daughter of a king. After her brother was killed, she died of grief on a hill near Tara. This hill was named Achall in her memory, though it is now known as Skryne. Many famous High Kings fought battles or lived on this hill during their reigns. For example, Cormac mac Airt lived there because he was no longer allowed to rule at Tara.

original_text: By . David Kent . Southampton have rejected a £3million bid from Cardiff for defender Jose Fonte. Saints have already sold Adam Lallana, Luke Shaw and Rickie Lambert this summer and are not keen on selling any more of their stars until new manager Ronald Koeman has settled in. Fonte moved to St Mary's from Crystal Palace in 2010 and has helped the south coast club rise from League One to the Premier League. Wanted man: Southampton have rejected a £3million bid from Cardiff for defender Jose Fonte . Getting forward: Fonte scores against Hull in the Premier League last season . He made 36 top-flight appearances last season and Bluebirds boss Ole Gunnar Solskjaer sees him as the perfect man to help his side bounce back from relegation. Cardiff could be without Steven Caulker next season after QPR manager Harry Redknapp confirmed that his club have agreed a fee for the England international. Fonte's defensive partner, Dejan Lovren, is also in demand after Southampton rejected a second bid of £15million plus add-ons from Liverpool. Centre of attention: Fonte (middle) features in an advert for Saints' new home kit along with Jay Rodriguez (left) and James Ward-Prowse (right)
simplified_kindergarten: The soccer team said no. The player must stay.
simplified_primary_school: Southampton did not sell their player Jose Fonte to Cardiff. The team already sold many players this summer. The new manager wants to keep his best stars. Fonte is a good player who helps the team win.
simplified_secondary_school: Southampton rejected a three million pound offer from Cardiff for defender Jose Fonte. The club has already sold several star players this summer and wants to stop more departures. New manager Ronald Koeman needs time to settle in before losing more talent. Fonte has been a key player since joining the team in 2010. Cardiff manager Ole Gunnar Solskjaer wants Fonte to help his team after their recent relegation. Meanwhile, other Southampton defenders like Dejan Lovren are also attracting expensive offers from big clubs.

original_text: fast radio bursts ( frbs ) are millisecond - duration radio signals occurring at cosmological distances . however the physical model of frbs is mystery , many models have been proposed . here we study the frequency distributions of peak flux , fluence , duration and waiting time for the repeating frb 121102 . the cumulative distributions of peak flux , fluence and duration show power - law forms . the waiting time distribution also shows power - law distribution , and is consistent with a non - stationary poisson process . these distributions are similar as those of soft gamma repeaters ( sgrs ) . we also use the statistical results to test the proposed models for frbs . these distributions are consistent with the predictions from avalanche models of slowly driven nonlinear dissipative systems .

original_text: A landslide caused a passenger train to partially derail in the Swiss Alps Wednesday, injuring at least six people, regional police spokesman Peter Faerber said. The train was headed from St. Mortiz to Chur in eastern Switzerland when at least two carriages derailed near Tiefencastel, said Simon Rageth, spokesman for the Raethische Bahn rail company. A rescue operation was under way Wednesday afternoon, said Faerber, a spokesman for police in the Swiss canton of Graubuenden.

original_text: The Nyabwa (Niaboua, Niédéboua, Nyaboa, Nyabwa-Nyédébwa, Nyedebwa or Nyaboa) language is a Kru language spoken in Ivory Coast. It is part of the Wee dialect continuum. Writing system Nasalisation is indicated by a tilde on the vowel. Tones are indicated by following signs: Very high tone is indicated by a double apostrophe ‹ ˮ › ; High tone is indicated by an apostrophe ‹ ʼ › ; Mid-tone is indicated by no diacritic; Low tone is indicated by a hyphen ‹ ˗ ›. References Wee languages Languages of Ivory Coast
simplified_kindergarten: People in Africa speak Nyabwa. They use marks to write sounds.
simplified_primary_school: Nyabwa is a language spoken in the Ivory Coast. It belongs to a group called the Wee languages. People use special marks when they write it. These marks show if a sound is high or low.
simplified_secondary_school: Nyabwa is a Kru language spoken by people in the Ivory Coast. It is closely related to other languages in the Wee dialect continuum. When writing Nyabwa, a tilde is placed over vowels to show nasal sounds. They also use symbols like apostrophes and hyphens to indicate different tones. These marks help readers know the correct pitch for each word.

original_text: New Tottenham boss Mauricio Pochettino has identified the capture of a new left-back as a summer priority, with Swansea’s Ben Davies the shock name on his hit list. Spurs watched the 21-year-old extensively last season and his name will be recommended to Pochettino. Davies was excellent for Swansea last season and attracted interest from Atletico Madrid. Scroll down for video... Top boyo! Swansea's Ben Davies is on Tottenham's short list to become their next left-back . All of the Premier League’s big guns monitored Davies’s progress last season, but Spurs are ready to make a move for the Wales international once Pochettino signals his approval. Danny Rose is currently the club’s first-choice left-back, but struggled with injury last season. Giving the go-ahead: Spurs are waiting for new manager Mauricio Pochettino to green light the move for Davies .
simplified_kindergarten: A new coach wants a player. He likes a man who runs fast.

simplified_secondary_school: Mauricio Pochettino is looking for a new left-back to join Tottenham this summer. He is interested in Ben Davies, a young player from Swansea who performed well last season. Other major clubs have watched Davies, but Spurs are ready to make a move. The team needs more help because their current player, Danny Rose, was often injured.

original_text: By . Nick Mashiter, Press Association . Aston Villa have confirmed the appointment of Tom Fox as their new chief executive. The former Arsenal chief commercial officer replaces Paul Faulkner, who left last month. Chairman Randy Lerner said: 'Tom's reputation as a leader and team builder makes him, to my mind, a great fit to take our club forward. 'I was delighted and even flattered by Tom's interest in the Villa job. His achievements at Arsenal speak for themselves.' Great start: Aston Villa won their opening game of the Premier League season, beating Stoke 1-0 . Boost: Fox's appointment will aid Villa boss Paul Lambert . Fox joined Arsenal in 2009 and had previously worked for Nike, running the company's Asian sports marketing business, before moving to the sportswear giant's US operation as sports marketing director. Chief financial officer Robin Russell had been Villa's acting CEO since Faulkner's departure and is now expected to return to his previous role. VIDEO Lambert delighted with team effort .
simplified_kindergarten: A new leader joined the team. He helps the players win.
simplified_primary_school: Aston Villa has a new boss named Tom Fox. He worked at Arsenal before this. The team owner is very happy he joined. Tom will help the team do a great job.
simplified_secondary_school: Aston Villa has appointed Tom Fox as their new chief executive to replace Paul Faulkner. Fox previously worked as a leader at Arsenal and held top roles at Nike. Chairman Randy Lerner believes Fox is the perfect person to help the club grow. His arrival is expected to support the team manager, Paul Lambert, after a strong season start.

original_text: (CNN) -- A suicide bomber hit an African Union Mission in Somalia convoy on Monday, killing 16 people, Lower Shabelle Gov. Adukadir Mohamed Sidi said. Sidi said the explosion destroyed two buses traveling behind the military convoy, which was transporting the governor as he traveled back from Mogadishu. The blast killed 16 civilians and injured 22 people, including two soldiers, he said. The Al-Shabaab terrorist group claimed responsibility for the attack and said Americans and South Africans were among those killed. The bombing comes a week after a U.S. airstrike claimed the life of the group's leader, Ahmed Godane. The group appointed a new leader last week and said Godane's death would not stop its attacks. Somalia has been on high alert for strikes since. Al-Shabaab names successor to slain leader; Somalia on alert . CNN's Faith Karimi, Jason Hanna and Michael Pearson contributed to this report.
simplified_kindergarten: Bad men hurt some people. Now many people are sad.
simplified_primary_school: A bomber hit some buses in Somalia on Monday. Sadly, sixteen people died and many others were hurt. A group called Al-Shabaab said they did it. This happened because their leader died last week.
simplified_secondary_school: A suicide bomber attacked a military convoy in Somalia on Monday, resulting in sixteen deaths. The explosion destroyed two buses and injured twenty-two people, including two soldiers. The terrorist group Al-Shabaab claimed responsibility for the strike. This attack occurred shortly after a U.S. airstrike killed the group's former leader, Ahmed Godane. Although Godane is dead, the group has appointed a new leader and promised to continue their violence. Somalia remains on high alert because of these ongoing threats.

original_text: our paper presents new methods for finding and testing of weak periodic variability of stellar objects developed for the purpose of detecting expected regular light variations of magnetic chemically peculiar ( mcp ) candidates in the large magellanic cloud . we introduce two new periodograms of the mcp star , bs cir ( hd 125630 ) , appropriate for rotating spotted variables and compare the results with those obtained by the well - known lomb - scargle periodogram . the usage of periodograms and the testing of the significance of the found period candidates are demonstrated with two examples : the observed and simulated observations of the magnetic field of the mcp star cq uma ( hd 119213 ) and the mcp candidate ogle lmc136.7 16501 . three newly developed tests of the periodic variability the shuffling , bootstrap and subsidiary ones , are presented . we demonstrate that the found periodic variations known with signal - to - noise ratio larger than 6 can be approved as real .
simplified_kindergarten: Stars blink in the sky. We find their light.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists found a new way to study stars. They look for light that changes in a pattern. This helps them find special stars in space. They use new tests to make sure the light is real.
simplified_secondary_school: This paper introduces new methods for finding weak patterns in the light of special stars. Researchers tested these methods using stars in the Large Magellanic Cloud. They compared two new tools with an older, well-known method to see which worked better. To prove their results, they used three different tests called shuffling, bootstrap, and subsidiary tests. These tests help confirm that the patterns they found are actually real. The study shows that clear signals are easy to identify using these new tools. This work helps scientists understand how certain stars rotate in space.

original_text: (CNN) -- President Barack Obama has ordered flags on federal buildings to fly at half-staff until the burial next week of Sen. Robert Byrd, except for Independence Day, when they should be flown at full staff to honor the veteran legislator, the White House said Wednesday. A proclamation issued by Obama made the order "as a mark of respect for the memory and longstanding service of Sen. Robert C. Byrd," who was the longest-serving member of Congress in history. Byrd, who represented West Virginia, is scheduled to be buried on July 6, and Obama's proclamation said the U.S. flag should fly at half-staff until sunset on that day. At the same time, it called for flying the flag at full staff on July 4 "in honor and tribute to this great patriot."
simplified_kindergarten: The flags are low today. We miss a kind leader.
simplified_primary_school: President Obama ordered flags to fly low for Senator Byrd. This shows respect because he worked for the country for a long time. On July 4, the flags will fly high to honor him. He will be buried next week.
simplified_secondary_school: President Obama ordered flags on federal buildings to fly at half-staff to honor Senator Robert Byrd. This gesture shows respect for the longest-serving member of Congress in history. The flags will remain lowered until his burial on July 6. However, they will fly at full staff on Independence Day to celebrate him as a patriot. Byrd represented West Virginia for many years before he passed away.

original_text: Tautology may refer to: Tautology (language), redundant statements in literature and rhetoric Tautology (logic), in formal logic, a statement that is true in every possible interpretation Tautology (rule of inference), a rule of replacement for logical expressions See also Pleonasm Redundancy (disambiguation) Tautological (disambiguation) Tautonym, a scientific name of a species in which both parts of the name have the same spelling hu:Tautológia#Nyelvtudományi és irodalmi tautológia
