simplified_secondary_school: Tautology has several meanings depending on the context of the conversation. In language, it refers to repeating the same idea using different words. In formal logic, it describes a statement that is always true regardless of the situation. Scientists also use the term when a species name uses the same word twice.

original_text: (CNN)Ricardo Medina, the former "Power Ranger" arrested Sunday in the fatal stabbing of his roommate, has been released from custody while law enforcement investigates Joshua Sutter's death. No charges have been filed against Medina, who police said stabbed Sutter in the abdomen with a sword, the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department said this weekend. The Los Angeles County District Attorney's Office has asked law enforcement to continue to investigate, spokesman Ricardo Santiago said. The two got into an argument in their Palmdale, California, home, Saturday that turned into a physical altercation, the department said. Police said Medina told them he stabbed Sutter after Sutter forced his way into Medina's bedroom. Medina called 911 after the altercation and was interviewed by detectives. He was arrested and held on $1 million bail until Tuesday, according to online court records. Local media reported Sutter's death Saturday, and TMZ was the first to identify Medina as the actor who played the Red Ranger in 2002's "Power Rangers Wild Force." He also appeared in "Power Rangers Samurai." The Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department confirmed to CNN that Medina was an actor who portrayed a Power Ranger. CNN's Henry Hanks contributed to this report.
simplified_kindergarten: An actor had a big fight. He is home now.
simplified_primary_school: An actor from Power Rangers was arrested after a fight. He used a sword to hurt his roommate. He is free now because police need to learn more. No one has been charged with a crime yet.
simplified_secondary_school: Ricardo Medina, an actor from the Power Rangers series, was released from jail after being arrested for stabbing his roommate. The police say the two men had a physical fight in their California home. Medina used a sword during the argument but called emergency services himself. Prosecutors have asked for more investigation before deciding to file any official charges. For now, he is no longer in custody while detectives look for more evidence.

original_text: Lagos, Nigeria (CNN) -- The mother of Nigeria's finance minister was kidnapped Sunday, sparking a "massive manhunt" for her captors, officials said. Kamene Okonjo, an academic in Nigeria and the mother of Finance Minister Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala, was abducted from her home in Ogwashi-Uku, Delta State, a Finance Ministry statement said. "At this point, it is difficult to say whether those behind this action are the same people who have made threats against the ... minister in the recent past or other elements with hostile motives," the statement said. Police spokesman Frank Mba told CNN that "the (Inspector General of Police) has ordered a massive manhunt for the perpetrators of the crime." "He directed the police operatives to ensure that no stone is left unturned in efforts at solving the crime, rescuing the victim and reuniting her safely with her family," Mba said. Okonjo-Iweala lost her bid in April to become the next president of the World Bank -- the first ever challenge to the U.S. nominee in the institution's history.
simplified_kindergarten: A mom is missing. Many people are looking for her.
simplified_primary_school: A woman was taken from her home in Nigeria. She is the mother of an important leader. Many police officers are looking for her now. They want to bring her home safely.
simplified_secondary_school: The mother of Nigeria's finance minister was kidnapped from her home on Sunday. Police have started a massive search to find the people responsible. It is unclear if the kidnappers are people who previously threatened the minister. Officers are working hard to rescue the victim and return her to her family. The minister recently tried to become the president of the World Bank.

original_text: The shang () is a Tibetan ritual upturned flat handbell employed by Bonpos and shamans. Description Shang range in size from approximately three to 20 inches in diameter. They are traditionally believed to originate in Zhangzhung and are symbolically similar to the dril bu or ghanta. Shang are traditionally consecrated and made of sophisticated metallic alloy. Use A shang consists of three principal parts: the flat bell part proper; the gankyil, which is the centre piece that holds the knocker; and the knocker or striker proper, which is often made of animal horn. The shang was believed to be useful in receiving information from the Eastern concept of æther, to induce trance or call spirits. The shang is often used in rites in conjunction with the phurba and namkha. References Bells (instrument) Tibetan musical instruments
simplified_kindergarten: This is a special bell. It makes a loud sound.
simplified_primary_school: The shang is a flat bell from Tibet. It is made of metal and has a horn striker. People use it to make music during special ceremonies.
simplified_secondary_school: The shang is a flat metal bell used by Tibetan shamans for ritual purposes. It consists of a bell, a center piece, and a striker often made from animal horn. These instruments vary in size and are used to help people enter a trance or call spirits. They are often used alongside other traditional tools like the phurba during important ceremonies.

original_text: a light front field theory for finite temperature and density is currently being developed . it will be used here to describe the transition region from quark matter to nuclear matter relevant in heavy ion collisions and in the early universe . the energy regime addressed is extremely challenging , both theoretically and experimentally . this is because of the confinement of quarks , the appearance of bound states and correlations , special relativity , and nonlinear phenomena that lead to a change of the vacuum structure of quantum chromodynamics . in the region of the phase transition it eventually leads to a change of the relevant degrees of freedom . we aim at describing this transition from quarks to hadronic degrees of freedom in a unified microscopic approach .
simplified_kindergarten: Tiny bits change into big bits. Scientists study how they grow.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists study tiny particles called quarks. These particles join together to make bigger atoms. This happened when the universe was very young. It is hard to study because it is very fast.
simplified_secondary_school: Scientists are developing a new theory to study how tiny quarks turn into nuclear matter. This process happened during the early universe and occurs during heavy ion collisions. This area of science is difficult because quarks are hard to measure. The study also looks at how the structure of space changes at high temperatures. Researchers want to use one single method to explain how these small particles form the world around us.

original_text: Kīlauea is an unincorporated community and census-designated place (CDP) in Kauai County, Hawaii, United States. As of the 2020 census it had a population of 3,014. Kīlauea shares the name of the active volcano Kīlauea on the island of Hawaii. The name translates to "spewing" or "much spreading" in the Hawaiian language. Geography Kīlauea is on the northeastern shore of Kauai and is bordered to the west by Kalihiwai and to the north by the Pacific Ocean. Hawaii Route 56 passes through the south side of the community, leading west to Hanalei and east to the Moloaa area which includes Moloaa Forest Reserve and overlooks Moloaa Bay. According to the U.S. Census Bureau, the Kilauea CDP has a total area of , of which are land and , or 5.71%, are water. Demographics References Census-designated places in Kauai County, Hawaii
simplified_kindergarten: Kilauea is a small town. It is near the blue sea.
simplified_primary_school: Kilauea is a small town on a beautiful island in Hawaii. About three thousand people live there. The town is next to the big ocean. Its name means to spread out in the local language.
simplified_secondary_school: Kilauea is a community located on the northeastern shore of Kauai in Hawaii. According to the 2020 census, the area has a population of about 3,014 residents. It shares its name with a famous active volcano, and the name means spewing in the Hawaiian language. The town is bordered by the Pacific Ocean and is connected to other areas by Hawaii Route 56.

original_text: the casimir effect in a dispersive and absorbing multilayered system is considered adopting the ( net ) vacuum - field pressure point of view to the casimir force . using the properties of the macroscopic field operators appropriate for absorbing systems and a convenient compact form of the green function for a multilayer , a straightforward and transparent derivation of the casimir force in a lossless layer of an otherwise absorbing multilayer is presented . the resulting expression in terms of the reflection coefficients of the surrounding stacks of layers is of the same form as that obtained by zhou and spruch for a purely dispersive multilayer using the ( surface ) mode summation method [ phys . rev . a * 52 * , 297 ( 1995 ) ] . owing to the recursion relations which the generalized fresnel coefficients satisfy , this result can be applied to more complex systems with planar symmetry . this is illustrated by calculating the casimir force on a dielectric ( metallic ) slab in a planar cavity with realistic mirrors . also , a relationship between the casimir force and energy in two different layers is established .
simplified_kindergarten: Small things can push each other. It is like a tiny hug.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists studied how tiny parts push against each other. They used special math to see how light moves through layers. This helps us understand how small objects stick together. It works even when the materials are not perfect.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers studied the Casimir effect, which is a force that happens between objects in a vacuum. They used a new method to calculate how this force works in systems with many layers. This approach accounts for materials that absorb energy rather than just reflecting it. The results match older theories but work for more complex setups. By using these formulas, scientists can predict the force on metal plates inside a cavity. This discovery helps us understand the relationship between energy and force in small spaces.

original_text: Borger-Odoorn () is a municipality in the northeastern Netherlands in the province of Drenthe. The local Hunebedcentrum Borger features several megaliths (or 'hunebeds') associated with the neolithic and mesolithic Funnelbeaker culture, as well as recreations of historical houses. Population centers Topography Dutch Topographic map of the municipality of Borger-Odoorn, June 2015. Notable people Albert Meems (1888 in Nieuw-Buinen – after 1957) a Dutch spy for Germany in the Second World War Pieter van Boven (1898 – 1952) a Dutch fencer, competed at the 1924 Summer Olympics Egbert Schuurman (born 1937 in Borger) a Dutch engineer, philosopher and politician Henk Nienhuis (1941 in Nieuw-Buinen – 2017) a Dutch footballer and manager. Henk G. Sol (born 1951 in Borger) a Dutch organizational theorist and academic Carsten de Dreu (born 1966 in Borger) Professor of Psychology at Leiden University and Behavioral Economics at the University of Amsterdam Twin towns The municipality has a partner city Gallery References External links Official website Municipalities of Drenthe Municipalities of the Netherlands established in 1998
simplified_kindergarten: This is a town in a green land. It has very big, old stones.
simplified_primary_school: Borger-Odoorn is a place in the Netherlands. It has very old stone graves from long ago. You can see how people lived in the past there. Famous people like sports stars and teachers were born in this town.
simplified_secondary_school: Borger-Odoorn is a municipality located in the province of Drenthe in the northeastern Netherlands. This area is famous for the Hunebedcentrum, which features ancient stone structures called megaliths from the Funnelbeaker culture. Visitors can also explore recreations of historical houses to learn about early human life. Several notable people come from this region, including Olympic fencers, famous professors, and professional football players. The municipality was officially established in 1998 and maintains a partnership with a twin city.

simplified_kindergarten: Stars make bright radio light. We see many big stars.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists studied three groups of very bright stars in space. They found that these stars stayed common for a long time. There is no proof that they started to disappear long ago. This matches what we know about other bright space objects.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers used three data samples to study the most powerful radio sources in space. They tested models to see how the number of these sources changed over time. The data shows that these radio sources did not suddenly disappear at high redshifts. This pattern is similar to what scientists have seen in other powerful quasars. Currently, there is no evidence for a sharp cut-off in their population.

original_text: (CNN) -- Jane Goodall left her home in London for the wilds of Africa when she was just 26-years-old driven by the love of and the fascination for animals. Jane Goodall has spent a lifetime studying primates. Despite no formal training, her enthusiasm to study primates led to the key discovery that chimpanzees have abilities and personalities similar to humans. Five decades on, and Dr. Goodall is now a tireless campaigner for conservation and environmental causes, especially the need to protect natural habitat. With Goodall's global Roots and Shoots movement, her work has moved from the jungle to the classroom and much of that effort has been focused on educating the next generation. Jane Goodall will be on CNN African Voices on Saturday, October 24, 12.30 and 19.30 GMT, and Sunday, October 25, 18.00 GMT .
simplified_kindergarten: Jane loves animals. She helps the chimps.
simplified_primary_school: Jane Goodall moved to Africa to study animals. She learned that chimpanzees are like humans. Now she works to protect our planet. She also teaches children how to help nature.
simplified_secondary_school: At age 26, Jane Goodall traveled to Africa because she loved animals. Although she lacked formal training, she discovered that chimpanzees have personalities similar to humans. Today, she is a famous campaigner for the environment and habitat conservation. Her organization, Roots and Shoots, focuses on educating young people about nature. You can watch her story on CNN African Voices this weekend.

simplified_kindergarten: Tiny shapes build a big world. Some shapes are very strong.

They found that tiny, hidden dimensions change how forces work together. Their math shows why some particles are much heavier than others. This helps explain the patterns we see in the physical world.

simplified_kindergarten: Stars move in a big circle. We found where they spin fast.

By studying oxygen levels in stars, they found the exact radius where the rotation matches the stars' movement. This study suggests that the galactic disc formed very quickly, about 1 billion years ago. These findings help explain how gas and chemicals are distributed throughout the Milky Way.

original_text: At the 1896 Summer Olympics, three fencing events were contested at the Zappeion. They were prepared and organized by the Sub-Committee for Fencing. The épée event for men was cancelled. All fencing was done to three touches. Events were held on 7 April and 9 April 1896. 15 athletes from four nations competed; 8 fencers from 3 nations won one medal each. Medal summary These medals are retroactively assigned by the International Olympic Committee; at the time, winners were given a silver medal. Participating nations A total of 15 fencers from four nations competed at the Athens Games: Medal table Sub-Committee for Fencing Ioannis Phokianos, president Georgios Streit, secretary Ioannis Yenissarlis Loukas Belos Nikolaos Politis Chas. Waldstein Dimitrios Aiginitis Dimitrios Sekkeris Spyridon Koumoundouros Konstantinos Manos Spyridon Antonopoulos See also List of Olympic medalists in fencing (men) List of Olympic medalists in fencing (women) References External links (Digitally available at ) (Excerpt available at ) 1896 Summer Olympics events 1896 1896 in fencing International fencing competitions hosted by Greece
simplified_kindergarten: Men played with swords long ago. They won shiny silver medals.
simplified_primary_school: In 1896, fifteen men had a sword fighting contest in Greece. These athletes came from four different countries to play. Winners received silver medals for their hard work. The games happened over two days in April.
simplified_secondary_school: The 1896 Summer Olympics in Athens featured three fencing events held at the Zappeion. Fifteen athletes from four different nations participated in the competition during April. Although winners originally received silver medals, the International Olympic Committee later updated these to gold. A special sub-committee organized the matches, which were played until a fencer reached three touches. While most events proceeded as planned, officials decided to cancel the men's épée competition.

original_text: Montevideo, Uruguay (CNN) -- A court sentenced former Uruguayan President Juan Maria Bordaberry to 30 years in prison on Wednesday for the coup that consolidated his power in 1973 and for human rights violations. Judge Mariana Mota convicted Bordaberry of violating the constitution, nine counts of "forced disappearance" and two counts of political homicide. Bordaberry, 81, had already been under house arrest since 2007 for the killings of two opposition legislators in 1976. Bordaberry assumed office in 1972 following an election that has since been questioned. In 1973, he backed a military coup that kept him in power, and dissolved congress and suspended the country's constitution. The military pushed him out in favor of another leader in 1976. Journalist Dario Klein contributed to this report for CNN .
simplified_kindergarten: A bad leader went to jail. He did not follow the rules.
simplified_primary_school: A former leader named Juan Maria Bordaberry must go to prison. He took power unfairly and hurt many people. He broke the laws of his country many years ago. Now, a judge says he must stay locked up for a long time.
simplified_secondary_school: A court in Uruguay sentenced former President Juan Maria Bordaberry to 30 years in prison. He was convicted of leading a coup in 1973 and violating human rights. The judge found him guilty of breaking the constitution and being involved in several deaths. Bordaberry first took office in 1972, but he later used the military to stay in power. He stayed under house arrest for years before this final court decision. This ruling holds him accountable for his actions during his time as a leader.

original_text: In Norse mythology, Útgarðar (literally: "Outyards", the plural of Útgarðr. The word can, according to Old Norse orthography be anglicized as Utgard, Utgardar and in other ways.) Útgarðar surrounded a stronghold of the Ettings. They are associated with Útgarða-Loki, a great and devious giant featured in one of the myths concerning Thor and the other Loki who competed in rigged competitions held in the Outyards. These outdoor arenas contrasted with the putrid, indoor cave where Útgarða-Loki is said to have dwelt, when chained, in the Gesta Danorum (12th century). In another version of Norse mythology, Utgard is thought to be the last of the three worlds connected to Yggdrasil being the home of the external cosmic forces. Utgard needs be compared with the Midgard, the world of human affairs, and Asgard, variously attested at the crux of the matter, the centre of the world, as identified with Troy by Snorri Sturluson. References Fictional elements introduced in the 12th century Locations in Norse mythology Fictional fortifications
simplified_kindergarten: A giant lives in a big fort. He plays games with Thor.
simplified_primary_school: Utgard is a big home for giants in old stories. A giant named Utgarda-Loki lived there and played tricks on Thor. It is a cold place far away from people. It is one of the three main worlds in Norse myths.
simplified_secondary_school: In Norse mythology, Utgard is a stronghold belonging to powerful giants. It is ruled by Utgarda-Loki, a clever giant who challenged Thor to unfair competitions. While some stories describe it as an outdoor arena, others portray it as a dark cave. Utgard represents the edge of the universe, far from the worlds of humans and gods. It is often compared to Midgard and Asgard as one of the three major realms.

original_text: Hong Kong (CNN) -- China has removed 162,629 "phantom staff" from government payrolls, as Beijing presses on with a campaign against official corruption and misuse of public money, state media reported. Hebei province in central China was the worst offender, with 55,793 officials found to be getting paid even though they never worked, followed by Sichuan and Henan, state news agency Xinhua reported on Monday. No "phantom employees" were found in Shanghai and Tibet, the report added. President Xi Jinping has made rooting out corruption a top priority since taking power amid widespread suspicion that government officials use their position for personal gain. The People's Daily also said that 114,418 official vehicles had been scrapped out of a total of 119,846. China has promised to phase out the use of government vehicles by the end of 2015, except for those used for emergencies and law enforcement. The news reports did not shed light on how employees could receive a paycheck without showing up for work, but Hong Kong-based commentator Frank Ching said it was not unheard of for senior Chinese officials to employ friends and family members.
simplified_kindergarten: China had many fake workers. They sent the bad workers away.
simplified_primary_school: China removed many people from government jobs. These people got paid but did not work. Many government cars were also taken away. This helps stop people from being unfair with money.
simplified_secondary_school: China has removed over 160,000 'phantom staff' who received government pay without actually working. President Xi Jinping is leading this campaign to stop corruption and the misuse of public funds. Officials also scrapped thousands of government vehicles to save money. Some experts believe that leaders often gave these fake jobs to their friends and family.

original_text: (CNN) -- Suspended New York Yankee Alex Rodriguez has dropped a medical malpractice lawsuit against the Yankees' team doctor and New York Presbyterian Hospital, according to court paperwork filed Thursday. Rodriguez had alleged that the Yankees' Dr. Christopher S. Ahmad had misdiagnosed Rodriguez's hip injury, clearing him for play when a second doctor recommended surgery. CNN National Correspondent Jason Carroll contributed to this report.

simplified_secondary_school: Alex Rodriguez stopped his lawsuit against a hospital and a team doctor on Thursday. He previously claimed the doctor missed a serious hip injury. Alex was told he could play even though he needed surgery. Now, he has decided to end the legal case.

original_text: XLISP is a family of Lisp implementations written by David Betz and first released in 1983. The first version was a Lisp with object-oriented extensions for computers with limited power. The second version (XLISP 2.0) moved toward Common Lisp, but was by no means a complete implementation. After a long period of inactivity, the author released a new version based on XSCHEME, his Scheme implementation. The most current version follows the Scheme R3RS standard. Derivatives AutoLISP, a programming and scripting language for AutoCAD, is based on a very old version of XLISP. XLISP-PLUS is a derivative of XLISP 2.0 that continues to add Common Lisp features. Winterp is a derivative of XLISP-PLUS. XLISP-STAT is an implementation of Lisp-Stat, an environment for dynamic graphics and statistics with objects. Nyquist is an extension of XLISP for sound synthesis. ANIMAL (AN IMage ALgebra) is an image manipulation environment created by Carla Maria Modena and Roberto Brunelli. A 1989 entry to the IOCCC identifies itself as "XLISP 4.0". References External links . . . . . Lisp programming language family Scheme (programming language) Object-oriented programming languages CP/M software
simplified_kindergarten: A man made a computer tool. It helps people talk to machines.
simplified_primary_school: David Betz made a computer language called XLISP in 1983. It helps people write programs on small computers. Many other tools were made from it. Some help make music or pictures.
simplified_secondary_school: David Betz created XLISP in 1983 as a tool for computers with limited power. Early versions added object-oriented features to the Lisp language. Later versions changed to follow the Scheme standard instead. Many other programs, like AutoLISP and Nyquist, were built using XLISP. These tools help people with tasks like statistics, sound, and image editing.

original_text: we present the reduced operator approximation : a simple , physically transparent and computationally efficient method of modelling open quantum systems . it employs the heisenberg picture of the quantum dynamics , which allows us to focus on the system degrees of freedom in a natural and easy way . we describe different variants of the method , low- and high - order ( including the interaction operators ) , defining them for either general quantum harmonic oscillators baths or specialising them for independent baths with lorentzian spectral densities . the method captures the decoherence of the system interacting with the bath , while conserving the total energy . the wide range of the method applicability is presented on the example of systems coupled to different baths with different strengths , and compared with the exact pseudomode and the popular quantum state diffusion approach . our results suggest that quantum coherence effects persist in open quantum systems for much longer times than previously thought .

This approach uses the Heisenberg picture to focus on the system's movement. It works for different types of environments while keeping the total energy stable. original_text: In telecommunication, an artificial transmission line is a two-port electrical network that has the characteristic impedance, transmission time delay, phase shift, or other parameter(s) of a real transmission line. It can be used to simulate a real transmission line in one or more of these respects. Early artificial lines were used in telephony research and took the form of a cascade of lattice phase equalisers to provide the necessary delay. The lattice phase circuit was invented by Otto Zobel in the 1920s. References Telecommunications equipment
simplified_kindergarten: This box sends signals like a wire. It acts like a long cord.
simplified_primary_school: An artificial line is a tool that acts like a real wire. It helps people study how sounds travel through phones. A man named Otto Zobel invented a part for it long ago. Scientists use it to test signals without using long cables.
simplified_secondary_school: An artificial transmission line is a special electrical network used in telecommunications. It is designed to mimic the behavior of a real wire by matching its delay and signals. This allows researchers to simulate real-world conditions in a laboratory setting. Early versions were used for telephone research and used circuits invented by Otto Zobel in the 1920s. These devices are important for testing equipment without needing miles of physical cable.

original_text: Konoe (written: 近衛 or 近衞) is a Japanese surname. It is sometimes spelled "Konoye" based on historical kana usage. simplified_secondary_school: Konoe is a Japanese surname that is sometimes spelled as Konoye. It is the name of a famous family branch related to the Fujiwara clan. Many notable people have held this name, including an emperor and several prime ministers. Other members of the family were successful artists, musicians, and court officials. Today, the name is still recognized because of its long history in Japanese politics and culture.

original_text: harmonic emission from cluster nanoplasmas subject to short intense infrared laser pulses is studied . in a previous publication [ m. kundu _ et al . _ , , 033201 ( 2007 ) ] we reported particle - in - cell simulation results showing resonant enhancements of low - order harmonics when the mie plasma frequency of the ionizing and expanding cluster resonates with the respective harmonic frequency . simultaneously we found that high - order harmonics were barely present in the spectrum , even at high intensities . the current paper is focused on the analytical modeling of the process . we show that dynamical stochasticity owing to nonlinear resonance inhibits the emission of high order harmonics .

original_text: (CNN)Reality star Farrah Abraham has nothing to hide, and that includes a lip augmentation procedure gone horribly wrong. The TV personality and adult film actress recently shared photos on Twitter of the procedure's results, which left her top lip egregiously swollen. "Girlfriends don't say I didn't warn ya!" the former "Teen Mom" captioned one of the images, throwing in the hashtag "#BOTCHED." According to TMZ, Abraham underwent a new kind of procedure in which a doctor uses an implant in the lip instead of fillers. Abraham reportedly believes she had an allergic reaction to the anesthetic used. It seems the 23-year-old has maintained a sense of humor about her medical mishap. In another tweet, she joked that her lip injections make her look like Leela from the animated series "Futurama."
simplified_kindergarten: The lady has a big lip. It looks very funny.
simplified_primary_school: A famous lady got a big lip from a doctor. She was not scared to show it. She shared photos and made a joke about it. She says she looks like a cartoon.
simplified_secondary_school: Farrah Abraham shared photos of her swollen lip after a medical procedure went wrong. She used a new kind of implant instead of normal fillers. She thinks an allergy to the medicine caused the problem. Farrah kept a sense of humor and compared herself to a cartoon character.

original_text: Dhaka, Bangladesh (CNN) -- The owner of a Bangladeshi apparel factory where 112 people died in a fire in November 2012 has been arrested and sent to jail. Delwar Hossain, the owner and managing director of Tazreen Fashions, and his wife, Mahmuda Akther, the chairwoman, surrendered Sunday and were ordered to jail by Metropolitan Magistrate Mohammad Tajul Islam. The magistrate denied bail. Most of those killed at the factory were female workers. More than 200 people were injured, and nearly half of the victims were burned beyond recognition. The government supervised the taking of their DNA before burying them. In December, a Bangladeshi criminal investigation found 13 people guilty of gross negligence of safety measures. They were charged with "culpable homicide," according to a court official. There were arrest warrants issued for six people, including Hossain and Akther. The other warrants were for officials of the company. The factory makes clothes for Western retailers. Ready-made garments make up 80% of Bangladesh's $24 billion in annual exports.
simplified_kindergarten: A bad fire hurt people. The boss went to jail.
simplified_primary_school: A big fire happened in a clothing factory. Many workers were hurt or killed. The owner did not follow safety rules. Now, the police have put him in jail.
simplified_secondary_school: The owner of a Bangladeshi clothing factory was arrested after a fire killed 112 people in 2012. He and his wife surrendered to a court that denied them bail. Investigators found that thirteen people were guilty of extreme negligence regarding safety measures. The factory produced clothes for Western stores, which is a major part of the country's economy.

original_text: the dark energy survey ( des ) is a project with the goal of building , installing and exploiting a new 74 ccd - camera at the blanco telescope , in order to study the nature of cosmic acceleration . it will cover 5000 square degrees of the southern hemisphere sky and will record the positions and shapes of 300 million galaxies up to redshift 1.4 . the survey will be completed using 525 nights during a 5-year period starting in 2012 . about o(1 tb ) of raw data will be produced every night , including science and calibration images . the des data management system has been designed for the processing , calibration and archiving of these data . it is being developed by collaborating des institutions , led by ncsa . in this contribution , we describe the basic functions of the system , what kind of scientific codes are involved and how the data challenge process works , to improve simultaneously the data management system algorithms and the science working group analysis codes .
simplified_kindergarten: People use a giant camera. They look at the stars.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists built a big camera for a telescope. They want to see how the universe grows. They will take pictures of many galaxies for five years. This helps them learn about space.
simplified_secondary_school: The Dark Energy Survey uses a powerful camera to study why the universe is expanding. Over five years, scientists will record the positions of 300 million galaxies. Every night, the telescope produces a massive amount of raw data. A special management system was created to process and store all this information. This project involves many institutions working together to improve their scientific tools. By testing the system, they can better understand the secrets of space.

original_text: ANSI is the American National Standards Institute, a private nonprofit organization that oversees the development of voluntary consensus standards. simplified_kindergarten: ANSI is a group for rules. It can also be a name.

simplified_secondary_school: ANSI is an organization that manages standards for many products and systems. The name also refers to specific computer codes and the BASIC programming language. Additionally, ANSI is the name of an ancient city in China and a village in Estonia. Several people, including famous football players, also share this name.

original_text: Flare is a science fiction novel by American writers Roger Zelazny and Thomas Thurston Thomas, published in 1992. Flare describes the world as it may be in 2081, and the effects a future inter-planetary civilization would suffer if a solar flare occurred after almost 100 years without any solar activity. The book takes a scientific approach to the idea, including almost no tangible story line. It is broken into short segments which describe different people in various places suffering from the effects of the solar flare. References Novels by Roger Zelazny Novels by Thomas T. Thomas 1992 American novels Collaborative novels 1992 science fiction novels American science fiction novels 2081
simplified_kindergarten: The big sun gets very hot. People in space must stay safe.

simplified_secondary_school: Flare is a 1992 science fiction novel written by Roger Zelazny and Thomas Thurston Thomas. The story takes place in 2081 when a huge solar flare hits a future civilization. Instead of one long story, the book uses short parts to show how different people suffer. It focuses on scientific facts rather than a normal plot.

original_text: we explore the connection between black hole spin and agn power by addressing the consequences underlying the assumption in the recent literature that the gap region between accretion disks and black holes is fundamental in producing strong , spin - dependent , horizon - threading magnetic fields . under the additional assumption that jets and outflows in agn are produced by the blandford - znajek and blandford - payne mechanisms , we show that maximum jet / outflow power is achieved for accretion onto black holes having highly retrograde spin parameter , an energetically excited yet unstable gravitomagnetic configuration .

However, this specific setup is very energetic and can be unstable.

original_text: Diez Estudios Escénicos (Ten scenic Etudes) is a musical work for the stage by Juan María Solare. simplified_secondary_school: Ten Scenic Etudes is a twenty-minute musical work by Juan María Solare, composed in 1996. The performance does not use any spoken text. It features three actors who use different objects on stage. A group of six musicians plays the music using instruments like the piano, horn, and clarinet. Solare dedicated this creative piece to Mauricio Kagel.

original_text: By . Associated Press Reporter . Police in Massachusetts say a man from France has been arrested after causing a disturbance on a Miami-to-Paris flight. A . spokesman for the state police says American Airlines Flight 62 was . diverted to Boston's Logan International Airport on Wednesday night . after a passenger became unruly. The passenger . was subdued by a federal air marshal while the plane was in the air and . arrested by state police once the plane landed at about 10 p.m. A spokesman for the state police says American Airlines Flight 62 (not pictured) was diverted to Boston's Logan International Airport on Wednesday night after a passenger became unruly . Police say 50-year-old Edmund Alexandre, of Paris, was charged with interfering with a flight crew. He was brought to Massachusetts General Hospital for observation and treatment of a pre-existing medical condition. Whenever doctors deem him able, he will be arraigned in East Boston District Court. It could not be determined if he has a lawyer.
simplified_kindergarten: A man was loud on a plane. The plane had to stop.

He was taken to a hospital to treat a medical condition before his court date.

original_text: Tiger Woods has announced Chris Como as his latest swing coach, filling a vacancy created by his split from Sean Foley in August. At 36, Como is two years Woods' junior and will initially work with the former world No 1 on a consultant basis. He has previously worked with Australian professional Aaron Baddeley on his swing. Tiger Woods last played at the US PGA Championship and has appointed Chris Como as a new swing coach . Woods wrote on Twitter: 'Happy to have Chris Como consulting and working with me on my swing. I'm excited to be back competing.' Woods, who last played at the US PGA Championship, is expected to return to action at the Hero World Challenge from December 4 which he will host at Isleworth Golf & Country Club in Florida.

Como is 36 years old and has previously coached other professional golfers. Tiger announced the news on Twitter and expressed his excitement about returning to competition soon. original_text: we present long monte carlo simulations of a simple model of biological macroevolution in which births , deaths , and mutational changes in the genome take place at the level of individual organisms . the model displays punctuated equilibria and flicker noise with a @xmath0-like power spectrum , consistent with some current theories of evolutionary dynamics .

original_text: Diego Costa was involved in an incident with Swansea midfielder Gylfi Sigurdsson that left some supporters claiming the Chelsea forward should have been sent off. Here, Sportsmail's Graham Poll gives his verdict... Diego Costa is a superb centre-forward who plays like an old-fashioned English target man. He is no lightweight soft foreigner and was incorrectly cautioned in his first game for diving at Burnley. There was evidence of the more robust nature of his play when he strongly pushed away Swansea’s Gylfi Sigurdsson after he was being held in the area. Perhaps he could have been cautioned had referee Kevin Friend been slavish to the law, but there was nothing I saw to suggest he should have been sent off. The fact he was not even booked perhaps levelled things up after his yellow card at Turf Moor. Diego Costa and Gylfi Sigurdsson grapple as the hosts prepare to take a free-kick . The Brazilian-born Spanish striker appeared to throw the Icelandic midfielder to the ground . Diego Costa scored a hat-trick against Swansea to take his Chelsea tally to seven goals in four games .

original_text: in the quiet sun , magnetic fields are usually observed as small - scale magnetic elements , ` salt and pepper ' , covering the entire solar surface . by using 3d radiative mhd numerical simulations we demonstrate that these fields are a result of local dynamo action in the top layers of the convection zone , where extremely weak ` seed ' magnetic fields can locally grow above the mean equipartition field ( e.g. , from a @xmath0 g ` seed ' field to more than 1000 g magnetic structures ) . we find that the local dynamo action takes place only in a shallow , about 500 km deep , subsurface layer , from which the generated field is transported into deeper layers by convection downdrafts . we demonstrate that the observed dominance of vertical magnetic fields at the photosphere and the horizontal fields above the photosphere can be explained by multi - scale magnetic loops produced by the dynamo .

simplified_primary_school: The sun has tiny magnetic spots that look like salt. These spots grow in a thin layer near the surface. The sun's movement makes these fields stronger and pushes them around. We can see these shapes using special tools.
simplified_secondary_school: Scientists used computer simulations to study small magnetic fields on the sun's surface. These fields look like salt and pepper and are created by a local dynamo process. This process happens in a shallow layer just below the solar surface. Extremely weak magnetic fields grow much stronger because of the sun's moving gas. These fields form loops that explain why we see different magnetic patterns. Convection then carries these magnetic elements deeper into the sun.

original_text: ISLAMABAD, Pakistan (CNN) -- Pakistan's citizens head to the polls on February 18 for parliamentary elections that may determine the future of President Pervez Musharraf and his allies. Pakistan has a federal parliamentary system of government. The nation's parliament consists of the Upper House (Senate) and the Lower House (National Assembly) and the four provincial assemblies. Elections to the national and provincial assemblies take place every five years. Source: Election Commission of Pakistan .
simplified_kindergarten: People will vote. They pick new leaders.
simplified_primary_school: People in Pakistan are voting for new leaders on February 18. This vote will help decide the country's future. They choose a new parliament every five years.
simplified_secondary_school: On February 18, citizens in Pakistan will vote in elections to choose their next government. This vote could decide the future of President Musharraf and his supporters. Pakistan uses a system where people elect members to the National Assembly every five years. Voters also choose leaders for four provincial assemblies during this time.

original_text: The UIC Franc (code: XFU) was a virtual currency unit used by the International Union of Railways (UIC). It was introduced in 1976 after the Gold Franc was abandoned for this purpose due to instability of the international monetary system. It was withdrawn from the ISO-4217 currency list in 2013 and replaced by the euro. See also Private currency Neutral Unit of Construction (NUC) – a related currency for the airline ticketing industry References External links International Union of Railways Private currencies Franc
simplified_kindergarten: Trains used special fake money. Now they use real money.
simplified_primary_school: Train companies used a special kind of money called the UIC Franc. They started using it in 1976. This money was not real cash you could hold. In 2013, they stopped using it and switched to the euro.
simplified_secondary_school: The UIC Franc was a virtual currency created in 1976 for the International Union of Railways. It replaced the Gold Franc because the global money system was becoming unstable. This currency was not physical money but was used for accounting between different railway companies. In 2013, it was removed from the official international currency list and replaced by the euro. Other industries have used similar systems, such as the NUC used for airline tickets.

simplified_kindergarten: Small bits of air move fast. Scientists use light to see them.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists used special lights to look at hydrogen. They measured how the tiny parts move very carefully. Their new tools helped them see things better than before. The tests match what they thought would happen.
simplified_secondary_school: Scientists measured the energy levels of hydrogen molecules with very high precision. They used a special laser method to study molecules that were moving fast. This new technique made the measurements much more accurate than older tests. The results match the world's best math theories about how atoms work. These findings are even more exact than what computers predicted.

original_text: Hardanger may be: Hardanger, a region in Vestland, Norway, consisting of the municipalities of Ullensvang, Eidfjord, Ulvik and Voss Hardangerfjord, a fjord around which the landscape of Hardanger is centered A mountain plateau in Norway, Hardangervidda Hardanger fiddle, a Norwegian stringed instrument Hardanger embroidery, a form of whitework hand embroidery from the district of the same name Hardanger cloth, a special kind of evenweave fabric used for Hardanger embroidery and other counted-thread embroidery
simplified_kindergarten: Hardanger is a place in Norway. It has music and pretty sewing.
simplified_primary_school: Hardanger is a beautiful place in Norway with a large fjord. It is famous for a special fiddle and pretty hand embroidery.
simplified_secondary_school: Hardanger is a region in Norway known for its famous fjord and mountain plateau. It also gives its name to a traditional stringed instrument and a style of white embroidery.

original_text: The Baltimore Colts were a National Football League franchise from 1953 until 1984, when they relocated to Indianapolis. Baltimore Colts may also refer to: Baltimore Colts (1947–1950), a franchise in the All-America Football Conference (1947–1949) and National Football League (1950) that disbanded after the 1950 season Indianapolis Colts, the current incarnation of the former Baltimore Colts (1953–1983) franchise Baltimore Stallions, earlier the Baltimore CFL Colts, a Canadian Football League team based in Baltimore from 1994 to 1995 See also History of the Indianapolis Colts Baltimore Ravens, Baltimore's current NFL team
simplified_kindergarten: A football team played in Baltimore. They moved to a new city.
simplified_primary_school: The Baltimore Colts were a famous football team for many years. They moved to Indianapolis in 1984. Other teams used the same name too. Now, Baltimore has a team called the Ravens.
simplified_secondary_school: The Baltimore Colts were an NFL team that moved to Indianapolis in 1984. Before that, an earlier team with the same name played from 1947 to 1950. Another team called the Baltimore Stallions briefly used the Colts name in the 1990s. Today, the city's professional football team is the Baltimore Ravens.

original_text: inclined air showers those arriving at ground with zenith angle with respect to the vertical @xmath0 are characterised by the dominance of the muonic component at ground which is accompanied by an electromagnetic halo " produced mainly by muon decay and muon interactions . by means of monte carlo simulations we give a full characterisation of the particle densities at ground in ultra - high energy inclined showers as a function of primary energy and mass composition , as well as for different hadronic models assumed in the simulations . we also investigate the effect of intrinsic shower - to - shower fluctuations in the particle densities . cosmic rays , extensive air showers , ground detector , simulation , muon component , electromagnetic component 96.50.s , 96.50.sd , 13.85.tp
simplified_kindergarten: Space rocks hit the air. They make small invisible bits.
simplified_primary_school: Space particles hit our air and make tiny bits called muons. We use computers to see how these bits fall to the ground.
simplified_secondary_school: When high-energy particles from space hit our atmosphere at an angle, they create showers of tiny particles. These showers mostly consist of muons and a small halo of light. Scientists use computer simulations to study how these particles spread out. They also look at how different types of space particles change the results.

original_text: nonspherical perturbation theory has been necessary to understand the meaning of radiation in spacetimes generated through fully nonlinear numerical relativity . recently , perturbation techniques have been found to be successful for the time evolution of initial data found by nonlinear methods . anticipating that such an approach will prove useful in a variety of problems , we give here both the practical steps , and a discussion of the underlying theory , for taking numerically generated data on an initial hypersurface as initial value data and extracting data that can be considered to be nonspherical perturbations .
simplified_kindergarten: Space has many round shapes. We study how they change.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists use math to study how space moves. They look at shapes that are not round. This helps them understand how stars and light behave.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers use special math called perturbation theory to study space. This method helps explain how gravity and radiation work in complex areas. Scientists can now take data from computer models to see how shapes change over time. This new approach is very helpful for solving many different problems in physics.

simplified_kindergarten: Tiny bits pull and push. We count them to learn more.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists study how small pieces of an atom move. They use a computer to find the energy. They made a new rule to explain it better.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers studied how electrons interact by splitting their force into short and long distances. They used a special computer method to find the energy of these particles. After testing different settings, they created a new math formula that fits their results. This formula helps other scientists predict how electrons behave in small spaces.

original_text: Potassium tartrate, dipotassium tartrate or argol has formula K2C4H4O6. It is the potassium salt of tartaric acid. It is often confused with potassium bitartrate, also known as cream of tartar. As a food additive, it shares the E number E336 with potassium bitartrate. Manufacturing Potassium tartrate is produced by the reaction of tartaric acid with potassium sodium tartrate (rochelle salt), and potassium sulfate, followed by filtration, purification, precipitation and drying. Other compounds Tartar emetic is produced when potassium tartrate is heated with antimony trioxide. Tartar emetic causes intense nausea, prostration and vomiting by irritating the gastrointestinal mucosa. References Potassium compounds Tartrates Food acidity regulators Food antioxidants E-number additives
simplified_kindergarten: Grapes make a white powder. It goes in your food.
simplified_primary_school: This white powder comes from grapes. People put it in food to help it stay fresh. It is like cream of tartar.
simplified_secondary_school: Potassium tartrate is a salt made from tartaric acid and is used as a food additive. It is often confused with cream of tartar because they share the same E336 label. To make it, scientists mix tartaric acid with other special salts and then dry the mixture. If you heat it with other chemicals, it can make people feel very sick.

original_text: Mauregatus the Usurper () was the king of Asturias from 783 to 788 or 789. He was an illegitimate son of Alfonso I, supposedly by a Moorish serf. He usurped the throne on the death of Silo, the husband of his half sister Adosinda and earning himself the nickname of the Usurper. The nobility had elected Alfonso II at Adosinda's insistence, but Mauregatus assembled a large army of supporters and forced Alfonso into Álava. Nothing is known in detail of his reign. The adoptionist dispute was raging between Elipandus, Archbishop of Toledo, and Beatus of Liébana and even occasioned the intervention of Charlemagne. Mauregatus also sent back an invading Muslim force. During his reign a hymn to Saint James was composed with an acrostic mentioning the king's name. This is considered to presage the legend of the saint's burial at Santiago de Compostela. References 789 deaths Beni Alfons Year of birth unknown Illegitimate children of Spanish monarchs 8th-century Asturian monarchs
simplified_kindergarten: A man named Mauregatus became king. He was not supposed to be king.
simplified_primary_school: Mauregatus was a king in Spain a long time ago. He took the throne even though another man was chosen. He had a big army to help him stay in power. He also fought off enemies and helped his country.
simplified_secondary_school: Mauregatus was the King of Asturias during the late eighth century. He is known as the Usurper because he took the throne by force from Alfonso II. During his reign, there were many religious arguments and a battle against invading Muslim forces. A special song about Saint James was written while he was king. This song is an early sign of the famous legends about the saint in Spain.

original_text: They say imitation is the sincerest form of flattery. An adorable 16 month-old baby replicates his father's beatbox ability with real passion, vigour and exuberance in this heartwarming video. The footage was filmed in Geneva, Switzerland earlier this month as the father and son enjoyed a special bonding moment. Cute: The adorable baby listens attentively to his dad's beatbox moments before he tries it himself . The father starts his beatbox routine first and then he encourages his 16-month old son to follow suit. The baby exudes confidence and he immediately accepts the challenge by swiftly demonstrating his admirable vocal ability, timing and rhythm. Your turn: The father then encourages his 16 month-old son to try some beatboxing . Baby beatbox: The baby shows lots of confidence and he performs with lots of enthusiasm . Chip off the old block: The baby also imitates his dad with real enthusiasm and surprising accuracy . The smiling baby demonstrates that the ability to beatbox is a family tradition by imitating his dad with real enthusiasm and surprising accuracy. The father looks very proud of his son as he watches the happy infant enjoying the amusing rendition during the short video.
simplified_kindergarten: A baby makes music sounds. He copies his dad.
simplified_primary_school: A baby in Switzerland copies his dad making music sounds. The dad makes a beat and the son tries it too. This baby is very good at keeping the rhythm. They are both very happy to play together.
simplified_secondary_school: A heartwarming video from Switzerland shows a sixteen-month-old baby imitating his father’s beatboxing skills. The father starts a rhythm and encourages his young son to follow his lead. Surprisingly, the baby shows great confidence and timing while copying the vocal sounds. The father looks very proud as they share this special bonding moment. This cute video demonstrates that the baby has a natural talent for music.

original_text: moving target defense ( mtd ) can enhance the resilience of cyber systems against attacks . although there have been many mtd techniques , there is no systematic understanding and _ quantitative _ characterization of the power of mtd . in this paper , we propose to use a cyber epidemic dynamics approach to characterize the power of mtd . we define and investigate two complementary measures that are applicable when the defender aims to deploy mtd to achieve a certain security goal . one measure emphasizes the _ maximum _ portion of time during which the system can afford to stay in an undesired configuration ( or posture ) , without considering the cost of deploying mtd . the other measure emphasizes the _ minimum _ cost of deploying mtd , while accommodating that the system has to stay in an undesired configuration ( or posture ) for a given portion of time . our analytic studies lead to algorithms for optimally deploying mtd .
simplified_kindergarten: Computers need to stay safe. We use tricks to stop bad guys.
simplified_primary_school: Moving target defense helps keep computers safe from bad people. It works by changing things quickly so attackers get confused. We found the best ways to use these tricks. This helps save money and keeps the system strong.
simplified_secondary_school: Moving target defense, or MTD, makes computer systems stronger against cyber attacks by constantly changing their configuration. This study uses a special model to measure how effective these defenses really are. We look at the time a system spends in a weak state and the cost of protection. Our research provides new algorithms to help experts use these defenses in the best possible way.

original_text: A perfect square is an element of algebraic structure that is equal to the square of another element. Square number, a perfect square integer Entertainment Perfect Square, a live recording by the band R.E.M. Perfect Square (publisher), a children's imprint label by Viz Media. See also Perfect square dissection, a dissection of a geometric square into smaller squares, all of different sizes Perfect square trinomials, a method of factoring polynomials
simplified_kindergarten: A square is a shape. It has four sides.
simplified_primary_school: A perfect square is made by multiplying a number by itself. For example, two times two is four. It is also the name of a music video and a book company.
simplified_secondary_school: In math, a perfect square is the result of multiplying an integer by itself. This term is also used for a live album by the band R.E.M. and a children's book publisher. You may also learn about perfect square trinomials when you study algebra in school.

original_text: Bendidee is a national park in the Goondiwindi Region, Queensland, Australia. Geography The park is north east of Goondiwindi on the Darling Downs, 264 km west of Brisbane. The park is part of the Brigalow Belt South bioregion and lies within the water catchment areas of the Macintyre and Weir rivers. The park was established to conserve an area of remaining brigalow–belah plant communities. Bendidee State Forest is adjacent to the national park to its north. The elevation of the terrain is 245 meters. Fauna A total of five rare or threatened species have been identified within the park. 30 amphibians and reptiles as well as more than 89 invertebrates have been found at Bendidee, including the endangered Bull oak jewel butterfly. The most interesting inhabitants of the park are the legless lizard (Paradelma orientalis) and the small Dwyer's Hooded Snake (Parasuta dwyeri). Amenities The park provides for walking, bird watching and nature appreciation activities. See also Protected areas of Queensland References National parks of Queensland Protected areas established in 1979 1979 establishments in Australia Darling Downs
simplified_kindergarten: This park has many pretty trees. You can see colorful birds here.
simplified_primary_school: Bendidee is a big park in Queensland, Australia. It protects special trees and many different animals. You can go there to walk and see beautiful birds. It is home to rare snakes and butterflies.
simplified_secondary_school: Bendidee National Park is located in the Darling Downs region of Queensland, Australia. It was created in 1979 to protect important plant communities like the brigalow and belah trees. The park is home to several rare species, including the endangered Bull oak jewel butterfly and the legless lizard. Visitors enjoy the park for outdoor activities such as walking, bird watching, and appreciating nature. It sits 245 meters above sea level near the Macintyre and Weir rivers. More than 100 different types of animals and insects live within its borders.

original_text: we present a simple method for determining the exact noise power spectra in linear chemical reaction networks . we apply the method to networks which are representative of biochemical processes such as gene expression and signal detection . our results clarify how noise is transmitted by signal detection motifs , and indicate how to coarse - grain networks by the elimination of fast reactions .
simplified_kindergarten: We watch how tiny things move. We learn how they make noise.
simplified_primary_school: We found a new way to measure noise in small body parts. This helps us see how genes work. We can also make the steps look more simple.
simplified_secondary_school: We created a simple way to measure noise in chemical reaction networks. Our method helps us understand how signals move through biological systems like genes. We also show how to simplify networks by removing very fast reactions.

original_text: In open systems, a virtual terminal (VT) is an application service that: Allows host terminals on a multi-user network to interact with other hosts regardless of terminal type and characteristics, Allows remote log-on by local area network managers for the purpose of management, Allows users to access information from another host processor for transaction processing, Serves as a backup facility. PuTTY is an example of a virtual terminal. ITU-T defines a virtual terminal protocol based on the OSI application layer protocols. However, the virtual terminal protocol is not widely used on the Internet. See also Pseudo terminal for the software interface that provides access to virtual terminals Secure Shell Telnet Terminal emulator for an application program that provides access to virtual terminals Virtual console for an analogous concept that provides several local consoles Sources Computer terminals ITU-T recommendations
simplified_kindergarten: Computers talk to each other. They use special tools.
simplified_primary_school: A virtual terminal lets different computers talk together. It helps people work on computers from far away. PuTTY is one example of this tool. Most people do not use this specific type on the internet.
simplified_secondary_school: A virtual terminal is a service that lets different types of computers communicate on a network. It allows managers to log in remotely and helps users share information for tasks. PuTTY is a common example of this technology. While the ITU-T defined a specific protocol for it, it is not common on the modern internet. Other similar tools include Secure Shell and Telnet.

original_text: we apply the generalized second law of thermodynamics to discriminate among quantum corrections ( whether logarithmic or power - law ) to the entropy of the apparent horizon in spatially friedmann - robertson - walker universes . we use the corresponding modified friedmann equations along with either clausius relation or the principle of equipartition of the energy to set limits on the value of a characteristic parameter entering the said corrections .
simplified_kindergarten: Stars and space have rules. We use math to study them.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists study how the universe changes over time. They use special rules about heat and energy. These rules help them check if their ideas are right. They use math to find the best answers.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers use the laws of thermodynamics to study how the universe expands. They look at different types of math corrections for the entropy of space. By using modified equations, they can test if these theories are accurate. This helps them find the correct values for important scientific constants.

original_text: (CNN) -- Russian authorities detained 140 people -- more than 30 of them foreign nationals -- on Friday in a raid targeting suspected members of Islamic extremist groups, state media reported. The operation was conducted at a place of worship in Dar Ul-Arkam in southern Moscow, state-run RIA Novosti reported, citing information from law enforcement agencies. Members of Russia's Federal Security Service, or FSB, carried out the raid along with police. Russia Today, a state-funded television network, said the FSB issued a statement about the raid. This site has been visited in the past by suspected radicals -- some from the North Caucasus region, which includes Chechnya -- the RIA Novosti report said.
simplified_kindergarten: Police caught some bad people. They were in a big house.
simplified_primary_school: Police in Russia caught 140 people on Friday. They went into a church to find them. Many of these people came from other countries. The police think they are part of a dangerous group.
simplified_secondary_school: Russian police and security agents detained 140 people during a raid on a Moscow place of worship. Officials believe some of those caught belong to extremist groups. More than 30 of the people held are from other countries. This location was targeted because suspected radicals had visited it in the past. Some of these individuals came from the North Caucasus region. State media reported that the FSB helped carry out the operation.

original_text: Bloomington may refer to: Places U.S.A. (most commonly) Bloomington, Illinois Bloomington, Indiana Bloomington, Minnesota U.S.A. (less commonly) Bloomington, California Bloomington, Idaho Bloomington, Kansas Bloomington, Maryland Bloomington, Missouri Bloomington, Nebraska Bloomington, New York Bloomington, Ohio Bloomington, South Dakota Bloomington, Texas Bloomington, Utah Bloomington (Louisa, Virginia), a historic house Bloomington, Wisconsin Bloomington (town), Wisconsin Bloomington Township, McLean County, Illinois Bloomington Township, Indiana Bloomington Township, Decatur County, Iowa Bloomington Township, Muscatine County, Iowa Bloomington Township, Kansas Bloomington Township, Minnesota Bloomington Township, Missouri New Bloomington, Ohio Canada Bloomington, Nova Scotia Bloomington, Stormont-Dundas-Glengarry, Ontario Bloomington, York Region, Ontario Other Bloomington (album), a 1993 Branford Marsalis live album Bloomington (film), a 2010 film See also Bloomington Thunder (disambiguation)
simplified_kindergarten: Many places have the name Bloomington. Some are big and some are small.
simplified_primary_school: Bloomington is a name for many different cities in the United States. The biggest ones are in Illinois, Indiana, and Minnesota. Canada also has places with this name. There is even a movie and a music album called Bloomington.
simplified_secondary_school: Bloomington is a common name used for many locations across North America. In the United States, the most famous cities with this name are located in Illinois, Indiana, and Minnesota. There are also smaller towns and townships named Bloomington in several other states and parts of Canada. Beyond geography, the name refers to a historic house in Virginia, a 1993 jazz album, and a 2010 film.

original_text: we introduce geoplotlib , an open - source python toolbox for visualizing geographical data . geoplotlib supports the development of hardware - accelerated interactive visualizations in pure python , and provides implementations of dot maps , kernel density estimation , spatial graphs , voronoi tesselation , shapefiles and many more common spatial visualizations . we describe geoplotlib design , functionalities and use cases .
simplified_kindergarten: This is a computer tool. It helps us make pretty maps.
simplified_primary_school: Geoplotlib is a new tool for making maps on a computer. It helps people see data in many different ways. It is fast and free for everyone to use. You can use it to draw dots or shapes on a map.
simplified_secondary_school: Geoplotlib is an open-source Python library designed for creating geographical data visualizations. It allows users to build fast, interactive maps using only Python code. The toolbox includes several features like dot maps, spatial graphs, and shapefiles. This paper explains how the tool works and describes its various uses.

original_text: Schwarzwald-Baar is a Landkreis (district) in the south of Baden-Württemberg, Germany. Neighboring districts are (from north clockwise) Ortenaukreis, Rottweil, Tuttlingen, Constance, the Swiss canton of Schaffhausen, and the districts Waldshut, Breisgau-Hochschwarzwald and Emmendingen. History The district was created in 1973, when the two districts Donaueschingen and Villingen were merged. Geography The district got its name from the two predominant landscapes in the district. The Black Forest (Schwarzwald), and the Baar, the foothills between the Black Forest and the Swabian Alb. Both the Danube River and the Neckar River have their origins in the Schwarzwald and Baar district. Coat of arms The coat of arms is modeled after the two coat of arms of the cities of Villingen and Schwenningen. Villingen had a horizontally divided blue-and-white arms, while Schwenningen had a vertically divided blue-and-white arms. Combining both divisions gives the four fields. The eagle in the top-left quarter is taken from the coat of arms of the city of Villingen, and derives from the Zähringen family who ruled that city in the 13th century. Cities and towns References External links Official website (German) Freiburg (region) Districts of Baden-Württemberg
simplified_kindergarten: This is a place in Germany. It has trees and two big rivers.
simplified_primary_school: Schwarzwald-Baar is a district in Germany with forests and hills. It was made in 1973 by joining two smaller areas. Two famous rivers called the Danube and the Neckar start here. The blue and white shield shows the history of the local cities.
simplified_secondary_school: Schwarzwald-Baar is a district located in the south of Germany within the state of Baden-Württemberg. It was established in 1973 through the merger of Donaueschingen and Villingen. The region is named after its two main landscapes, the Black Forest and the Baar foothills. Both the Danube and Neckar rivers begin their journey within this district. Its coat of arms features a blue and white design with an eagle to represent its historical roots. The district shares borders with several other German areas and the country of Switzerland.

original_text: Briartite is an opaque iron-grey metallic sulfide mineral, Cu2(Zn,Fe)GeS4 with traces of Ga and Sn, found as inclusions in other germanium-gallium-bearing sulfides. It was discovered at the Prince Léopold Mine, Kipushi, Shaba, Congo (Léopoldville) in 1965 by Francotte and others, and named for Gaston Briart who had studied formations at Kipushi. Briartite is also found in Namibia, Greece, and Spain. See also List of minerals List of minerals named after people References Copper minerals Zinc minerals Iron(II) minerals Germanium minerals Sulfide minerals Tetragonal minerals Minerals in space group 121
simplified_kindergarten: It is a grey rock. People found it in Africa.
simplified_primary_school: Briartite is a grey metal rock found deep in the ground. It was first found in Africa in 1965. It is named after a man named Gaston. You can also find it in Spain and Greece.
simplified_secondary_school: Briartite is a grey metallic mineral made of copper, zinc, iron, and germanium. It was first discovered in 1965 at a mine in the Congo. The mineral was named to honor Gaston Briart, who studied the area. It is also located in other countries like Namibia and Spain.

original_text: we present results from an instrument combining surface acoustic wave ( saw ) propagation and surface plasmon resonance ( spr ) measurements . the objective is to use two independent methods , the former based on adsorbed mass change measurements and the latter on surface dielectric properties variations , to identify physical properties of protein layers , and more specifically their water content . we display mass sensitivity calibration curves using electrodeposition of copper leading to a sensitivity in liquid of 150@xmath0 @xmath1 for the love mode device used here , and the application to monitoring biological processes . the extraction of protein layer thickness and protein to water content ratio is also presented for s - layer proteins under investigation . we obtain respectively 4.7@xmath20.7 nm and 75@xmath215% .
simplified_kindergarten: We use light to see tiny things. We look at how proteins grow.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists used two tools to study tiny proteins. They measured how much the proteins weigh and how they change. They found out how much water is inside the proteins. This helps us see how biology works.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers used a new tool that combines sound waves and light to study proteins. By measuring mass and surface properties, they can find the water content of protein layers. They tested the device with copper to ensure it was sensitive and accurate. The study showed that a specific protein layer was about 4.7 nanometers thick. It also revealed that the layer contained around 75 percent protein.

original_text: nucleation in the two - dimensional @xmath0-state potts model has been studied by means of monte - carlo simulations using the heat - bath dynamics . the initial metastable state has been prepared by magnetic quench of the ordered low - temperature phase . the magnetic field dependence of the nucleation time has been measured as the function of the magnetic field for different @xmath0 and lattice sizes at @xmath1 . a size - dependent crossover from the coalescence to nucleation region is observed at all @xmath0 . the magnetic field dependence of the nucleation time is roughly described by the classical nucleation theory . our data show increase of the anisotropy in the shape of the critical droplets with increase of @xmath0 . * key words : * potts model , nucleation , metastable state , critical droplet , monte - carlo simulations .
simplified_kindergarten: Tiny dots grow into big shapes. Computers help us see them.
simplified_primary_school: Scientists used computers to watch how small shapes grow. They used a special model to see how things change. Small shapes join together to make bigger ones. This happens because of a magnetic field.
simplified_secondary_school: Researchers used computer simulations to study how small droplets form in a special magnetic model. They started with an ordered state and then changed the magnetic field. The time it took for droplets to form depended on the field's strength. The results mostly match a famous rule called classical nucleation theory. They also found that these droplets change their shape as the model gets more complex.

original_text: The year 647 BC was a year of the pre-Julian Roman calendar. In the Roman Empire, it was known as year 107 Ab urbe condita . The denomination 647 BC for this year has been used since the early medieval period, when the Anno Domini calendar era became the prevalent method in Europe for naming years. Events Middle East Elam refuses to extradite an Aramaean prince, giving the king Ashurbanipal of Assyria an excuse to invade the country. He sacks the city of Susa. Ashurbanipal and his Queen in the Garden, from the palace at Nineveh (modern Kuyunjik, Iraq), is made. It is now at the British Museum, London. Births Deaths References
simplified_kindergarten: A mean king took a city. He made a pretty picture.
simplified_primary_school: Long ago, a king from Assyria was very angry. He took over a city called Susa. He also made a famous stone picture with his queen. You can see this art in a museum today.
simplified_secondary_school: In 647 BC, King Ashurbanipal of Assyria invaded the land of Elam. He attacked the city of Susa because they would not hand over a prince. During this time, a famous piece of art was created showing the king and queen. This ancient carving is now kept in the British Museum in London.

original_text: (CNN) -- At least 36 people were killed in a stampede at a stadium in Mali after a speech by a prominent imam, the British Embassy in the country said Tuesday. The stampede at the Modibo Keita stadium took place Monday afternoon as the crowd was leaving the facility, said Mohamed Ba, a consular at the embassy. Another 64 people were wounded, he said. The crowd had come to hear Imam Osman Madani Haidara speak. "The stadium seats 25,000 and it was absolutely full, as it is every time Imam Osman Madani Haidara gives a sermon," Ba said.

original_text: By . Oliver Todd . Follow @@oliver_todd . Controversial striker Nile Ranger has signed for crisis club Blackpool. The former Newcastle United and Swindon centre forward has agreed a one year deal at Bloomfield Road, with the option of a further year. He is available for selection in the seaside club's first home game of the season against Blackburn on Saturday. On his way: Former Newcastle striker Nile Ranger has agreed terms with Blackpool . Ranger, whose career has been blighted by a string of off-field controversies, left Swindon Town in May after his contract was mutually terminated. The 23-year-old former England youth international scored eight goals in 22 games for Swindon. He has also had spells on loan at Barnsley and Sheffield Wednesday while on Newcastle’s books.

Ranger previously played for Newcastle and Swindon, where he scored eight goals. His career has faced some problems, but he now has a fresh start.

original_text: Washington (CNN) -- A cancer with no place in the 21st Century. A poison opposed with "every breath in our body." Talk is tough against ISIS, the Sunni jihadists rampaging through Syria and northern Iraq, following the group's release of a video showing the beheading American journalist James Foley.
